title: Dwellers Of The Forbidden City
description: From the cover: "Somewhere in the heart of the steaming jungle lies the answer to the whispered tales - rumors of a magnificent city and foul, horrid rituals! Here a brave party might find riches and wonders - or death! Is your party brave enough to face the terrors of the unknown and find - the Forbidden City! Parts of this module were used in 1980 for a major East Coast convention tournament. Information is presented here to reconstruct this exciting tournament and even more has been given to expand play. Here characters may adventure several times in a unique and interesting mini-campaign. Included in this module are background and tournament notes, referee's keys, new monsters, pre-generated characters, and eight different maps."

title: Moorguard
description: from the back cover: From out of the past an ancient wizard builds his temple from the bones of the fallen god-king... Moorguard Driven into unknown dangers the players must search for a legendary axe and shield that hold the key to their freeedom. On sacred grounds and against deadly enemies they will have to use thier greatest magic to win both safety and sweet reward. Moorguard is an exciting High Fantasy adventure created to take the work out of fantasy role playing. It contains 24 pages of new creatures, magic items and weapons, complete with a Players Handbook designed on parchment paper. This role playing game will provide hours of enjoyment as the players work together to solve the riddles of this enchanting mystical world.

title: Knights and Berserkers and Legerdemain (2nd Ed.)
description: Adventure! Worlds of Wonder Wealth Power From the back of the box: Why do you play role playing games? Probably the reason I play, to turn fantasies into an acceptable reality, and the key word is REALISM! Compare realisms. Compare KABAL to your present system and see!

title: The Iron Wind
description: From inside the front cover: "This is the first of the Loremaster Series, a group of fantasy aids designed to each provide the background for a complete campaign of any level. Laid out for your players on the four-color map are no less than 5 dungeons and 3 cities through which they may search for riches or death. But worlds are not all towers and dungeons, so the Loremaster series provides the cultural and political background for a series of wilderness adventures as the players journey across the map learning about their world. Two maps (scale 1" = 20 miles) are provided, one labeled with suggested locations for each city or dungeon, so that you can release to the players just the information their characters might actually have. The dungeons and citadels are shown in sinister perspectives and laid out with numbered, keyed, floorplans - ready for you, as Gamemaster, to people them with creatures that will provide an appropriate challenge for your players. A second volume in this series is currently in development to allow you to widen the scope of your player's adventures and more will be forthcoming. Also available (by Spring '81) is a full-color continental map done in the same style as the back cover of this booklet. Ten million square miles of forests, savannas, steppes, deserts, mountains, islands plains and lakes on a 24" x 36" poster-sized map. Look for it at your hobby store."

title: The Temple to Athena
description: from back cover: The fanatical worship of the goddess of all was interfered with by Desar, the ultimate evil ...and as the temple ages and deteriorates the state of things becomes more uncertain. Evil magic and devout clerics exert their efforts to control the party of adventurers! Adaptable to any fantasy role playing system.

title: The Castle Perilous
description: User summary - An RPG system published in the early days of the hobby. Based on the use of three D6 dice for resolution. For those who don't have enough 6 sided dice, a table of random numbers is included. This books contains the complete rules and two sample adventures.

title: Beasts, Men & Gods (1st Edition)
description: The rulebook for the Beasts, Men & Gods RPG.

title: The Maghrib
description: From the front cover: Wyrdworld II. An adventure for 8 to 15 characters of levels 7 through 12. Set in Wyrdworld. With adaptations for AD&D and Variant Systems. Playing time is from 4 to 6 hours. From the introduction: Heliarchus II, Emperor of Ilchania has called the party together to act as an escort and guard for his nephew Imhadrek, who is going to Asfar, the City of the Jinn to collect tribute from Malik Katsham, king of the Jinn. The duties of the party are to guard Imhadrek and the tribute, and to make sure they both return in good condition. User note: The production value for this item is very low: 10 xeroxed sheets of paper stapled together.

title: The Nine Doctrines of Darkness
description: from the back cover: TOME OF EVIL It was the King's favorite wine-flask -- until the ages-old spell wore off. Now the black bound tex of evil doctrine is the center of attention for both good and evil beings. It is your goal, Mortal!

title: Game Masters Shield and Reference Tables
description: From one of the 3rd edition Inserts: Project Director's Screen (GA-1) The Project Director's Screen has the most important tables and charts for The Morrow Project printed on heavy cardboard. Everything the Project Director needs to run The Morrow Project combat system efficiently and effectively is included on the screen. Also included in this package are separate sheets with the statistics of the basic weapons of The Morrow Project as well as statistics for other weapons which the Project Director might need. These sheets are three-whole punched in order to allow the Project Director to keep all of his Morrow Project information in a binder. GA-1 has: 1 - 11" x 24" Screen and 3 - 8 1/2" x 11" inserts printed on heavy cardboard

title: Furioso
description: from the back cover: This scenario is the complete Advanced Dungeons & Dragons tournament adventure used at the Denver Gamers Association convention, Ganghis Con held in May 1980. Centuries ago, in a far away place, an awesome weapon was invented that threatened to destroy the world. A great battle was fought over this weapon and as a result, it was tossed into the deepest part of the ocean. The weapon was thought destroyed and the world was safe once again. Many years passed, and rumors began circulating that religious fanatic named Cimosco was attempting to find the final resting place of this weapon of awesome power. If he managed to find and restore this weapon, its horror would once again be unleashed on the world. Cimsco searched for years and finally found the resting place of the terrible weapon. He enlisted the aid of the sea-dwellers and raised the remains of the artifact. What emerged from beneath the sea was nothing more than a coral-encrusted lump, but repeted blows with mighty sledges released the weapon from its stone like bondage. Four centuries of coral ans salt had not harmed it in any way. Until a way is found to destroy the weapon once and for all, its hellish power threatens all living things.

title: Kahldath, the Messiah
description: From the front cover: Wyrdworld adventure 1. An adventure for 10 to 20 characters of levels 7 through 9. Playing time is from 5 to 7 hours. From the introduction: Zhoral, the High-Priest of Ahriman in the city of Carzal has sent forth notice through all the lands of the Crimson King that he is seeking a party of strong-willed adventurers to take on a quest of an unusual nature, for which they will be amply rewarded. Those who are interested in this proposal should meet in the temple of Ahriman in Carzal, at the 15th hour on the first day of the month of the Striped Lizard. They will be expected to bring all of their equipment with them and to be prepared to embark the next morning. User note: The production value for this item is very low: 9 xeroxed sheets of paper stapled together.

title: The Rescue of Ardene
description: From the introduction: The evil wizard Gregorvich Brownski has captured Ardene, the beautiful elf princess of Silverwood. He holds her on the remote island of Laiah, deep underground. He has a standing challenge to all who dare, to try to rescue her. Official dungeon for American Eagles Tournament held 24 Feb 1980.

title: Merc - D.O.W.
description: Publisher description: "Mercenaries, Dogs of War, a role-playing or miniatures game of late 20th century mercenaries. Rules include complete weapons lists, character generation, scenarios, maps, floor plans, which are excellent with or without a GM." This version of the core rules includes two rulebooks plus a number of stapled-in inserts consisting of maps, charts, and tables. The primary rulebook has a cover printed on green stock. The second rulebook, with a cover made of white stock, is marked inside as "Volume Two" and "Book Two." Comments in the rulebooks indicate that the second rulebook was created to add revised and updated rules that arose from further playtesting.

title: Skinwalkers & Shapeshifters
description: From the title-page: A Guide to LYCANTHROPY for Players, Dungeon Masters, and The Curious

title: Mage
description: MAGE is a a module of the Archaeron Games System designed to be used with most existing role-playing games as well as with the Archaeron games System itself. Other fantasy role playing rules can be used in conjunction with MAGE simply by converting the spell levels of those rules to MAGE Magic Levels or EL. The Endurance (EN) of a persona or character can be expressed in terms of 'hit points', 'fatigue points', etc. simply by dividing the total of 'hit points' by EN to yield the points fer EN level shich are lost. MAGE can also be used by players as a game in its own right as a contest between MAGICIANS. Source: Book introduction

title: Imperial Form 1: Military Player Character Forms
description: A set of 25 character sheets plus directions for use.

title: Roaming Monsters - Level 2
description: High quality metal figure set for use with DUNGEONS & DRAGONS and other fantasy role-playing games. Dungeon Dwellers are 25mm metal figures specially designed for use with Dungeons & Dragons and other fantasy role-playing games. They have been boxed in special assortment sets that give the player exactly the monsters and adventurers he needs for Basic D & D. All guesswork has been eliminated for the beginning player. Each Action Adventure box contains 18 to 22 figures suitable for the dungeon level indicated. All together, the sets provide every figure needed for Basic D & D - in useful quantities for gaming. This set, Roaming Monsters-Level 2, contains the following: - HOBGOBLINS - CARRION WORM - ORCS - GIANT TICKS & GIANT BEETLES - GNOMES - GHOULS/ZOMBIES (Source: From the back of the box)

title: Pieces of Eight
description: From the back cover: PIECES OF EIGHT expands upon the original rules for SKULL AND CROSSBONES and is intended for use with that game. Many of the additional rules sections are not hard and fast rules, but rather are recommendations and discussions of subjects for the Game Master. In many cases it is better for the Game Master to make rulings when many factors can enter into a ruling than to attempt to detail long and complicated rules to cover the almost limitless factors that can enter into a ruling. Some of the new rules sections, such as the rules on voodoo, are optional and are intended to be used by Game Masters with a liking for the fantasy element of fiction. Those familiar with Robert E. Howard's Black Vulmea will immediately understand how and when to apply these rules and recommendations. Finally, PIECES OF EIGHT includes three complete adventures plus outlines for further adventures. Also included with the adventures is a map of Port Royal (not the historical city but the simplified version from the designer's campaign) with a description of the buildings and some of the inhabitants. A similar map of a smaller city, Fort de France, the port city of Martinique is also included. These descriptions give the Game Master some ideas of how to construct and populate some of the other towns which are mentioned on the SKULL AND CROSSBONES map and, hopefully, will generate other adventures using them as a basis. Though we have said it before, it still warrants mention that no rules are cast in bronze and any of them can be changed by the Game Master to vary the adventures, but care should be taken when doing so.

title: The Tombs of Valla
description: From publisher blurb: The third in a series of playing aids based on the world Dyon. It is one of three dungeond used at ProCon I during the summer of 1980. Over a year in the making this dungeon proved a challenge to all who entered.It was used in the 1st-3rd and 4-6th level categories. The other dungeon used at this level was Notnaidag Robber Headquarters also one of my designs.I hope that you who are playing this dungeon will have as much fun as the people at ProCon and the Stygian Wargaming club that play-tested it. Author John C. Clegg Tombs of Valla, 1st Printing 1980

title: Bifrost Volume 4
description: From the introduction: This is Bifrost Volume IV. Within its pages you will find the means to ride into combat fully caparisoned on your black war-horse, or to swoop out of the sun astride a winged steed. Furthermore, there is a menagerie of fantastic creatures against which to match strength and skills. There are also some amendments and additions to the first three volumes.

title: Weres: The Accursed
description: This "mini-system" adds rules for playing shapeshifting were creatures in the Ysgarth Rules System. The rules can be used for player characters or non-player characters.

title: Grint
description: From publisher blurb: "Het vreemde verhaal van het dorp Grint, waar Priesters, Druïden, Magiërs, Tempelridders, Zonneruiters en de Wolvenvrouwe wanhopig proberen het gevaar van de Dragers van de Zwarte Kroon te keren. Grint is een verhaal voor het systeem Queeste, en bevat 84 kaarten." translation: "The strange story of the Grint village, where Priests, Druids, Magiciens, Knights Templar, Sun Riders en the Lady of the Wolf, desperate try to turn the danger of Wearers of the Black Crown. Grint is a story for the Queeste system and contains 84 cards."

title: Tin Star
description: Publisher Description Gunfights and characters in this old West role-playing and miniatures set of rules, with character types, floorplans, two pull-out pages for adentures with pre-rolled characters. BGG Description This is a set of rules for recreating tactical gunfights and shoot-outs in the setting of the American Wild West. The rules cover everything that is needed to play including character design and stats, scenario creation, and rules for campaign games. The gunfights take place as a table top miniatures game using 25mm scale figures. There are floor plans included in the rule book.

title: Dungeoneer's Action Art 8001
description: Figure Set for Adventure Gaming From the back of the box: "Gary Gygax, one of the "Founding Fathers" of Adventure Gaming, is the guiding hand behind the development of Advanced Dungeons & Dragons into the extremely popular family pastime that it is today. Gary has designed the figures in this set to be the ideal small party to explore any Dungeon. Each figure has been meticulously sculpted to ensure complete fidelity to the Advanced Dungeons & Dragons Cosmology" Figures included: 1. Wizard 2. Lantern Bearer 3. Cleric 4. Paladin 5. Gnome 6. Elf Female/Magicuser 7. Thief 8. Female Fighter 9. Crossbow Man 10. Javelin man 11. Ranger 12. Fighter/Spear & Bow

title: The Compleat Fantasist - How To Convert The Major Fantasy Role Playing Systems To Each Other
description: From the back cover: AT LAST!...no longer need characters from one system to be stuck there. And never more will you have to arbitrarily decide how to change personal statistics to fit a different set of rules. Fantasy roleplaying veteran Mark Wagner gives guidelines to help convert many parts of each system the others, and describe aspects that don't concert well.

title: Woodland Adventurers 5003
description: Figure Sets for Adventure Gaming From the back of the box: Grenadier Models Inc. is proud to present the official Advanced Dungeons & Dragons figure line. The figures in this collection were designed with the cooperation of T.S.R. Hobbies Inc., the creators of Advanced Dungeons & Dragons to ensure complete fidelity to the Advanced Dungeons & Dragons cosmology. This fantasy figure set was specifically created for the fantasy role playing gamer

title: Fantasy Set 01 - Player Characters
description: The figures in this set include all the basic character types you'll need for any fantasy game. Use them for your personal figures, or let them represent non-player characters. Each figure has a brief description on its base - but don't let that limit your games! Most of these figures can represent several different kinds of character - see below. Copyright 1980 Steve Jackson Games

title: Shadows
description: From inside the binder on the first page: The Traveller Adventure Titled Shadows was used as the Traveller Tournament situation at WinterWar 1980, held at the University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign, January 18, 19 and 20, 1980 Pre-Publication Draft This is 3-hole punched in a Black "official" Traveller Binder. These were sold at the GDW booth at Origins 1980 is is believed (I'm trying to follow up with Marc Miller on this). I know that at least 10 were sold and perhaps more. This was eventually turned in one of the two adventures in Traveller: Double Adventure #1

title: Demon's Pawn
description: In Demon's Pawn characters of 4th-8th level must face down the demon lord Arekitach in his citadel to save their very souls.

title: Ravensgate (2nd Edition)
description: Ravensgate provides an introduction to the slums and back alleys of Carzal, the capital of Ilchania. For character levels 1-4.

title: Sector 57: Tournament Science Fiction
description: user summary A tournament SF scenario for 2 players based (roughly) on classic Traveller. From p.20 of the referee's guide: Many of the concepts in Sector 57 have been adopted from Traveller game elements. You will note that H'alster and Monorian are glorified characters from the GDW system. NOTE: Traveller is not needed to play this scenario. The module contains the following components: 24 page 8.5x11" referee's guide 11x25.5" (trifold) referee's shield Players situation sheet (8.5x11") 2x player character info sheets (8.5x11") 3 additional 8.5x11" sheets with figures/maps for the scenario

title: Supergame (1st Edition)
description: The first comicbook superhero role playing game to give you: Complete rules and combat systems for any role playing situation Total player control in character construction The only fully quantified, unlimited combination armor system of any fantasy role playing game

title: Adventuring Party 2013
description: Figure Set for Adventure Gaming From the back of the box: "This 25mm fantasy figure set is specifically created for the fantasy role playing gamer. ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS is a trademark owned and licensed by TSR Hobbies, Inc. The policy of GRENADIER MODELS is to constantly upgrade our product & we reserve the right to alter specifications and contents without prior notice." This set includes: A. Faf- Armored Dwarf w/War Axe B. Rayr- Half-Elf Fighter/Magic User C. Norien- Female Magic User D. Snow-Slinging Druid E. Charn-Warrior in Plate F. Morhir-Monk with Spear G. Kereo-Cleric in Plate w/Flail H. Dent- Bard with Bag-pipes I. Mellison-Halfling Fighter/Thief J. Choff-Female Ranger in Splint

title: GamesMaster Catalog:  A Comprehensive Illustrated Guide to Games
description: From the back cover This is the first comprehensive catalog on games. The new fully-illustrated GamesMaster Catalog contains photographs, descriptions and prices of the products manufactured by over 190 games companies. Nearly half of the 1,000 items listed are in full color. The GamesMaster Catalog is the most complete and up-to-date source available - an essential reference book for every game enthusiast! Contents Introduction Fantasy Role-Playing Games, Adventures & Playing Aids Fantasy & Mythology Games Science Fiction & Science Fantasy Games Historical & Contemporary Games Gaming Miniatures & Miniature Rules Accessories for Gaming Computer, Video & Electronic Games Educational & Children's Games Sports Games Leisure Time & Family Games Books & Magazines Play-By-Mail (PBM) Games Future Releases Manufacturers Index Game Category Index Product Index 1981 Game Convention Calendar

title: Dawnworlds, Gateway to the Open Cluster
description: From the back of the book: Dawnworlds, The Gateway to the Open Cluster is a unique and exciting new space sector supplement for any S.F. roleplaying game. Dawnworlds is different from other space supplements in that it is complete with history, detailed planet specifications, ship diagrams and deck plans, encounter matrix, and new fully described technologies. The Open Cluster is fully maped(sp) and DAWNWORLD is written like a S.F. novel with lavish NPC developement(sp). Also, it is written by published S.F. writters(sp) for adult S.F. enthusiasts. The writters(sp) have sfollowed the guidelines that DAWNWORLD must be different and excitingm(sp) not just different; strange and interesting, not just weird; planned, logical, and realistic, not just a hodgepodge of ideas pulled from various S.F. books. In this attempt the writters(sp) have suceeded(sp) admirably and have created a fantastic framework for the referee to spin his space adventure on. The book is stapled sheets inside a 4-fold map that make up the cover. Although it claims to be for any system, much of the terminology within is very traveller specific (jump drives, Xboat routes, etc.)

title: Arduin Character Sheets Combined Pak
description: Includes twenty-four different characters on individual sheets. Intended for use with Arduin but advertised as "compatible with most fantasy role-playing systems". Included various races, classes, and genders. Includes one character sheet (single page, single-sided); remaining pages are full-page black-and-white character art.

title: Melanda: Land of Mystery (1st edition)
description: Presumably similar in systems to Melanda: Land of Mystery (2nd edition). It included character sheets and an adventure titled "In Search of Starfax," which the 2nd edition did not. Edit: actually, the character creation system was different. The second edition streamlined it, but the first edition had some interesting ideas which were sadly cut.

title: 24 Fantasy Adventure Character Sheets
description: From the description page: These pre-drawn characters may be cut out and colored in for use with your FANTASY ADVENTURE CHARACTER SHEETS.

title: A Traveller's Guide to the Marischal Subsector
description: This was a book about an out of the way sector in the Traveller universe

title: Monstrum I
description: Form the Introduction: Welcome to the Varanae series of books. We hope the monsters in this edition will increase the interest and excitement of your game. This publication was designed to be used with any fantasy or science fiction game. The description of each creature will help you understand the personality of each one and ways is can be played. However these are only guides and the personality of each creature can be changed by the Referee. User summary: This book contains images and descriptions of 100 different creatures.

title: Owlhoot
description: Publisher Description Campaign rules for the OLD WEST, complete with pull-outs for a town's floorplans, stat sheets for its inhabitants, have a cattle drive, a range war, a posse after wanted men--or live a life of crime. User Summary A campaign expansion for the Tin Star RPG of Wild West gunfights. The expansion includes a large scale hex map of the surrounding area and smaller scale maps for use in playing out tactical encounters using 25mm figures. Stats for NPC inhabitants of the town of Owlhoot are included.

title: Rahasia
description: Rahasia is the original scenario that formed the basis of the latter publications: RPGA1: Rahasia B7: Rahasia It was produced by the self-publishing concern of the author prior to its purchase by TSR. From the introduction: A series of 'one-night' scenarios designed to be used in conjunction with existing fantasy role-playing systems.

title: Wizards' Realm
description: Publisher Blurb: Quite possibly, Wizards' Realm is your first introduction to the adventure of fantasy role-playing (though you've most likely heard of such gaming before). FRP, as it is abbreviated, lets you capture all the fun and challenge of "make-believe" again: You and your fellow-players are actors in a drama of your own invention, as you create your own characters and send them across imaginary worlds in search of wonder.

title: Personal and Vehicular Basic Loads and The Morrow Project Role Playing Expansion
description: From the back of the book: LOAD UP! With this new basic loads package for the Morrow Project! This package contains both Personal and Vehicular basic loads comprising all of the following: Vehicles: MARS Science SKS V-150 APC V-150 TOW V-150 81mm V-151 20mm Commando Scout XR 311 Commando Ranger Air Scout HAAM Suit All 22 Personal Loads: Rifles, Machine Guns, and Sub Machine Guns, Laser, Dragon Missile, Flame Thrower, Medical and more. Computer compiled Structure Point/Blood Point Tables to aid in character generation. Separate, new format character sheets And The all new Role Playing Expansion Booklet Making possible new skills, college degrees, prior military service, and more! Your Morrow Project Games Can Only Get Better!

title: The Freak Show
description: A 3 mini solo adventures for 1st to 4th level characters. Each adventure shares a common setting but is designed for a different character class (i.e. one scenario designed for a Paladin or Lawful Good Warrior-Cleric, one for an Illusionist or other user of magic, one for a Ranger of other Fighter adept at tracking.

title: Prince of Thieves '81
description: User Summary A collection of the three scenarios that comprised the Prince of Thieves tournament that was conducted by Gamelords Ltd. in major gaming conventions during the summer of 1981.

title: The Fantasy Gamers' Dictionary and Glossary
description: From the introduction: We intend this book for those players just entering RPG and FRP; a gift for your encultruation. and for those long time players who have wished, once a while, everyone would mean the same thing with the same term.

title: The Haunted House
description: Publisher blurb Your dog Homer is your best friend. One afternoon he chases a white cat into a creepy old house. You call Homer’s name, but he doesn’t come back! You think this house looks haunted. You can follow Homer inside the old house, but if you meet a ghost or ghoul, you’ll have to decide what to do. Are you afraid? It’s all right to be a little bit scared, but Homer needs you! This adventure requires you to be brave and step inside the haunted house.

title: The New Ysgarth Rules (3rd Edition)
description: The New Ysgarth Rules, 3rd Edition, was published in 1981 as a single 90-page booklet with a green cover showing a partial map of Ysgarth. This volume includes both game mechanics and some basic world background. It was supported by the publication of a series of adventures set in the world of Jahannam.

title: High Fantasy GM Screen & Character Sheets
description: This is a folio that can be set up like a GM's screen and includes 20 character sheets

title: Stones of the Selt
description: A fantasy whodunit adventure for for character levels 1-5. Billed as "Expae Island Mystery Number One", and lists three other planned scenarios in the series, but none of them were published. ______________ From the 2nd Edition (Revised) September 1981...the additional Epxae Island Mysteries are as follows: Hall of the Eld - Stock #81-005 - Sept. 1981 The River of Death - Stock #81-006 - Oct. 1981 City of Not-Men - Stock #81-007 - TBA The Devil's Sea - Stock #81-008 - TBA Fortress of Evil - Stock #81-009 - TBA

title: Flight of the Stag
description: From the front cover: This folio adventure is designed for characters with Navy, Marine, or Scout Service backgrounds recalled to active duty during the Fifth Frontier War. The characters used in FLEETWATCH are personnel assigned to the Close Escort Stag.

title: The Dungeon Trap Handbook
description: The "Dungeon Trap Handbook" contains 38 tables for the fantasy game referee to use to roll up random traps for unwary adventurers. It is especially useful for designing on-the-spot traps for a party that has had things too easy up to now. Starting with Table 1, the referee will roll percentile dice, and the result will lead him/her to the next table, the result there will lead to the next table, and so forth for a half-dozen rolls until the trap is complete. Subsets of the tables can be used to generate the color, smell, taste, and effect of unknown potions or mystical fountains or sacred wells that adventurers are foolish enough to drink from; or they could be used for the effects of magical spells gone awry. A very versatile little booklet.

title: Monster File Number One
description: User description: 44 new monsters for your FRP game. Each page has 4 creatures with the image on front and stats and description on back. The pages are intended to be cut to make a deck of cards.

title: Uchelglan, the Blood Tribute
description: Blood Tribute is the first adventure in a series set in the world of Jahannam. It was released in support of the 3rd Edition of Ysgarth. This booklet includes 12-page module and 4-page pull-out map section. It was printed at least twice.

title: Imperial Data Recovery System
description: The Imperial Data Recovery System is an Apple ][ computer program published as a play aid to speed up bookkeeping for Traveller, and assist with sector maps, character and ship records, accounting, and encounters.

title: The Castle Perilous (Boxed Set)
description: From the back of the box: "This game provides all you need to play an adventure which ends only when you wish it. Inside you will find: FIVE METAL FIGURINES: SAMURAI DWARF BARBARIAN RANGER & SHIELD MAIDEN COMPLETE RULES TWO COMPLETE ADVENTURES POSTERS PLAYER AIDS THREE SIX SIDED DICE (no other dice are necessary)"

title: Submachine Gun
description: Publisher's description from front cover: "Could YOU have led the disastrous British commando raid on Rommel's HQ to victory instead of defeat? Have you got what it takes to jump out of a plane in a German uniform on a moonless night, knowing if you are captured you will be shot as a Spy? Have you ever wanted to blow up an airfield? Here's your chance!" "Submachine Gun is a role-playing game featuring commando raids of the Second World War. It contains complete, easy to understand rules for arming and leading both the raiders and the defenders, including sections on: American, British and German small arms plus tanks, bunkers, flamethrowers, mines, demolition charges, martial arts, artillery, night raids, use of German uniforms, garrison confusion and much more. The only other things you need are paper, pencil, some dice, imagination and guts. Good luck!"

title: Ironhoof Highlands
description: From the back cover: Ironhoof Highlands An Adventure Campaign Set in a Fantasy World. Add zest to any fantasy-role-playing-game with Group One's new adventure campaign system. The first release, Ironhoof Highlands, sets the stage for a world-wide campaign that offers adventure in the same epic style as the great fantasy novels.

title: Starweb
description: STARWEB a play-by-mail game of star-spanning empires RULES for playing the multi-player strategic space game by FLYING BUFFALO INC. - from the front of the book At the front there is a title page, copyright page and Order of Content page. Has 17 numbered pages plus a Starweb Turnsheet, Sample Starweb Game Printout, Sample Starweb Map, and Index. Inside the front cover there’s also a loose double-sided page titled “News Flash! New Starweb Program Now Running”.

title: Escape from the Minotaur's Lair
description: The player characters are called upon to rescue a hand maiden who has been kidnapped by an evil minotaur. For 4-6 characters of 1-3 level.

title: La Legion Etrangere
description: Publisher's catalog description: Rules for miniatures and role-playing in the heydey of the French colonial empire, including charts, diagrams, and maps for donning your white kepi and fighting with the French Foreign Legion. From the rulebook: "A role-playing game of the French Foreign Legion 1850-1930." "This is a set of miniatures and role-playing rules, and as such has two separate combat systems. Although these systems are separate in the functions they fulfill, they are integrated in the sense that it allows an individual to take part in a larger action." "With a GM, this booklet is intended as a jumping-off place, a beginning spot, and not intended as the all inclusive and definitive set of rules for the French Foreign Legion." "[T]he intent of this set of rules is to create the atmosphere of the days of glory that existed at the end of the 19th century and in the early days of the twentieth century, days when patriotism and colonialism were not necessarily curse words." User summary: The game's character attributes are Strength, Firearms Accuracy, Dexterity/Agility, Hand to Hand Combat, Constitution, and Military Ability.

title: The City of Valla
description: The City of Valla consists of two large maps that are joined together to present a single large city, providing an unusually detailed city environment (for its time). The product is two folded maps, each expanding to 22" x 18" when unfolded. The back of the map pages are blank.

title: The Burgundy Pit
description: From the front cover: A complete fantsy dungeon adventure!

title: Periastron
description: From the cover: This folio adventure is designed for characters of almost any background attached to a Trade Pioneer Team aboard the Type A2 Far Trader Scotian Huntress. The referee may draw characters from any source desired, including existing campaigns. It is urged that characters have some skill in ground combat.

title: Code Name: Nova
description: From the rulebook: "This booklet is a supplement to the Merc-D.O.W. set of rules. It is to incorporate the weapons used by the Russians and Warsaw Pact countries. Also it will have expanded rules for armor and armored combat."

title: The Ring of Gilrod
description: This introductory adventure finds the beginning characters in a difficult predicament from which escape seems impossible.

title: Striker: Rules for 15mm Traveller Miniatures
description: From the back of the box: The Universe of Traveller - Communication is limited to the speed of courier ships. Remote central governments exercise only limited control over the affairs of their frontier territories. Megacorporations struggle for control of sparsely settled mineral-rich worlds. And everywhere, there are mercenaries for hire to settle disputes. Striker fills an important place in the Traveller universe - rules for ground combat with 15mm figures and vehicles. The basic combat system used in Striker is based on the popular Azhanti High Lightning game system. The movement system is simplified for use with larger forces and a ground scale of 1"=25 meters. The basic rules are written with the beginning miniatures player in mind and can easily be used as a combat resolution system for existing Traveller games. All weapons and vehicles covered in Traveller and Mercenary (Book 4) are rated for use in the game. The true value of Striker, however, lies with the advanced rules. A complete system for designing armored vehicles and aircraft at different tech levels is presented, along with air-to-air and air-to-ground combat rules. The package contains everything a Traveller adventurer needs for campaigning with miniatures.

title: The Nine Doctrines of Darkness: The Second Adventure
description: from the back cover: GATES OF DOOM! High on the cliffs of a secluded gorge, deep in snow-capped mountains, twin gate loom. What dangers lay beyond them? Evil beyond imagining, worship of a dark god, knowledge hidden for centuries, a black book...

title: Town of Joundan
description: From the back cover: The town lies as a front for the evil and secret workings of Began and Desan... And thus the adventurers reach the end of their long quest. Before them stands the the wild and evil town of Joundan. A complete adventure or the conclusion of a three-part campaign. An adventure for levels 7 - 10.

title: Aliens for Traveller
description: This was a very early publication given out at conventions detailing aliens in the Traveller universe. Some of the names changed later but listed in the book are: Humans (Including Zhodani, Vilani Solomani and others), Aslan, Centaurs, Vargr, Droyne and "The Hive."

title: Mini Module M1: The Maze of Death
description: This "mini module" is a fan-created, hand-written scenario. It was created in 1981 and published as a PDF in 2012. Several versions have enjoyed wide circulation since abt. 2015 as either collected JPG files or PDFs with various formats. It is intended for a single player character of low level with an estimated play time of 90 minutes.

title: Sewerville Shootout
description: From the front of the book: A Laserburn Scenario

title: Adventure Gaming
description: From the introduction: You are the Adventurer! This special publication includes THREE GAMES designed to introduce you to Adventure Gaming. Plus - game buyer's guide, hints on play, history of gaming Science Fiction: The Creatures That Ate New York Military History: Napoleon at Waterloo 18 June 1815 Fantasy Role-Playing: The Tower of Azann - a Magical Quest User summary: The Creatures That Ate New York is a board game; Napoleon at Waterloo is a hex-and-chit wargame; The Tower of Azann is a peculiar, simple roleplaying game.

title: The Official Superhero Adventure Game
description: A self-published miniatures based RPG of superhero combat from 1981. The game utilized a custom set of superheros rather than licensed characters. Comes with character cards for 50 heroes and villains and notably omits any rules for creating new ones. More a combat game than a role-playing game, though there are some notes on roleplaying and even experience.

title: TSR Hobbies' Games & Accessories - Your Gateway to Adventure
description: TSR Hobbies' Games & Accessories - Your Gateway to Adventure was a secondary TSR catalog for 1981.

title: Mini Module M2: The Priest of Evil
description: This "mini module" is a fan-created, hand-written scenario. It was created in 1981 and published as a PDF in 2012. Several versions have enjoyed wide circulation since abt. 2015 as either collected JPG files or PDFs with various formats. It is intended for a single player character of low level with an estimated play time of two hours.

title: Asphodel Inn
description: Publisher Description A complete fantasy adventure set in the world of Acquitaine . . . with the inn, basement, caves and surrounding terrain.

title: Amazon Mutual Wants You!
description: From the covers: "Four short adventures for use with D&D and AD&D: Temple of the Four Gods, Stronghold of the Mer-Witch, Mission to Danger, and Grimethorp's Manor. Four short modules suitable for low-level thinkers or med-high level hackers. This edition includes: pre-figured experience points, plot summaries, monster/trap summaries, and character levels for different styles of play."

title: The Egg of the Phoenix
description: From the back of the module: In the lonely town of Northending, the Council of Five has called the famous Paladin Athelstan to their aid. The fabulous artifact known as the Egg of the Phoenix has disappeared. It was stolen by evil beings and hidden far away - in the Negative Material Plane itself, guarded but a real Phoenix! This is the third of the RPGA series of AD&D modules. Each RPGA module has been used at an official GEN CON tournament, and has been designed to provide maximum entertainment. THE EGG OF THE PHOENIX is usable in campaign or tournament play (using the unique RPGA Tournament System, included herein), and is designed for a balanced party of 5-8 characters of levels 5-9. The original 6 tournament characters are provided, along with detail and overview maps, scenario and character background information, and detailed encounter descriptions for the Dungeon Master.

title: Jonril:  Gateway to the Sunken Lands
description: Jonril: Gateway to the Sunken Lands is a fully populated, predominantly human city designed to be used with the Midkemian Game System, The Tome of Midkemia. It is also compatible with most of the currently available fantasy role player games, and, with some modification, with some science-fiction role playing games and modern military role playing games. Jonril is a truly cosmopolitan city, drawing people from many parts of Triagia (the largest continent upon Midkemia). From as far distant as the King's city of Rillanon and the heart of the Empire of Kesh come traders and adventurers seeking riches from the Sunken Lands. This accounts for the high number of foreigners residing in Jonril. Jonril is a city with little internal constraints upon business, or tariffs upon trade, though some drugs are contraband. The small import/export duty and the few contraband goods means smuggling in Jonril is done on a small scale.

title: Beastmaker Mountain
description: From the back cover: The massive, black monolith dominates the small valley. At its base is the long abandoned villa of the Wizard Orlow. Its open door beckons adventurers to their fortune...or their fate.

title: The Egyptian Trilogy
description: Adventure 2, Episode 1, 2 & 3 From the back cover: You stand before an ancient stone monument at Giza. Though dwarfed by the great pyramids, it possesses the answer to a mighty mystery. For hidden inside, in some secret, long forgotten, chamber rests the power to destroy the old gods and to allow one man to rule Egypt. In addition to all three Episodes of Adventure Number 2 this module expands the Man, Myth & Magic system by adding ... 31 New Character Classes, including Physicians and Alchemist! Nearly 70 new Magic Spells, Charms, Amulets and Concoctions! New Poisons! Dozens of new weapons and armor classes! Rules for Charioteers!

title: Runequest Character Sheets
description: Taken from the advert in White Dwarf (Issue 37 - Jan 1983) Character & Adventure Sheets for Runequest Games Workshop have revised and amended the character record sheet as published in the Runequest rule book so that players can have a permanent and east-to-read record of their character without damaging their copy of the game. We have also designed a smaller "adventure sheet" which is ideal for use during adventures. The sheet covers spells in mind, equipment, current finances, damage and remaining hits and power points. You get 30 character sheets and 80 adventure sheets for just £1.99.

title: The Wine of the Moon
description: User Summary Supplement for the Ysgarth Rule System that examines several areas of play. It includes new skills, character classes, an experience system, deities, and more. Also includes an adventure.

title: FTL: 2448 The Game of Role-Playing in Space (1st Ed)
description: From the introduction: Faster Than Light 2448 is a role playing game of planetary exploration and adventure in the not so far future. You will become a human or alien in a time of exploration and corporate expansion. Fortunes can be made and lost by the seldom considered individual. You will travel to worlds of wealth or poverty, work hells or worlds built on ideals. Aliens and robots will be your friends as you travel the frontiers of settled space. In FTL you will find no Galactic Empires of Star Kings, Dreadnaughts, Battle Cruisers, or planet destroying Galactic Rangers. You will find real people interacting with alien cultures under the watchful eyes of a police agency dedicated to fairness and peace. Welcome to 2448.

title: Through Dungeons Deep: A Fantasy Gamers' Handbook
description: from the author I spent a good fraction of my college days playing Dungeons & Dragons when I should have been studying. In the process, I discovered a wealth of ideas and techniques in how to play well and be a good Dungeon Master. I wrote these up in Dragon Magazine and others. One day I discovered that my list of topics looked more like a table of contents than a list of future magazine articles, so I began writing Through Dungeons Deep.

title: Places of Mystery I - Chilling Chambers
description: from the back cover Adventure, Anyone? ...Because of the dirt, trash, and wood chips that litter the floor, it is difficult to notice the pentacle lightly chiseled into a single, large flagstone. The dried bodies of various small beasts, including a bat, several fish, two snakes, and a rat, hang by pieces of string attached to the beams. Hanging above the table, is a human arm, the dried bones brown and stiff... ...the rolled-up parchment contains two scribbled and scrawled verses that take some work to decipher. They read, "The green dragon has been slain. The crowned king of beasts devours." and, "The birds s are driven from the vile body of the dragon and allowed to fly to Heaven, only to return again:... ...Bleached bones are found within the "stew" formed by the dissolving bodine... What does our party do?

title: Hela's House of Dark Delights
description: A Tunnels and Trolls Mini-Solo Adventure. From Back of Book: Within Hela's House of Dark Delights you will find any pleasure or pain you desire, you need only ask for it... this was originally printed in Different Worlds (Issue 22 - Jul 1982)

title: Wizards & Warriors
description: This is a hardcover book for High Fantasy that has both the modules: In the Service of Saena Sephar and Murder in Irliss in one volume

title: The Guns of Navarone
description: From the back of the book: The Guns of Navarone is an exciting adventure based on the classic movie of WWII commandoes on an impossible mission. Intended fro use with FASA's WWII role-playing system Behind Enemy Lines, the adventure allows players and a GameMaster to recreate the movie, or explore any number of possible alternatives. Complete character descriptions (including new characters to give a greater number of options fro treachery and intrigue), thorough maps, and new rules, plus complete event tables and situation descriptions hight this Behind Enemy Lines mission.

title: Sword's Path — Glory, Book 1: Combat System
description: From the back cover: The Ultimate Medieval Melee System At last, a realistic hand-to-hand combat system. First in a series of role-playing aids stressing accuracy, the Sword's Path — Glory combat system includes these features: Simultaneous movement and combat, without turns or written orders. The effects of agility, speed, and strength on combat performance. The effects of weapon weights and designs, stabbing, slashing, or impact. The first accurate representation of armor protection, based on actual penetration tests. And, above all, over 67 anatomical hit-location tables which do away with "Hit Points" and other such arbitrary measures of how many times you have to hit the ogre to kill him. Stab him in the heart and he'll die the first time! This system is fast, simple, and yet because of the data provided, a highly realistic simulation of weapons, armor, and physical damage. This realism, combined with the simultaneous movement and combat system, provides a unique and efficient blend of realism and playability. It has been designed for the experienced player, and is recommended for the age group 16 and over. The system provides the conscientious game-master and/or war gamer the opportunity to inject the ultimate realism into any hand-to-hand combat. We invite YOU to walk the Sword's Path and move up to the Leading Edge. From the SPG Overview section: Sword's Path — Glory is a set of rules covering all aspects of role playing in either a historical or fantasy background, with ancient or medieval technology. This is Book I of the series and deals only with hand-to-hand combat.

title: Beasts, Men & Gods (2nd Edition)
description: The 2nd edition rulebook for the Beasts, Men & Gods RPG. After the game's 1st edition of 500 copies sold out, Bill Underwood revised the book and printed a 2nd edition of 1000 copies in 1982. Much later, Underwood revised the rules again and reformatted the text for a print-on-demand revised 2nd edition in 2011. From the back of the rulebook: "What is Beasts, Men & Gods? "It is a dynamic system of combat that combines the facets of realism into a plausible and playable form. "It is a unique magic system that allows diverse magicians and priests without hindering play. "It is a solid form of Fantasy Role-Play that engages players' interests from beginning of play to the end of it. "It's what you've been looking for to spice up your campaign."

title: Endless Plans 8 (Forests)
description: User Summary Endless Plans 8 is a floor plans set for 15mm or 25mm miniatures for any RPG. The plans are of forests and what can be found in them. Contents 4 Basic forest floors 2 Straight bridleway 2 Bridlleway corners 2 Large clearings 1 Clearing extension & small clearings 1 Clearing extension & special features 2 Short bridleway & medium clearing 1 Lengths of river 1 Bridleway junctions & river crossings Endless Plans 8 is compatible with all the other sets in the series.

title: Murder in Irliss
description: From the back of the book: Someone has killed the prince! As the prince's bodyguard, you are told by the King that you must find the murderer--or die yourself. At any time in your search you can be killed--it all depends on your resourcefulness, your skill, and your ability to outwit your pursuers! Murder in Irliss is a solo adventure--the reader is given the plot, the adventure, and a series of scene selections.

title: Miniatures from M. A. R. Barker's World of Tékumel
description: This guide describes the correct painting colors and styles for use on the setting-specific miniatures produced for Tékumel. Individual figurines are referenced by Group and Figure numbers.

title: Baelnok, Holy City of Arberth
description: In Baelnok, Holy City of Arberth, the characters are thrust into a world of politics and intrigue in a major city. For character levels 3-5.

title: In the Service of Saena Sephar
description: From the back of the book: You have arrived on an island of fantasy--inhabited by gruesome lizard-people. Your mission is to stop an assassination, before it is too late! An evil queen has set a secret bomb in the castle of a good queen, and it is up to you to find the bomb and defuse it!

title: Aboard the Death Ship
description: From an ad in the September 1982 issue of Variant magazine: A large merchant vessel has been discovered in orbit around a huge gas giant. Attempts to contact the crew receive only solar static. No exterior damage is apparent. Come aboard and solve the secrets of the derelict if you dare.

title: Lords of Space
description: User Summary Included in the black 3-ring binder that holds the core rules for Lords of Space are all the rules needed for play. In Lords of Space, each player take on the role of a Planetary Leader (or PL) sometimes cooperating and sometimes at odds with the other players. The GM in Lords of Space, called the Overlord, has essentially godlike powers over the game. He or she acts as an intermediary in almost all player interactions; takes the role of the populace of each civilization; creates media stories (some based on player action and some not), manages large scale space battles (with the help of a small amount of charts and tables); determines the success or failure of missions, research projects, attempts at exploration based on written plans submitted by each player; and even plays the role of each player's trusted team of advisers. More opened-ended than most RPGs of the era, about half the binder is dedicated to helping the Overlap with the various fine points of game play. The game comes with a small set of Appendices that assist the Overlord with adding chance elements into the outcome of events. Also included are the various forms that each player needs to track their military, government spending, missions, etc. This core set includes Scenario One: The Galaxy of Cerberus, which itself comes with a small packet of Overlord Secrets to spice up game play. J. A. Gregory seems to be solely responsible for this product and he even goes so far as to dedicate the game, at least in part, to himself.

title: Inner City
description: From inside the book, Inner City is a contemporary role-playing game. That is, the players in the game assume the roles of the characters in Inner City while playing. In order to play this game, one of the players must assume the role of City Master, the referee of the game. The City Master must, with the help of this rule book, pass arbitrary judgement upon the actions of the players in the game and the outcomes of these actions. The other players imagine themselves as, or in other words, assume the roles of, people in a slightly alters modern-day city. Generally speaking, the city of Inner City is what a man from another planet might expect of an average American city after watching several days of prime-time television. Rampant violence, corruption, good cops, bad cops, villainous criminals, and organized crime are the mainstay of Inner City. So!, be your favorite crook, cop, private investigator, or whatever, and above all have fun! Contents: Inner City Rule Book (40 pp.) Game Map Set of Dice Sheets of Play Money

title: The Kingdom of the Sidhe
description: "A Role Playing MODULE for use with Man, Myth & Magic" Adventure 1, Episode 6 User summary and back cover: Sixth part of nine (where the first four parts came with the boxed set). "Your journey has been long and arduous but at last you and the rest of your party camp within the great stone ring of Salisbury plain and spend an anxious night waiting for the golden glow of the midsummer day's dawn. Your thoughts are not upon the mad emperor, Caligula, who sent you on this maniacal quest, nor do they dwell upon the evil clans of Black Druids who stood between you and this night; but rather of the unknown, mystical dangers that lurk within these very stone megaliths [...]"

title: World of Silverdawn
description: World of Silverdawn was a campaign setting on a full continent. It included a full color map (22" x 17"), and descriptions of all major locations and points of interest, as well as residents with whom the characters could interact in various ways. It is now out of print and hard to find.

title: The Mountains of Ikurna
description: The Mountains of Ikurna draws the characters into some major aspects of life in the wilds of Uttgart. For characters of levels 2-5.

title: The Glastonbury Labyrinth
description: "A Role Playing MODULE for use with Man, Myth & Magic" Adventure 1, Episode 8 User summary and back cover: Eighth part of nine (where the first four parts came with the boxed set). "Your journey has been long and arduous but at last you and the rest of your party camp within the great stone ring of Salisbury plain and spend an anxious night waiting for the golden glow of the midsummer day's dawn. Your thoughts are not upon the mad emperor, Caligula, who sent you on this maniacal quest, nor do they dwell upon the evil clans of Black Druids who stood between you and this night; but rather of the unknown, mystical dangers that lurk within these very stone megaliths [...]"

title: Bavenlon: Danger in the Dark Forest
description: From advertising flier: Bavenlon, Danger in the Dark Forest is a description of the forest and political/relidious structure fo the evil "Dark Elf" kingdom. Is contains descriptions of inhabitants, the major temples of evil gods, the major figures in the forest, new monsters, encounter tables, adventure suggestions, and a detailed map of Bavenlon.

title: Peddler's Ferry
description: Peddler's Ferry was a village game setting for heroic fantasy role playing games. This adventure module had a map of the town, and details about residents, vendors, and services. It also described adventures which could be found in and around the town, and quests of longer durations that a role play group could undertake for residents of the town.

title: The Catacombs of He Who Never Sleeps
description: From the introduction: [The] entrance is made of tough granite and was once decorated with elegant carvings and lettering. Now, however, it is in absolute ruin, choked with rubble from the raid. It is evidently impenetrable, and no amount of searching or digging will reveal any way to gain entrance. User summary: This fan-created, hand-written scenario is intended for low-level characters and was written as an expansion to B2: The Keep on the Borderlands. An overland hex map locates several areas from both scenarios. Written abt. 1982, the scenario has enjoyed with circulation since abt. 2015 as both a collection of JPG images and compiled PDFs.

title: The Time Raider
description: From the Back of the Book: You find yourself at the control panel of the TIME RAIDER, an amazing time machine. Push a button and you will hurtle forward into the future. Push a different button and you will sail back into the distant past. Which will you choose? Will you choose to visit the future City of Silver where slaves on a spaceship beg you to free them from their evil master? Turn to page 8. Will you travel back to early pioneer days where you must save the life of Daniel Boone-or change history forever? Turn to page 64. In a TWISTAPLOT book, you decide what path to take-and what will happen once you get there. When you finish one adventure, you are ready to set out on another. It's time to start now. You decide where time and the TIME RAIDER will take you. Happy travels!

title: Famous and Rich
description: Fourth book in the Which Way series. In this issue you have a magic wish to become rich and famous. This leads you into an adventure, detective work or several other endings.

title: Which Way Books Boxed Set
description: A boxed set of the first five Which Way Books, including: Which Way Book 01: The Castle of No Return Which Way Book 02: Vampires, Spies and Alien Beings Which Way Book 03: The Spell of the Black Raven Which Way Book 04: Famous and Rich Which Way Book 05: Lost in a Strange Land

title: The Fair at Tezkorel
description: The Fair at Tezkorel puts the characters into a surprising situation at a pleasant country fair. Levels 3-7.

title: Spindelkonungens pyramid
description: From the back of the book: Ljusskenet från Tomrös fackla kastar fladdrande skuggor längs längs gångens väggar. Ellieneha ser ner på sitt svärd. En ljusblå låga löper utefter eggen. "Var beredda" väser hon. "Onda varelser närmar sig". Ett svagt släpande ljud hörs bortifrån den mörka korridoren. För 400 år sedan dog spindelkonungen Arach under ett magiskt experiment. Hans sörjande livvakt lät uppföra en stor pyramid vid karavanvägen mellan Detwach och det fjärran Faar. Där begravdes han tillsammans med sina skatter. Den allians av nomadstammar som Arach lett, föll snabbt samman under den följande maktkampen mellan hans generaler. Nomaderna återgick till sitt forna levnadssätt i öknen, men skräcken för Arach och legenden om hans återuppståndelse levde sig vidare in i våra dagar. Ända tills nu har fruktan för nomaderna, magiska tabun och värdsliga översåtar avskräckt gravplundrare, men nya tider har kommit. Även till det konservativa Cerand. En gammal man, Assar Besvärjaren, har länge trängtat efter tre magiska statyetter som sägs ingå i spindelkonungens gravskatt. Med hjälp av sitt guld har han nu lyckats övertala en grupp äventyrare att våga sina liv i Arachs pyramid. Du är en i den gruppen. OBS! Detta är inget egentligt spel. Du måste även ha tillgång till DRAKAR&DEMONER för att kunna ha glädje av äventyret.

title: Pitfall I: The Tomb of Trent
description: From the introduction: The Tomb of Trent (Pitfall I) is a dungeon designed for tournament play with eight predesignated characters of levels 1-2. We hope you enjoy this module, which first appeared at Gen Con XVI (1983), as much as we have. It is definitely an adventure that seperates the thinking characters from those of the hack-and-slash type.

title: Journey from Rigour
description: From the introduction: Will the fertile plains teeming with life be turned into an arid desert? Will the hoards from the Red Tower swarm out over the land sowing death and destruction? Will the remote and icy Queen of Pentacles spark green flame at you or will you melt that ice to fire? The answers to these and many more questions are inside Maxi #2 “Journey from Rigor”. Can you Resist?

title: Action Pack
description: Taktische Gefechte in Raumschiffen und Gebäuden Der erste Traveller-ActionPack versetzt jeden Spieler in die Lage, taktische Gefechte an Bord von Raumschiffen oder im Inneren von Gebäuden abzuwickeln. Das erweiterte Kampfsystem aus TRAVELLER II - Söldner, Scouts und Handelsprinzen ermöglicht dies in leicht verständlicher, aber sehr realistischer Form.

title: Dungeon Floor-Plan
description: Author's blurb: Mr. Steven Johnson is pleased to announce that he is now in a position to offer the first in a series of Dungeon Floor-Plans. The first dungeon comprises approximately 300 rooms and one mile of passages on six levels. This dungeon is suitable for use in any fantasy role-playing game, is capable of expansion and come [sic] complete with notes.

title: Monster: Aliens and Fantastic Beings
description: From a publisher product list: "ILLUSTRATED WITH OVER 125 CREATURES for Role Playing Games. COMPLETE ENCOUNTER TABLES, Treasure Types, Random Creatures and Alienoids. Random Exotic Plants." User Summary: A book of monsters for fantasy/post-apocalyptic setting originally described in Exotic Characters and Worlds for Role Playing Games. Unlike its predecessor, this book doesn't seem to use Gamma World rules for its post-apocalyptic monsters, but the table of contents lists radiation matrices and random mutation tables that don't actually appear in the book!

title: Castle of Sigisman
description: From the introduction: For many years the mighty walls of Castle Sigisman have been empty of all but shadows. The halls of the great fortress have echoed only to moans of the past. Will the Blasted Heath ever be recovered? And can the spirits be driven from their ill-gotten home? The answers must be sought within the Castle of Sigisman. Castle of Sigisman is designed for play using the rules of the fantasy role-playing game of your choice. It is ideal for mid-level (level 3-6) fighters, clerics, wizards, rogues or thieves. Any character races may be played. This is a full length multi-player adventure with maps and notes for G.M.'s.

title: The Train of Terror
description: From the Back of the Book: Do you dare to ride... the train of terror? It's no ordinary train. In one car, a beautiful woman is turning passengers into robots! In another, a scarfaced man watches your every move! And you are sure that the person next to you is a jewel thief! How will you get away from these people? If you try to outwit the robot woman, turn to page 54. If you want to find out what the scarfaced man is doing with that knife, turn to page 38. If you decide to forget your trip and join up with the jewel thief, turn to page 42. But be warned! In a Twistaplot book, you decide what path to take and what to do once you get there. The wrong choice could cost you your life. The right one could make you a star! The choice is up to you. So climb aboard and see where you and the Train of Terror are headed!

title: Tower of the Stormlords
description: An adventure set in the world of Jahannam. Characters face off against the King of the Ifrits in his tower eyrie. For characters of 8-15th level.

title: Endless Plans 3 (Labyrinthine)
description: User Summary Endless Plans 6 is a floor plans set for 15mm or 25mm miniatures for any RPG. The plans are for dungeons

title: The OMEGA File: A Merc - D.O.W. Adventure
description: User Summary The OMEGA File consists of a 22-page digest-sized booklet and five pull-out sheets. Four of the pull-outs are hex-grid maps. The fifth pull-out is a "corrigia and addentia" sheet that updates aspects of the adventure and adds to the original Merc - D.O.W. firing rules. The booklet includes stats for 36 NPC's and descriptions for dozens of rooms and locations involved in the adventure. The booklet states the adventure was designed (for?) and first used at Gencon South, Jacksonville Beach, 5-7 February 1982. From the Rulebook OMEGA FILE. What is that? It is: O--Offensive M--Military E--Espionage and G--Guerilla A--Assault It is a governmental agency you will not find, one much more well-hidden than the CIA, or NSA, or ASI (Central Intelligence Agency, National Security Agency or Army Security and Intelligence). It recruits men from all walks of life and with all sorts of skills, but they all possess one common quality: They are all prior service military men from our government or another, and they are all loyal--to their paymaster. The OMEGA File is a document control system that insures the security of all cases and files that are in the "OPEN" or "UNSOLVED" drawers of intelligence agencies. These files are at their final destination: Here they will be solved or simply left to lie forgotten, but for this is the last extreme, the final bureau of a buracracy (sic), the ultimate resting place or solution. Your mission: Return THEODOR STEUBEN to custody of friendly forces to stand trial for offenses in violation of Geneva Convention and acts subversive to the peace and welfare of Africa by direct violation of the status quo. If possible, bring him back alive; if not, sanction him with extreme prejudice as a last resort. At present Theodor Steuben is in the interior of "his" country at a secret training base on the shores of Lake Tekl'lii. The area is approachable by land, air or across the waters of the lake.

title: The Dungeon Hobby Shop
description: From the introduction: The Dungeon Hobby Shop TSR Hobbies, Inc. The Dungeon Hobby Shop Dept. 501 PO Box 756 Lake Geneva, WI 53147 User summary: Included long retail order form (with item name, number, price), advertisements (Best of Dragon Vol II, TSR RPGA, etc.), RPGA Membership Application.

title: Blood Wine
description: From the Introduction: Blood Wine is a complete adventure packet. It combine the elements of an overland, town, and dungeon adventure in a well integrated manner. The adventure can be used as either a self-contained adventure or one that can be used with any fantasy role-playing system. The optimum party for this adventure is six, three to sixth level characters with a good mix of classes.

title: Cassiopeian Empire: Player's Book
description: From Wikipedia: Cassiopean Empire is a role-playing game published by Norton Games in 1982. Cassiopean Empire is a science-fiction space-adventure system, with rules for character creation, spaceships, star systems, aliens, robots, and a description of the interstellar Empire. Cassiopean Empire was designed by Raymond Norton, and published by Norton Games in 1982 as a 16-page book with a character record sheet.[1] The game included art by F.Scott McKown

title: The Northwest Frontier
description: Large-scale Maps for Campaigns in the World of Tékumel From the introduction: This set of six maps was first drawn for the use of the players in Professor M. A. R. Barker's own Empire of the Petal Throne campaign. ... This set of [six] maps covers the area from Pijnar in the northwest to Khirgar in the south. User summary: See also Gazetteer for the Northwest Frontier Map Set

title: Journey to the Cloud Castle
description: from the back cover Kidnapped by a great red dragon, your party of adventurers flies to a castle in the sky! The mission is to rid the castle of its bothersome inhabitants. Fail and face the wrath of the great worm. Succeed and be rewarded with your lives -- maybe. User summary A total of 9 pieces of 8.5 by 11 paper stapled together.

title: Endless Plans 7 (Cavernous)
description: User Summary Endless Plans 7 is a floor plans set for 15mm or 25mm miniatures for any RPG. The plans are of Caves and Caverns

title: High Ranger Guard
description: High Ranger Guard describes the defending force of an elven forest. Players "join" the Guard with their role play characters and use the rules, table, and other role play materials in this adventure module to patrol and defend the forest against numerous threats. The title is long out of print and hard to find.

title: Keltek: Servant of Stawitchi
description: From the back: The seers are claiming that KELTEK'S power is returning to him - that he shall soon rise to enslave the kingdsoms once more. The only hope is to retrieve the three lost treasures... KELTEK: SERVANT OF STAWITCHI is the first in the STAWITCHI series of exciting fantasy role playing adventures. To be used with most major fantasy role playing systems. User summary: This scenario originally was titled "Keltek the Immortal", and was advertised as the first in a series of four adventures.

title: Portals & Pathways, Book #0
description: User Summary An 8-page supplement for the first edition of Fringeworthy. Contents include errata corrections, new weapons, clarifications for the Weapons System, language skills, a non-human player character race (the Slargs), and a one-page scenario titled Emree.

title: Dungeon Mapping Sheets
description: A pad of 50 special gridded sheets to plot your dungeon or city complex, plus instruction for use. Compatible with Dungeon Floor Plans and Dungeon Floor Plans 2. Description taken from advert in White Dwarf (Issue 37 - Jan 1983).

title: Tigris
description: From publisher blurb: "Tigris is het vervolg op de Queeste verhalen 'Tocht naar de onderwereld' en 'Grint'. Het verhaal speelt in de stad Tigris, waar de spelers opnieuw de strijd tegen het kwaad aanbinden met machtige tegenstanders. Ondergebracht in Tigris is het aparte verhaal 'Spokenhuis'." translation: "Tigris is the sequel to the Queeste stories 'Journey to the underworld' and 'Grint'. The adventure takes place in the city of Tigris, where the players continue their battle against the evil and its powerfull allies. Included is the seperate story of the 'Ghost house'."

title: Dungeons & Dragons Quadrille Graph Paper
description: Pad containing 44 sheets of blue-gridded quadrille (4 squares/inch) graph paper. Produced under license from TSR Hobbies Inc. by St. Regis Consumer Products of Birmingham AL. Cover branding matches TSR's Basic/Expert Dungeons & Dragons line, but… it's graph paper, usable with any game.

title: Message from Heartbreak Mountain
description: From the introduction: Will the message from Heartbreak Mountain change your life? Dare you accept the oracle’s enigmatic challenge? Will Quicksilver ever thrill again to the tunes of Bragi’s songmaker, or is his voice forever stilled? Have the Alligatormen of the swamps truly kept their peace treaty with Windemere, or did their love of gems overcome their word of honor? Has Lord Blimp discovered a new means of transportation? There’s only one way to find out – Play Quicksilver’s Maxi-Solo #1!

title: Endless Plans 6 (Tortuous)
description: User Summary Endless Plans 6 is a floor plans set for 15mm or 25mm miniatures for any RPG. The plans are of Caves and Caverns

title: Gorga, The Space Monster
description: From publisher blurb: The reader is given choices to make which determine the outcome of an adventure with a space monster who arrives in Cape Cod.

title: The Dungeon That Shall Not Be Named!
description: Publisher's blurb: Here is the result of my 15-year-old self's first (I'm fairly certain) attempt at dungeon creation: The Dungeon That Shall Not Be Named! From the introduction: This area is dank, musty, and foul. There are bones and skeletons lying around. Also lying around are ragtag scraps of clothing...

title: Creatures Fair and Fell
description: User Summary Supplement for the Ysgarth Rule System that contains new characters and creatures for YRS, an alternate combat system, and an adventure.

title: The Castle of No Return
description: From the back of the book: On night all the televisions in town are disrupted by mysterious electrical waves coming from the woods behind your hose. You go out to investigate when suddenly you come upon a castle with a moat, turrets and high impenetrable walls that hide monsters, a mad scientist, and dark secret dungeons... But what will happen only you can predict - because you must create your own fun-filled - or terror filled adventures!

title: Ysgarth Rule System Record Sheets
description: User Summary Package of blank character record sheets for YRS as well as blank GM adventure record sheets.

title: Society of Sorcery
description: Society of Sorcery described a magic system and a guild of mages for use as a component in a heroic fantasy role playing platform. Included were various branches of the Society, their goals, rules of the Society, and other information upon which to build character interactions and adventures. It is now out of print and hard to find.

title: Fantasy Furnishings
description: Companion Pieces, Vol I - Fantasy Furnishings from back cover: "a series of game aids designed for use with any fantasy role play system" "Accessories for customizing and enhancing your fantasy maps"

title: Sunken Treasure
description: From publisher blurb: The reader is asked to make choices which will determine the outcome of a search for sunken treasure.

title: Exotic Characters and Worlds for Role Playing Games
description: From the book's introduction: "This Supplement is Offered as a Multiversal System for Role Playing Games using Polyhedral Dice and Miniature Figurines. Provided herein is Complete Information and Data; Essential for the Game Master wanting to bring Utmost Realism into their Campaign." User Summary: An unlicensed AD&D supplement that incorporates parts of the Gamma World and Traveller rules to support its briefly-described fantasy/SF setting. Includes new character races, classes, spells, alien races, and space ship construction rules.

title: The Ring, the Sword, and the Unicorn
description: From the Face-page: You'll show them! your brother and sister don't believe that the forest behind your house is enchanted and filled with all kinds of magical beasts. You know better. What's more, you're going to prove it! From the back of the book: Will you be attacked by fierce monsters? Will you be trapped by an evil wizard's magic spell? Or can you outsmart them all? Turn the page and find out! Only you can decide where your adventure will lead. From TSR, Inc., the producers of the Dungeons and Dragons game.

title: Thoth's Dagger
description: From the first page of the scenario: “This is a multi-session, fully-detachable, 12-page scenario for Call of Cthulhu.” Published in issue 27 (March 1983) of Different Worlds Magazine

title: Places of Mystery III - Sylvan Settings
description: from the back cover Adventure here The Viking Burial Longship ...Eerie, bobbing lights give dim illumination through the fog. All is suddenly still as a crow's warning cry, loud enough to awake the dead, echoes in the darkness. As though in response, aphosphorescent globe moves from behind a tree and casts upon a wall of fog a terrifying shadow - the head of a dragon, raised high, jaws agape, its gaze fixed on the path... The Sacrificial Oaks ...The seven stumps, each as large as the thirteen trees that ring them, have been stripped of bark to reveal the twisted grain and intricate root structure. The tops are a uniform, dark color. They are very smooth, as though red brown oil. ...or some other substance... had anointed them to protect against the assault of the elements... The Carrion Crow ...The trees, covered with deep green fungus, drip wet moss in a 15-foot-long strands. The ground flattens as it gives way to water: a yellow-brown blanket covers the marsh, No sound shatters the solemn... oppressive... silence... ...What does your party do? What does our party do?

title: The Pride of Ogyane
description: In The Pride of Ogyane the characters move into higher social circles, meeting some of the legends of Uttgart. For characters of levels 4-8.

title: Tower of Magicks
description: Sequel to Beastmaker Mountain From the back cover: Years before, a demon lead horde assaulted the lone Tower. Now you must enter the dread Tower where magic has gone wild to discover the secrets of a desperate Wizard. Can you meet the challenge of a dungeon where not only fantastic creatures and cunning goblins, but the very dungeon itself, may be your opponent? An adventure for those who can meet and conquer not only the unknown but also the unexpected. The second Adventure in the Flight of Orlow the Beastmaker is for four to eight players of third to sixth levels of experience.

title: The Werewolf of Europe
description: Adventure 3 From the back cover: Northern Europe, 1 A.D. The pale, full moon begins to rise, heralding another night of terror in the small, isolated village of Tharn Grivet. You, along with your hastily assembled comrades, check your seemingly useless weapons before posting guards and going to bed. Laying on your raised pallet, your mind is filled with the horror that grips this place. Somewhere, nearby a monster, virtually immune to weapons and magic alike, is waiting to kill again. Each morning brings the discovery of a new, mangled body, today the victim had been one of your comrades who had been guarding in the predawn hours. Suddenly the night is shattered by a shrill howling. Grimly determined and sickeningly afraid, you leap from your bed, grab your pitiful weapons, and move out into the erie night. As your small group assembles their faces are filled with resolve. There have been too many deaths already, there can be no more. Tonight, for better or for worse, you will find .... the WEREWOLF of Europe

title: Keep of the Ancient King
description: From the Face-page: You are Amy. Your horse, Acorn, has been stolen by the evil Shadow Rider. When the Good Knight of the Golden Dragon and his warriors pass your father's inn on their way to the KEEP OF THE ANCIENT KING, you volunteer to lead them. Maybe you'll find your horse there. From the back of the book: Will you be attacked by fierce monsters? Will you be trapped by an evil wizard's magic spell? Or can you outsmart them all? Turn the page and find out! Only you can decide where your adventure will lead. From TSR, Inc., the producers of the Dungeons and Dragons game.

title: Plague of Terror
description: from Module Scenario Description The setting of play in this module is Wentworth, a small manor village of historic and strategic importance. Wentworth occupies the flat land to the west of a narrow pass, Shaltor Notch, in one of the branches of the Snowpeak Mountains. Through this notch runs the Kingsway, a paved road many centuries old, patrolled and maintained by the king. Spanning the notch is a fortification that has been turned into an inn. He who controls the Kingsway controls the flow of goods and troops across the province.
