He who controls Shaltor Notch controls the Kingsway, and he who controls Wentworth Manor controls Shaltor Notch. The basic plot deals with the takeover of Wentworth Manor, with the intent to capture Shaltor Notch, disrupt trade, and break the King's hold on this part of the province. When the module begins the baron's wife has died mysteriously, the baron is paralyzed with grief, the baron's son is a puppet of the man taking over, who is scheduled to marry the baron's daughter.

title: Lair of the Freebooters
description: A campaign setting for the world of Haven. It provides details on the Southron Realms / Biracean Sea area of Haven - 3,000 miles south and east of the Free City of Haven. The northern part of this area is the continent of Erinoir and the Eastern Realms of the once-great Namori Empire. The rest of the area is comprised of independent island nations populated mainly with pirates. Several towns are detailed, along with adventure possibilities.

title: The Serpent Islands
description: From the back cover: Your party is among a group of adventurers who have been hired to serve as elite mercenaries in the dwarven wars against the servants of Kaishnalai, at an extravagant salary commensurate with your level of experience. To reach the battle zone, a two week sea voyage is necessary. After one week at sea, the ship is caught in a terrible storm which lasts three days. Late one night, when the storm finally relents, the ship finds itself in unfamiliar waters with the rudder and mainmast destroyed. After a few hours adrift, during which the crew tries various schemes to build a makeshift rudder and find out where you are, calm seas suddenly explode under the fury of a huge serpent; a toothed snake with six tentacles like those of a squid. It attacks with complete surprise and totally destroys the ship before any effective defense can be given. In the heat of the attack, the captain's voice is heard: "Abandon ship!" and "Every man for himself!" Can you face the horrors that thrive on the Serpent Islands? Will destiny lead you to riches and a safe return or leave your bones silently smoldering beneath the mud? Your fate awaits you...

title: Ruins of Rangar
description: From the Face-page: You are Erik. On a quest with your brother, Sir Gregor, Good Knight of the Golden Dragon, you travel to the RUINS OF RANGAR, seeking to destroy the evil skeleton king, Nightshade. From the back of the book: Will you be attacked by fierce monsters? Will you be trapped by an evil wizard's magic spell? Or can you outsmart them all? Turn the page and find out! Only you can decide where your adventure will lead. From TSR, Inc., the producers of the Dungeons and Dragons game.

title: Jungle of Lost Souls
description: From the back cover: Almost a century ago. Durenian colonists landed on the norther coast. There, they raised bustling cities under the cooling breezes from the sea, but, except for soldiers, traders, and ambitious adventurers, no one ventures far into the fever-ridden, mosquito-cursed interior. Even these are usually content to stay close to the outposts, taking an occasional profit when some of the friendlier Taweii come in to sell their pelts, their medicinal herbs, and some exotic, jungle products. You turn and stroll back to the fort. The musical call of the auctioneer catches your attention. He is selling a catch of slaves that a party of adventurers just brought back from the wilderness. Yet, they came back disappointed; like you, they had heard the rumors of great wealth waiting to be found in the jungle, ruined cities rich in grave goods, undiscovered civilizations, fountains of youth, and the bountiful graveyards of the giant, ivory bearing beasts. You have already decided to try your luck in the forbidding jungle. To begin your adventure, go to... Designed for characters of a maximum of 40 combat adds and 4th level spells.

title: Talisman of Valdegarde
description: From the back of the book: You are Gwynne, and you are training to be initiated into the ancient arts of a Druid priestess. But you must test your skills sooner than you expected when the monstrous forces of the evil Lytir threaten your peaceful kingdom. He is Neil, a handsome, enigmatic bard whose lilting melodies on the lute have the power to enchant. So, too, do his emerald eyes, as you learn as you begin to question whose cause he actually serves. Will your fantasy adventure lead you to your first romance, or will the decisions you make carry you into realms in which there is no future? In a HEARTQUEST book, you, the reader, are challenged to PICK A PATH TO ROMANCE AND ADVENTURE.

title: Worlds of Adventure
description: Worlds of Adventure was an early supplement for To Challenge Tomorrow and presented generic "science fiction" setting material for the game master. Although it was published during the 1st Edition period of the game it remained a useful resource throughout the next several editions.

title: Blasted Land
description: A tournament AD&D dungeon from Dallcon '83. The party must find and "neutralize" a powerful artifact in a limited amount of time. The module contains all of the original tournament rules, characters, and point scoring system. Sold as a limited, numbered edition of 1000 copies.

title: City of the Sacred Flame
description: "A complete campaign module set in the world of Haven" From the back cover: Primitive Passions ... City of the Sacred Flame is a complete campaign module, detailing the inhabitants, shops, and other places of interest in Zal' Akhen. This module can be used independently or in conjunction with other Gamelords campaign packs — particularly Within the Tyrant's Demesne, which describes the fortress city of dhar Ankhes on the southern end of the High Valley, where Zat'ak met his fiery end. This module utilizes the Thieves' Guild rules system, but is compatible with any major fantasy role-playing system. City of the Sacred Flame contains a detailed city map, dozens of shop and NPC descriptions, city encounter tables, and two complete introductory scenarios — enough material for months of adventuring. If you like rough-and-tumble excitement with an exotic flavor, then the city of Zal' Akhen is the place for you!

title: Kobold Hall
description: Tournament AD&D adventure run at Origins '83 in Detroit. The module title is play on words for the convention venue (Cobo Hall). In part one of the adventure the players must find and gather all 14 magical fire opals to power a pyrohydra statue to defend their homes from the onslaught of the evil Statue of Sliff sent by a neighboring island. In part two the party must place the 14 fire opals in new remote hiding spots. Sold as a limited edition of 750 numbered copies.

title: To Challenge Tomorrow (1st Edition)
description: To Challenge Tomorrow: Role Playing Past, Present, and Future Science-Fiction and Historical Rules User summary: To Challenge Tomorrow 1st Edition presented a surprisingly complete and modern role-playing game with a focus on detail and realism. The system inherited many aspects of the Ysgarth fantasy system, including a classless Skill system.

title: Doc's Island
description: From the front cover: After centuries of strife between the forces of Good and Evil, your party, under the leadership of the famed Paladin Athelstan, seeks to recover the Holy Sword, Chrysomer – one of the great Legendary Swords now in the hands of Evil forces. This is the last in the "R" series of AD&D® modules. Each RPGA module has been used at an official Gen Con tournament, and has been designed to provide maximum entertainment. Doc's Island is usable in campaign or tournament play. The original five characters are included in the module, along with character details, historical background, overview maps, and detailed encounter descriptions for the Dungeon Master.

title: Brotherhood of the Bolt
description: form scenario description In this series of adventures, the setting of play is the wilderness near the village of Wentworth, Shaltor Notch, and the mountains to the east that border the Kingsway. Shaltor Notch, of historic and strategic importance, is a narrow pass in the Snowpeak Mountains through which runs the Kingsway, a paved road many centuries old, patrolled and maintained by the King. Spanning the notch is a fortification that has been turned into an inn, and at the foot of the notch on the west side is the ancient Barrow of Shaltor Plain. On Thunder Peak stands the castle of Morlach the Malicious, a member of the Brotherhood of the Bolt, an ancient secret society of necromancers. Morlach is attempting to gain power, either by becoming an undead lord (the ultimate aim of all of the members of the Brotherhood) or by becoming powerful in local mundane matters. He is convinced the way to these goals is by breaking into the Barrow.

title: Within the Tyrant's Demesne
description: "A complete campaign module set in the world of Haven" From the back cover: Whose Side Are You On?!! Within the Tyrant's Demesne is a complete campaign module, detailing the inhabitants, shops, and other places of interest in dhar Ankhes. It can be used independently or in conjunction with other Gamelords campaign packs — particularly City of the Sacred Flame, which describes the stronghold of dhar Ankhes traditional nemesis, the barbarian tribesmen of Zal' Akhen. This module utilizes the Thieves' Guild rules system, but is compatible with any major fantasy role-playing system. Within the Tyrant's Demesne contains a detailed city map, two complete introductory scenarios, several additional mini-scenarios and city encounter tables — enough material for months of adventuring. So if it's cloak-and-dagger suspense you're after, then seek no further, effendi — dhar Ankhes is the place your campaign has been looking for!

title: The Elixir of Life
description: From the cover - The Kingdom of Pellham is in danger. Only the return of the great King Llywelyn can solve the realm's problems and satisfy the people. Many arduous tasks must be performed before the great king can be resurrected-tasks YOU have been chosen to undertake. You must solve arcane mysteries and overcome dire perils to accomplish your quest. Are you equal to the challenges you face? The Elixir of Life is the second in a series of RPGA modules concerning the Prophecy of Brie. The module contains two AD&D adventures for 6-10 characters, levels, 4-7. The module was originally run as part of a tournament at the GEN CON XVI Convention. The module contains two wilderness adventures, background information, maps and detailed encounter keys.

title: Shadowcastle
description: From the Face-page: You are Molly. You and Thistle, the little halfling, follow the Good Knight of the Golden Dragon and his soldiers to SHADOWCASTLE in search of your friend and Thistle's brother. From the back of the book: Will you be attacked by fierce monsters? Will you be trapped by an evil wizard's magic spell? Or can you outsmart them all? Turn the page and find out! Only you can decide where your adventure will lead. From TSR, Inc., the producers of the Dungeons and Dragons game.

title: The Book of Swords and other Magical Weapons
description: From the back cover: Inside this book you will find a wide range of interesting weapons to enhance and Fantasy Role-Playing game. Each weapon is beautifully illustrated and described in detail. The Book of Swords will add a note of realism to your game, while helping your players to visualize your world. Adding your own imagination to this valuable playing aid will make your world truly unique.

title: Margin of Error
description: From the introduction: Stardate 7931.43. Three hours out from the Epsilon One station on the Klingon frontier. We are hoping to aid that station in examining a strange phenomenon that occurred across the Klingon border three months ago. I have called my department heads to the briefing room to discuss the situation there. User summary: Manuscript copies of the scenario are available for public reading at Tracy and Laura Hickman papers on Star Trek Margin of Error role-playing game; MSS 8180; Tracy and Laura Hickman papers; L. Tom Perry Special Collections; Literary Manuscripts; 1130 Harold B. Lee Library; Brigham Young University; Provo, Utah 84602. The folder contains multiple copies of the manuscript in various stages of development; several are heavily annotated. The version recorded here, at 18-pages, is probably the final development stage in the folder.

title: Indian Trail
description: Publisher blurb You live with your tribe in a pueblo village. There has been no rain for a long time and the crops you grow are dying. The only hope for your village is to ask for help from the spirits your people call kachinas. You are told they are very powerful and helpful spirits. They can even change the weather! You have danced and prayed to the kachinas, but now the time has come to go find them. The trail up the mountain will be long and dangerous. Will you bring your friends? Or do you decide to face the wilderness alone?

title: The Formula for Trouble
description: From the back of the book: You're curious. You're thirsty. You can't resist. One gulp, and anything can happen! Turn to page 14, and learn what life is like at the other end of a leash when your dog starts giving you the orders! Help! On page 26, you stare at the mirror and discover that a hairy werewolf is staring back at you! Go ahead! Take a sip of the Formula for Trouble. In a TWISTAPLOT Book, you're the star of the story! You decide which path to take and what will happen once you get there. The choice is yours. Bottoms up!

title: Wizard's Island
description: Wizard's Island ... In a time near the end of the Dark Years, when the forces of evil were fighting to desperately to hold back the changing tide brought on by the Legions of the Four Sages, there was a wizard, Master of Mind Magics, who dabbled with the forces of the Thousand Hells until he had relearned the forbidden Black Art, Necromancy. His powers were such that he could create undead. But he was not satisfied with just blaspheming the dead, he wanted also to blaspheme the living. So he chose instead to conduct other more heinous experiments. He sent his undead ships to raid the coasts of Avland and Tryuna for humans, humanoids, and other creatures to use as guinea pigs. The result of his experiments was the Blasphemer. No two Blasphemers were alike, as each combined the features of two or more living things. Satisfied with his experiments, the evil mage was ready to conquer the world. Only the world had changed in the years he had been engrossed in his experiments: The Dark Year were ended and the golden age of Silverdawn had begun The Golden Empire had rapidly risen to a level of power and wealth unheard of since times before the Dark Years. Still, given time, the necromancer realized that he could build great armies of undead and Blasphemers, and once more assert the rightful dominance of evil. But he had no time. The Sages had heard of his evil exploits and so sent one of their number to end his reign of terror. The Sage of Wizardry had spent the past several years in actual battle so that his combat spells were at their strongest. Kazanlan, the necromancer wizard, was no match for the battle-hardened Sage. Thus it was that the evil mage Kazanjian screamed with his final dying breath this curse: "I swear by the name of my birthing, Kazanjian, that I will return. My blasphemous hordes will reassert evil's dominion and will send the Sages to some dark hell!" Kazanjian; the evil mage, is known in most historical accounts simply as The Wizard. Necromancy was often called Black Wizardry in those times ... Now a group of adventurers have decided to explore what is left of his domain, which they assume to be abandoned ...

title: Dungeon of the Dragon Lord
description: From the introduction: The conjurer Drakon and his evil apprentice have been wreaking havoc on the countryside. Many parties have entered the dungeon never to return. Your job is to stop this evil magic-user while he is just beginning to grow, for he is, at the moment, only controlling a small number of young dragons. User summary: For 1st - 4th level characters.

title: Invasion of the Black Slime and Other Tales of Horror
description: From the back cover: You are crossing America on your bicycle. . . When you stop to explore and old mining town. Soon you discover that Silverlode has been invaded by a terrible, bubbling black slime that makes animals howl and people hiss and attack each other in the night. You have no choice but to stay and help solve the mystery! But whether you find yourself trapped in a river of oozing black evil in your own hotel room, running for your life from a hideous manlike monster, walking a narrow path over a pit of rattlesnakes, imprisoned as a sacrifice for a mad doctor's experiments, battling billions of attacking insects, or defying the eerie powers of the yellow-eyed people- never fear! If you make the right choice, you might also win a million dollars! So get ready to plunge into adventure. And if you get caught in the black slime, just go back to the beginning and start again. THIS ADVENTURE BELONGS TO YOU! Welcome to the world of Which Way books- Where YOU make things happen.

title: The Video Avenger
description: From the back of the book Your dream has come true. The fantastic ME-II computer you won is sitting in your bedroom, just waiting for you to set dozens of video games in motion. You can hardly wait to play... until you find out that the games are real and that, with on touch, you are trapped inside the computer.

title: Overlord's Handbook
description: User Summary The Overlord's Handbook includes general advice for the Overlord in running a campaign and takes the time the emphasize the role-playing possibilities open to the Overlord. The Handbook also introduces a variant that would allow players to get together on a regular basis in a way much more reminiscent of classic RPG play than is suggested in the core rules. Several sections expand on the core rules adding wrinkles to: Politics (population moral, alternate taxation, & new ways to handle media); Spying (several new charts are included); War and Battle (war aids for the Overlord, new ship types, cost to build custom ships, etc.); and Trade (rules for computing how many important trade goods are available at any given moment).

title: Desert Plots: Amazon Mutual #2
description: From the covers: "A desert mystery campaign plot with two complete dungeons, 5 plot outlines, 13 new monsters, 18 new magic items, and 18 new traps & tricks. AD&D modules & generic plots linking The Efreet Secret Weapon, Adventurer's Life Assurance, The Great Art of Master Ovid & His Good Demons, The Orcan Liberation Front, The Sexual Mores of the Efreeti Queen, and Belworth George - The Paladin of St. Fallwell."

title: The Bigfoot Mystery
description: From publisher blurb: While you're camping in the woods your campsite is attacked by something that leaves behind huge footprints.

title: Isle of Illusion
description: From the back of the book: You are Licia, secretly trained as a fighter, and your skills are about to be severely tested as you follow the footsteps of your missing brother in his quest to destroy the evil illusionist, Treg, who holds your family's future in his hands. He is Tym, a young adventurer with a frank, open face and the skills of a thief. And then there is Max, a scholarly magic-user, with the eyes of a mystic and the ability to make you feel emotions you've never felt before. Will your fantasy adventure lead you to your first romance, or will the decisions you make carry you into realms from which there is no return? In a HEARTQUEST book, you, the reader, are challenged to PICK A PATH TO ROMANCE AND ADVENTURE.

title: Crash Landing!
description: From the back of the book: Choose from over 30 different endings! CRASH LANDING! You gasp in terror as you hear the pilot announce those terrifying words! You can't believe that your free vacation is about to end in flames! Will you become a newspaper statistic? Or will you use your wits to face the danger...and survive! On page 6, you learn how to guide a parachute over perilous mountains and deserts! Deadly piranha fish threaten your surival on page 41! And if you ever get home, what will you do with all the gold you discover on page 52? In a TWISTAPLOT Book, you are the star of the story! You face the danger! Your survival depends on the choices you make and the pages you turn. So fasten your seatbelt. Your flight is now departing.

title: Cynfelyn, Prince of Prydein
description: In Cynfelyn, Prince of Prydein characters move up in the world of international intrigue and espionage between neighboring countries. For levels 4-6.

title: Threat From Nowhere - The Gift
description: From the introduction: Six days ago you were in the town of Akhmen, resting after successfully freeing Nefru, an orphan ward of the temple of Anubis, from a deadly magical bond. Some of you had just completed training when the high priest of Anubis in Akhmen told you to seek a rapid return to your home city of Thinnes, though he later had no memory of this event.

title: The Imliv River Valley
description: From the front cover: Adventure in the Magical Grimbolwood! Caught between two fighting goblin tribes, the gnomes of the Hilberry Hills are unable to deliver their goods. A party of adventurers is needed to brave the horrors of Brimbolwood and save the town of Drimiliv. The Imliv River Valley is an exciting role-playing adventure designed for use with almost any fantasy role-playing system.

title: The Deeper Dream
description: Players in The Deeper Dream generate eight attributes (Will, Imagination, Innate Power, Solidity, Strength, Toughness, Balance, and Aim) using 3d6 for each each. Players then distribute 100 percentage points among one to four skills (taking fewer than four skills allows for faster future progression). Most skills are Dreamworld skills, but some are used in the Waking World. To determine success in opposed actions, percentage dice are rolled and compared to the appropriate skill statistic. If no skill applies, a d20 roll may instead be called for to be made against a suitable attribute. In both cases, the die roll must be lower than the skill or attribute to achieve success. One unique aspect of the game concerns death, which is rarely permanent in dreams. Instead, characters may lose the ability to dream for several days or suffer other negative effects.

title: Jungle Safari
description: From publisher blurb: You go on safari with your uncle Stanley and his daughter, your favorite cousin June. During your journey you hope to find a Kawamba, a rare species of ape not seen in many years.

title: Raid Against the Empire
description: Publisher's blurb: Melee & Magic game scenario for 6 to 10 people including [Game Controller] and 2 to 3 actors. It requires 4 game sets made from 3 houses/apts. but little work to prepare the sets for play. It involves no monsters and minimal props. It will take 1 to 2 days to play.

title: Search for the Treasures of Term
description: From the introduction: We've tried to present scenarios that can be applied with a minimum of fuss to many game systems. Here are some guidelines to smooth over any rough areas you may encounter... ... Rumors abound in the town of Windemere; they fly to and fro as if riding the backs of the swift Belldoves that nest on Heartbreak Mountain. It's difficult at times to tell the truth from falsehood, but when pilgrims from afar start trudging up the circuitous paths of Rising Moon Hill to pay homage to a new find unearthed by a group of clerics, you know there's more to the hearsay than imagination.

title: Dream Trips
description: From publisher blurb: You go to sleep and have dreams.

title: The Accursed Castle
description: Publisher's blurb: This location was something I drew up shortly after seeing The Last Unicorn for the first time. I recall really liking the idea of the old king haunted by his past, and drew a lot of inspiration from the concept of a seaside castle fallen under a Wuthering Heights-esque pall of doom and gloom. So, moved by the Muse, I immediately set to work with my mechanical pencil, compass, and graph paper. The result is The Accursed Castle, an immense structure whose foundations are carved deep into the rocky cliffs of The Cold Sea, and whose handful of spires reach high into the gray sky above. It's an "empty" location - that is, aside from room labels, the castle maps are not keyed. Anybody wishing to use this has a blank slate on which to work.

title: Have Your Own Extra-Terrestrial Adventure
description: From the introductory text: That day I was wearing a canary yellow, one-piece suit, trimmed in terra cotta and sky blue at the collar, shoulders and wrists. My belt and boots, made of the multi-coloured hide of a Frygian sand-dragon, were almost conservative. As for the rings on my fingers, the jewelled pendant around my neck and the jewelled headband that held back my hair, they were modest compared to the decorations of most city folk.

title: Melee & Magic
description: Publisher's blurb: Rules for live action adventure gaming. Consists of: a system of mock hand to hand combat with weapons construction and medical treatment of wounds a system of scoring games based on the combat a guide for designing and refereeing game scenarios a guide for incorporating props of various types including treasure and magical artifacts

title: Trapped in the Black Box
description: You volunteer to test the time-travel devices in an amazing new museum and are given the opportunity to explore the World of Fantasy Creatures, the Earth of the Future, or the World of Dreams.

title: Rogue 417
description: From the back cover 1994 As the flask of Rogue 417 smashed to the lab floor, the technician quickly flooded the area with an antiseptic and called for help. But this was the middle east, a savage war zone, where special equipment, trained personnel and ultra-modern containment technology are often lacking when desperation forces half-trained bio-chemists to create new weapons. The technician dies within hours and Rogue 417 was loose in the world to sythe a path of destruction and madness unparalled in history. Rogue 417 is a game system suppliment for Fringeworthy or Stalking the Night Fantastic that can become a game in itself with use of Tri Tac role playing systems. With Fringeworthy, Rogue 417 becomes a campaign of inter- dimensional explorers and survivors pitted against a madman. Rogue 417 is a game of human survival.

title: Kata Kumbas
description: Kata Kumbas An original game set in a "Mediterranean Fantasy": not the classic heroic fantasy with Nordic influences but an ironic mix of Italian history, legends and fairy tales, in a Medieval setting. All blended with humour that is further evident in the geography: the world is Laitia, that's an anagram of Italia (Italy), and all its region have names that are anagrams of Italian ones. Together with "I Signori del Caos", that was developed at the same time but was released a few months before, it is the first Italian role-playing game, preceding the Italian edition of Basic D&D in 1985. This box, released by a well known toy company, contains a basic rulebook, a bestiary, the "La Maschera d'Oro e il Cimitero dei Pellegrini" adventure ("The Golden Mask and the Pilgrims' Cemetery"), a colour map of the world, dice and a sandglass.

title: The Curse of Batterslea Hall
description: (Reader summary) In Richard Brightfield's third CYOA book, the reader is sent to England to help solve the mystery of a cursed estate. This is the 30th book in the Choose Your Own Adventure series.

title: 10th Anniversary Dungeons & Dragons Collector's Set
description: The 10th Anniversary Dungeons & Dragons Collector's Set was a simple compilation of previously-published materials, bundled and sold in a shrink-wrapped, faux leather slipcase at Gen Con 17 (and subsequently via mail order). One thousand sets were produced. Once removed from the shrink-wrap, the contained items are not individually distinguishable from the corresponding "non-collector's-set" items. The set includes: Dungeons & Dragons Set 1: Basic Rules (Player's and DM's booklets) Dungeons & Dragons Set 2: Expert Rules (Expert Set booklet) Dungeons & Dragons Set 3: Companion Rules (Player's and DM's booklets) AC6: Player Character Record Sheets M1: Blizzard Pass B1: In Search of the Unknown B2: The Keep on the Borderlands X1: The Isle of Dread AC2: Combat Shield and Mini-adventure AC3: 3-D Dragon Tiles featuring The Kidnapping of Princess Arelina Dice, Crayon, Pen

title: The Adventurer's Handbook: A Guide to Role-Playing Games
description: From the back cover: Return with us now to those thrilling days of Tolkien, Moorcock - even King Arthur's Britain. Now you can do more than read these (and other) charming fantasies. YOU can be a King - or a Princess - or a Dragon if you like. With this creative new book you can explore, conquer, and perhaps even amass a fortune. The Adventurer's Handbook takes you to the world of fantasy - a world of role-playing games in which the players create and control characters who live their imaginary lives in a specially made game land. You can base your games in such well-known fantasies as Tolkien's Middle Earth, Ursula Le Guin's Earthsea, or Moorcock's Young Kingdoms. Or if originality is your forte, you can create your own unique world of wonder and clone a hero of your own. user comment this book contains a core set of rules, very similar, but simpler, to Chaosium's Basic Role-Playing system.

title: Invasion U.S.!
description: From the Back of the Module FIGHTING FOR FREEDOM Tyranny in the Streets In the 1990's America has fallen to the communists after the third world war. With the US under a harsh Soviet occupation force, you fight back to regain your lost freedom. Invasion US Invasion US is a game module of small group RPG action against a hostile force. Characters will live in the Peoples Republic of America where every movement is watched by the KGB and the slightest slip will send them to prison or death. Invasion US is a game system supplement for Stalking the Night Fantastic or Fringeworthy that can become a game in itself with the use of Tri Tac role playing systems. The Rebellion Has Begun!

title: Rhand: Morningstar Missions
description: Back cover text: A Complete Fantasy Role-Playing Game on the World of Rhand Rhand is a fallen world, a world at war. A shattered humanity retreats each year before a wave of ice. Driven by hunger, the cultures of Rhand fight among themselves for the planet's dwindling resources, slowly reverting to complete barbarism. The faceless, inhuman Spectrals stalk through the ruins, existing only to destroy humanity. In this chaos the only real stability lies in the city-states, small nations struggling to survive in a land of ice, war, and hatred. Beleaguered and isolated, the city-states and indeed all of Rhand have only one hope for the future: The ORCA. An elite group of heroes and healers, the ORCA is the last chance for life and civilization in a world gone mad. Join the ORCA and enter the eternal battle for the fate of Rhand as you: • Encounter the wily Jineta, master horsemen and rulers of the plains. • Deal with the Clochardi, subtle and powerful humans who wrest life out of the icelands. • Aid the reclusive Vid in their battle to save the vanishing forests they have made their homes. • Combat the Vissers, warped, malevolent creatures that were once human. • Pursue the twisted people who call themselves Blades, madmen who have betrayed their race for greed, power, and hatred. • Battle the jet-black, unthinking Spectrals, the enemies of all life on Rhand. And Rhand: Morningstar Missions gives you more than alien cultures, fascinating opponents and the glory of the ORCA's struggle to save a world; it gives you a revolution in game design. The unique rules system puts you on the battlefield: • In the chaos of combat you must stab, slash, duck, and parry. • Aim your blows where you wish — the limbs, the head, or even the heart. • Batter down your opponent's shield to create an opening for an ally. • Drag an enemy from horseback and continue the fight with or without weapons. • Feel the tension of the bowstring on your fingertips. • Wield the forces of magic — the powers of the elements and a full range of mental abilities. • Master any skills you wish by study or field experience.

title: The Lost Wizard
description: Publisher Blurb You are Andru. Shrunk to the size of a fly, you are on a quest with your new pixie friend in search of the Lost Wizard. As a wizard's apprentice, you can use your magic powers to get you out of jams -- but not always!

title: Sword of the Samurai
description: Publisher blurb This book is a time machine. Travel back in time 330 years and become a samurai.

title: The Mystery of the Highland Crest
description: (Reader summary) Whereas The Curse of Battslea Hall (CYOA #30) sent the reader to England to solve a mystery, this time you're headed to Scotland for some serious sleuthing. This is the 34rd book in the Choose Your Own Adventure series.

title: Bureau 13: Stalking the Night Fantastic (2nd Edition)
description: From the introduction: The history of the human race is filled with evidence of eerie and unexplained occurrences. Our myths, legends and fairy tales consistently reaffirm that the supernatural exists. This knowledge of the "supernatural" has been with mankind since before the dawn of history. Mostly these occurrences were misunderstood and greatly feared by the general populace. With no organization, it was usually the small mobs of angry peasants that stalked the creatures of the night, and more often than not, exterminated the supernatural, good and evil. Always, though, there have been a few who were capable of discerning the passing difference between good and evil. In the early 1860's the United States established a secret supernatural investigative agency under the cover of the Civil War. Only a few top officials knew of its existence and it became known simply as "Bureau 13". For the next century the few employees of the Bureau went quietly about their "business" of ferreting out and eliminating the destructive aspects of the supernatural. So successful were their efforts that the memories of the public dimmed and the fear of the unknown was replaced by awe (and suppressed fear) of the new technologies. Foreign branches of the organization were established in Europe, Africa and Asia. The years have passed and worldwide memories have faded. The public has come to believe that magic and the supernatural are the stuff of children's dreams and nightmares. They are wrong. 1989: Bureau 13, now an ultramodern force, more secret than before, fights to stem the growth of ancient magic and the supernatural that threatens the innocent. Wherever the supernatural waits, good and evil, the agents of Bureau 13 will be there, but... Evil is growing.

title: Tower of Midnight Dreams
description: user summary Book 1 in the Dungeons & Dragons Cartoon book series. A Pick-a-Path-to-Adventure book. You are Bobby the Barbarian. When your friends are captured by the evil Venger, you must use all your courage to rescue them from the Tower of Midnight Dreams.

title: Vikings and Valkyrs Volume I: Character Classes, Skills, Magic
description: This book covers character creation and development and the magic system. Characters have attributes strongly reminiscent of those used in Dungeons & Dragons at the time (basically, the D&D attributes plus Luck and Will). Classes are berserkers, runemasters, skalds, warlocks, warriors, and wizards.

title: The Future King
description: From the back cover: Six historical heroes: Doc Holiday, Nostradamus, Bruce Lee, Harald Hardraada, Owen Glendower, and Cyrano de Bergerac have been gathered together out of the mists of time. Their mission is to find and wake King Arthur, for it is time that he again don the mantle of kingship. All you need to play The Future King is this adventure booklet and a common pair of dice. It is not necessary to first learn some seperate rules system -- nor do you need any of the odd-shaped dice used in so many role-playing games. So just take The Future King home, read it, and you'll be ready to play.

title: Falcon 2: Mechanon
description: From the back of the book: The Ultimate Weapon. The fate of Earth lies in your hands! As special agent Falcon, you have just been briefed on the alarming timeline changes emanating from the long-dead world of Mechanon. Once it had been an enormous factory-planet, where teams of robot warriors assembled a fearsome array of space weapons...until a terrible nuclear catastophe reduced it to a tangled ruin of rock and metal. Now a renegade Time Lord is using his powers to wipe out that past disaster, and make Mechanon once more the most deadly arsenal the universe has ever seen! Time is short - and only you own ingenuity in altering the course of history can save mankind from the Time Lord's ultimate weapon!

title: Castle Thrax
description: From the back of the book: Tired of doing all the work for the group you adventure with? Now you can play too. Castle Thrax does the work for you. A revolutionary concept in Fantasy Role-Playing modules is designed to be played without a dungeon master! No referee of any kind! One person is assigned to simply read the descriptions and instructions from this unique adventure packet. No dungeon mastering experience is necessary. Sure, being the dungeon master is fun, but not all the time. Castle Trax gives you the opportunity to adventure with your friends, meet the same challenges, slay the same vile threats to humanity. And gain experience points right along with them. Castle Thrax has other playing opportunities. Perhaps your friends live a great distance from you or they don't get together to play as often as you like. Get out your favorite character and some NPC's and play Castle Thrax solo. There's no reason to be left behind as your friends' characters gain increasingly higher levels because they play more often than you.

title: Warlords
description: From the Back of the Book: Role-Playing in the Orient Inside are two books for you and a friend to use in playing an all-new kind of adventure. One of you will take the part of Prince Matabei, dispossessed of his kingdom by his uncle, Lord Kenshin, who will be played by the other player. In the dead of night, on a fog-shrouded coast, the prince returns to Tsuke, his former kingdom. During the coming days he must investigate the entire province, locate Lord Kenshin's troops, and determine who is for him and who against. At the same time, Lord Kenshin will be fighting to keep control in a land that has come to hate him. Either of you can win... and either of you can die. This an exciting game of hand-to-hand combat, troop movements, and subtle negotiation, all in the oriental style. No dice are required. Instead a traditional paper-scissors-rock game determines the outcome of combat and other encounters. All you need to start is one friend and two pencils! PLAY ANYTIME! ANYWHERE! PLAY MANY TIMES! THE GAME IS ALWAYS DIFFERENT!

title: Falcon 1: The Renegade Lord
description: From the back of the book: Race against Time It's 3033AD. You're a special agent - codename Falcon - working for TIME. You've been personally selected to pilot a time machine on a perilous journey into the unknown. Your mission: to stop the renegade Time Lord whose meddling with the past is threatening to throw the timelines of future history into total chaos! Your quest will take you deep into the tumult of bygone days, and your only weapon will be your blaster and the powers of your mind with wich you - and you alone - can change the destiny of mankind!

title: Ice Elves
description: From the back cover: Beyond the dark forests, beyond the ragged mountains, beyond the realm of the humans, lies a FORBIDDING LAND OF ICE AND SNOW. ICE ELVES First glimpsed in the pages of last year's best-selling module, ELVES, these barren plains are now brought to life. Your adventurers must trek over the frozen wastes in a QUEST FOR THE SACRED GEM. Face the treachery of man and the perils of a hostile wilderness, command an ice rigger in mortal combat with pirates, and journey into the stronghold of the evil raiders. A blood-chilling adventure for 4-6 characters of skill levels 7-9.

title: The Witch's Spell Book
description: user summary Book 3 in the Dungeons & Dragons Cartoon book series. A Pick-a-Path-to-Adventure book. You are Sheila the thief, and you must rescue your friends from orcs.

title: James Bond in Programmed for Danger
description: Publisher Blurb: You are James Bond! The revolutionary Z-dis - a micro-energy source - has been stolen. Will you recover it before it falls into the wrong hands?

title: Tarzan and the Well of Slaves
description: Endless Quest #26 From back of book: You have followed the trail of strangely clad figures with their leashed leopards deep into unexplored mountain country in the hope they will lead you to the answer behind the disappearances amoung your loyal Waziri followers. But nothing has prepared you for what you now see as you stand atop a ridge high above the valley spread below you. For beneath you lies a magnificent city where none should be, a replica of those built by the Pharaohs four thousand years ago! And suddenly you know the answer to your quest lies within its ancient, mysterious walls! You are Tarzan of the Apes, and the choices are yours in an ENDLESS QUEST Book.

title: Dragonsword of Lankhmar
description: From the Back of the Book: A New Kind of Game Inside are two books for two players. One of you becomes the famous pair of thieves, FAFHRD and the GRAY MOUSER. The other takes the part of members of the Thieves' Guild. Fafhrd and the Gray Mouser are in search of the fabled Dragonsword. The Thieves' Guild is in search of Fafhrd and the Gray Mouser, trying to put an end to their "unauthorized" thieving activities! Enter the famous city of Lankhmar where wealth, beautiful women, and danger await you on every corner. The Gods of Trouble and Death have an intense interest in your quest! (Your opponent takes the part of all monsters and enemies you fight, just as you take the part of any enemies he faces!) An easy, dice-less combat system simulates battle. In is an exciting game of thievery, assassination, and adventure as you roam the streets of Lankhmar. Everything you need to play is provided: two books, magic spells, Combat Table, Character Sheets. No dice required. All you provide is one friend and two pencils. PLAY ANYTIME! ANYWHERE! PLAY MANY TIMES! THE GAME IS ALWAYS DIFFERENT!

title: Clash of the Sorcerers
description: Super Endless Quest Adventure Gamebook #11 From Back Cover: Now you can experience all the thrills of playing the ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Game by yourself-- in book form! ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Adventure Gamebooks use a simple, unique role-playing game system based on the popular ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Game, incorporating two standard six-sided dice, hit points, skill points, experience points, and much more. CLASH OF THE SORCERERS brings you to the exciting climax of the KINGDOM OF SORCERY gamebook trilogy, as Carr Delling confronts his old enemy, the archmage Arno, in an attempt to save the land from corruption. ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Adventure Gamebooks read like books, play like games, and offer a thrill a minute-- with YOU as the hero! Book 3 of The Kingdom of Sorcery Trilogy. 26 possible endings.

title: Night of the Ninja Mastery Manual
description: subtitled Reality Role-Playing Game from the back cover Can you think like a Ninja? Do you Expect the Unexpected? Make the Impossible Possible? Night of the Ninja Mastery Manual includes 68 pages of easy to learn player's and ninja master (NM) rules section. Inside cover contains Ninja Master data screen for easy chart and table references. Complexity level 3 needs only 2 10-sided dice to generate action, combat, suspense and hours of imaginative, intense entertainment. A brief history of the warrior ways of the ninja is included.

title: Monster Manuscript
description: User Summary The Monster Manuscript, subtitled An Adventurer's Guide to Fantastic Creatures, contains page after page of D&D-style monster write-ups. Originally a free item, the Manuscript stats out a variety of creatures that appeared in Grenadier Models Dragon Lords Monster Manuscript miniatures line that appeared in 1986 & 1987. The last couple of pages include painting tips for the various creatures that appear in the book, as well as a write-up for an entire undead legion. This module-sized booklet was given out with Grenadier set #1501 (which contained a mix of fantasy monsters).

title: Falcon 5: The Dying Sun
description: From the back of the book: Against All Odds! You've just been chosen for what's probably the toughest assignment you'll ever have tackled. Your mission is to neutralise the menace of the evil Yelov and your orders are brutally simple: either you capture him or you void him! Yelov has back-up from a fearsome team of cosmic warriors, but against such unyielding might your own support crew is almost hopelessly outnumbered. As you skip through time and space in your cosmic racer, arcing perilously close to the dying sun of Yelov's home planet Sirius, you'll have only split seconds in which to make the key decisions on which so many innocent lives depend!

title: Falcon 6: At The End of Time
description: From the back of the book: Trapdoor Through Time! Fate has hurled you into a vortex of unbelievable horror - a vital flaw in the fabric of time that sends everything that has ever been or ever will be plummeting towards the cosmic scrapheap that marks the very end of time. Impossible creatures from ten millions years ago - or hence - hurtle past as you struggle to escape your onward plunge into oblivion. The slightest error of judgement on your part, or a momentary hesitation, and all will be lost - and you'll never again be able to find your way back through that terrifying trapdoor in time!

title: The Case of the Silk King
description: From the back of the book: One day an envelope is mailed to your detective agency. Inside are two one-thousand-dollar bills, a plane ticket to Thailand, and a newspaper article. The article is about the incredible disappearance of Jim Thompson, the 'Thai Silk King'. It promises a huge reward to whoever finds him! Someone wants you to investigate- but who?

title: The Doomsday Device: Fantastic Four vs Doctor Doom
description: From the Back of the Book: The Future of New York is in Your Hands! Inside are two books for two players. One of you becomes the crazed scientist and would-be world conqueror DOCTOR DOOM. The other plays Doom's arch rival in the MARVEL UNIVERSE, REED RICHARDS, and the FANTASTIC FOUR. If you play the part of the brilliant super villain Doctor Doom, your objective will be to make New York a has-been on your way to taking over Florida -- and the world! If you play the part of the Fantastic Four, your goal will be to make Doctor Doom and his doomsday device look like amusements from Coney Island. Everything you need to play is provided: two books, Combat Table, and Character Sheets. No dice are required. All you need is a healthy supply of luck and two pencils! PLAY ANYTIME, ANYWHERE, MANY TIMES! The game is different every time you play, but your well-being is always at stake in THE DOOMSDAY DEVICE.

title: Traveller Character Generator
description: Publisher's blurb: Generate Basic Traveller characters on your Apple // computer! Includes 16 character careers from Book 1: Characters and Combat and Supplement 04: Citizens of the Imperium, or make your own! Print out characters. Supports Alternate Survival Rule. For Apple ][+, //e, //c with 48K or more memory.

title: Escape from Colditz
description: Back of the Book As World War II draws to its climax, you, an RAF pilot, are shot down over Dresden and after several abortive attempts to escape from other POW camps you are sent to Colditz, the ultimate challenge for would-be escapers. Heartless German soldiers patrol the battlements, indifferent to the bitter wind. No transgression of the rules, however slight, ever goes unpunished. But in the prison quarters, men from different Allied nations are plotting tirelessly, racking their brains to think of ever more devious ways to outwit the German Commandant and escape. But it is never easy and success is all too rare. You must succeed where so many others have failed, and escape from this apparently unassailable fortress. And, as if that weren't enough, you must then make your way through enemy territory until you reach the safety of either a neutral or Allied country. Can you bring off your escape from Colditz? Pit your wits against the Nazis and cross your fingers that luck is with you. You'll need it! In this game book, you are the hero. A die, a pencil and an eraser are all you need to set off on your adventure.

title: Vision of Doom
description: Endless Quest #35 From the back cover: You are certain that your nightmare vision of doom is a prophec of the future. And if it is, soon the Vampire King will arise from his sleep of living death to roam the land, wreaking his aweful revenge, unless you can somehow stop him... You are about to enter the exciting world experienced by the players of the popular DUNGEONS AND DRAGONS Game. It is a fantasy world filled with enchanted castles and fearsome monsters. Are YOU brave enough to meet its challenges? In other ENDLESS QUEST Books, you can combat secret agents skilled in espionage, journey to the corners of the galaxy, or attempt to survive in a future world ravaged by wars. Each ENDLESS QUEST Book is based on an exciting role-playing game, but you don't need to know the game to enjoy the book. Just make your choices, and accept the consequences! Remember! Only your choices lead to success in ENDLESS QUEST Books.

title: The Monsterous Civilizations of Delos
description: "In Delos, Monsters aren't always ... visit the Ogre Nations of Delos, travel the Forests of Night and meet the legendary Wampyr and their Wool-o-the-Wisp and explore the legendary Broken Lands where even the normal is stranger than you can imagine. As you journey through the Monstrous Civilizations of Delos, you'll learn the real perils of succubi, how to get a giant drunk and (maybe) what has become of the fabled Gnoptic Chalice. Overall a dastardly miscellany of new traps, poisons, artifacts...."

title: Rivermasters of Arania
description: In Rivermasters of Arania the characters move north to encounter the strange merchants and heroes of the Kingdom of Arania while on a trading mission. For character levels 6-9.

title: Rupert and the Pirates' Den
description: From the Front of the Book: A strange old map starts you and Rupert on an exciting search!

title: Seaside Mystery
description: From publisher blurb: Do you dare learn the secret of the lost mermaid? You're spending the summer at a seaside boardinghouse. One moonlit night you hear someone crying on the beach. You peek out your window — and see a mermaid named Tana. You'd like to help her, but she's in a terrible mood. "I don't know where the rest of the mermaids are," she snaps. "I've been looking for two days, but I can't find any of them. And if you want to hear any more, you'll have to follow me underwater!"

title: Dragonwand of Krynn: Lord Patrick vs Lord Ariakan
description: From the Back of the Book: A New Kind of Game Inside are two books for two players. One of you becomes Lord Patrick, Knight of Solamnia, while the other becomes Lord Ariakan, Dragon Highlord. You are both searching for the same thing: the fabled DragonWand of Krynn! Enter the landlocked city of Tarsis, where you must first locate the ancient Temple of the Dragon. Once inside the temple, your mission is to find the powerful DragonWand before your opponent can reach it and use it for his own purposes. It is a game filled with the huge dragons, the fierce draconians, and the wonderful magicl artifacts that you've come to expect when you enter the adventure-filled world of the Dragonlance adventures. Everything you need to play is provided: two books, Combat Table, and Character Sheets. No dice are required. All you provide is one friend and two pencils! PLAY ANYTIME! ANYWHERE! PLAY MANY TIMES! THE GAME IS ALWAYS DIFFERENT!

title: Daredevil vs Kingpin: The King Takes a Dare
description: From the Back of the Book: The Big Apple Has a Rotten Core Midnight in the city of New York. It's raining. Three miles away from each other, two men make exactly the same decision at the same time. One is Daredevil, the red-costumed fighter for justice, who uses his uncanny senses to fight evil where he finds it. The other is Kingpin, the pinstripe-clad warrior, who has followed his own code of ethics while fighting and clawing his way to the top of a criminal empire more powerful than many small nations. Each has decided to eliminate the other in a duel to the death on the streets of New York. Inside are two books for two players. One of you becomes Daredevil, the other Kingpin. Everything you need to play is provided: two books, Combat Table, and Character Sheets. No dice are required. All you need is a healthy supply of luck and two pencils. PLAY ANYTIME, ANYWHERE, MANY TIMES! The game is different every time you play, and your well-being is always at stake in THE KING TAKES A DARE

title: Avengers
description: An alternate timeline for TimeLords. From the back cover: "Avengers. 2212 AD. A small team of probability researcchers watches helplessly while a temporal ambush wipes out 99% of Earth's population. In the only surviving orbital colony, they become Avengers, to protect and rebuild, and to find and destroy the organization known only as Black Death. A generation later, the fight goes on. Earth is still a ravaged wasteland, but the tiny reconstruction teams are making a difference. Black Death is still out there, but has grown more subtle. Avenger agents must journey to alternate worlds to stop threats aimed at their own, or journey into Earth's wastelands to stop long-term plots. Avengers contains complete backgrounds on this alternate timeline, plans of the space colony Chronos, new equipment, character generation, and the short adventure, Fall of Parugue, suitable for Avenger agents or introductory SpaceTime characters.

title: The Black River Emerald
description: The once-exclusive Belton school for boys in Shropshire hasseen better days, but theft, blackmail and attempted murder are not yet part of the curriculum! Prowl the shadows of the Belton School grounds and shadow suspects through the crowds of London in this thrilling adventure. (From back cover.)

title: Encyclopedia of Xanth
description: A Crossroads Adventure in the world of Piers Anthony's Xanth User Summary: You take the part of young Alister, with the Talent to find anything. Alister must locate and procure the Encyclopedia of Xanth in order for Good Magician Humphrey to answer his Question. Part RPG, part choose your own adventure novel.

title: Miekka ja magia
description: From the back of the book: »Miekka ja Magia« on roolipeliseikkailupeli, jossa Sinä ja ystäväsi voitte kokea henkeäsalpaavan jännittäviä hetkiä maanalaisissa onkaloissa ja synkissä luolissa seikkaillen. Tehtävänänne on löytää vuosisatoja kateissa ollut salainen kulta-aarre, jota vartioivat kammottavat hirviöt ja salaperäiset taikoja käyttävät Velhot. Seikkailunne aikana joudutte moniin jännittäviin tilanteisiin ja hengenvaarallisiin taisteluihin. Vampyyrit, käärmeet, maahiset, örkit ja lohikäärmeet janoavat vertanne ja vain nopeat ratkaisut, tarkat miekaniskut ja hallittu magian käyttö voivat johtaa teidät päämääräänne. Miekka ja Magia on tarkoitettu 3-7 pelaajalle, joista yksi toimii pelinjohtajana. Hänen tehtävänsä on luoda jännittävä seikkailu tämän kirjan ohjeiden ja sääntöjen perusteella. Kaikki mitä tämän kirjan lisäksi tarvitset Miekka ja Magia -rooliseikkailun pelaamiseen, ovat noppa, kynä ja paperia sekä annos ennakkoluulotonta seikkailuhenkeä! Miekka ja Magia -rooliseikkailupelin pelaajien ikäsuositus on kymmenestä vuodesta ylöspäin. Peli on tarkoitettu myös aikuisille. --- Translation: »Sword and Magic« is a roleplaying adventure game, where you and your friends could experience breathtakingly tense moments in underground caves and dimly lid dungeons. Your goal is to find centuries ago forgotten secret golden treasure, which is now guarded by hideous monsters and mysterious spell casting wizards. During your adventures you will undergo many perilious moments and life threating battles. Vampires, snakes, goblins, ogres and dragons are thirsty for your blood and only fast decisions, keen swordplay and well controlled use of magic can lead you to your aim. Sword and Magic is for 3 to 7 players. One of the players is assuming duties of gamemaster. Gamemasters mission is to create thrilling adventure in guidelines and rules of this guidebook. All you need for playing Sword and Magic roleplaying adventure game are dice, pen, paper and unbiased adventurous spirit! Sword and Magic -roleplaying game is for ages ten to up. Game is also meant for adult enjoyment.

title: To The City of Brass
description: From the Introduction: "This module was created by Robert Kuntz (one of the original founders of D&D), and was to be published by his company, Creations Unlimited (who previously published Garden of the Plantmaster and the four modules in the Zayene series). The company unfortunately went out of business, and the planned series of RPGA modules based on the City of Brass never saw the light of day. Kuntz released this first module to the Internet and it is freely distributable, provided it is kept intact and in its entirety." ... All of you have been gathered to hear the High Priests of Nusu-Sa's message. Though you have been informed through various sources that the Sun God's edict is forthcoming— no doubt, with a redeeming and accusative quality, as is the case with pronouncements such as these—you are still pensive, and some of you are concerned. As you stand on a humongous rug of white fur, you hear the rustling robes of your religious sovereign as he enters the audience chamber. His steps are measured, as has been his guidance of the Sun God's cause. With a wave of his hand he dismisses the servants; and with the other he bids you a silent welcome. He then sits, and you do so also, reclining upon the thick white furs. As he speaks, he pulls forth a scroll from his vestments and unrolls it. On it are various marks and signs, all painted in fiery red pigments. From your vantage you note a strange land, totally unfamiliar. He sets the map on the rug and waves his arm above it, as if this were a means of introduction.

title: Never Cry Crawdad
description: Never Cry Crawdad is the only adventure published for the humorous post-apocalyptic beer and pretzels roleplaying game [Creeks & Crawdads]. The module parodies dungeon crawls, and includes zombie crawdads from Hell, and... a basilisk?

title: The Brilliant Dr. Wogan
description: From the back of the book. A machine that could stop radiation would shift the balance of world power overnight. In the right hands, it could herald a new age of world peace. In the wrong hands, it would spell disaster. The brilliant Dr. Wogan has designed such a machine. But she has gone missing, along with the plans. As her right hand assistant, everyone looks to you to help find her. "Wars are best before they begin." Or so said the great Chinese military strategist Tsun Tzu in the 6th century. Nuclear disarmament would sure help, you think. Dr. Wogan's device will make a better world. But it's not a world everyone necessarily wants. Good luck!

title: Ogre Forest
description: From the back of the bookThis module is designed to give starting adventures for inexperienced characters, players and GMs. It contains two adventures, Ogre Forest and The Return of Twain Alderyn in addition to five special encounters. It also includes six pages of high quality color maps. It was originally bundled with (and shrinkwrapped together with) the screen.

title: Murder at the Diogenes Club
description: Scandal shakes London's most exclusive men's club as Colonel Philip Sylvester lies murdered in the reading room. Can you uncover the murderer, the motive and the method? Is anyone among the respected but anti-social members a likely suspect? (From back cover.)

title: Roger Zelazny's Nine Princes in Amber: The Black Road War
description: From the back of the book: CONQUER THE HEART OF AMBER Amber, that incredible, beautiful, terrifying world where Random rules as king and where Corwin the Murderer wanders free. Amber, world of misbegotten beauty and unrealized power. Land of order, land of oppression, land of destroyed dreams. You are the son of Eric, and you lay your claim on Amber. You're determined to infiltrate the heart of that kingdom. But are you strong enough to survive its many dangers - and claim its savage throne?

title: Empires & Dynasties
description: The Empires & Dynasties box contains everything needed to play: - 2 68 pages books. The first "Le monde de Reah", contains the main rules, the character creation system, the experience system, and a description of the game world, including an overview of the grammar and pronunciation of the Reahn language. The second book contains chapters about magic (including rules), religion, technology, a bestiary and an introduction scenario, "La disgrâce". - a 3 panels gamemaster screen - a 80×60 cm map of the game world.

title: La Noche del Demonio
description: user summary in English "La Noche del Deminio" (the night of demon) is an unofficial module for Call of Cthulhu self-published by Alex de la Iglesia and Joaquin Agreda, were part of the "Club de Rol los Pelotas". This module is based on the movie Night of the Demon by Jacques Tourneur, iIlustrado with maps and photographs of the film. There was a limited edition of 50 copies and they were all numbered. "La Noche del Deminio" is a first adventure is continuing with "El Ámbar Rojo" (the red amber), another module self-published from the same designers. (do not know the exact year of publication, but is between 1986 and 1989). In Spanish: "La Noche del Deminio" es un módulo no oficial de la Llamada de Cthulhu autopublicado por Alex de la Iglesia y Joaquin Agreda cuando formaban parte del "Club de Rol Los Pelotas". Este módulo está basado en la película Night of the Demon de Jacques Tourneur, ilustrado con mapas y fotografías de la propia película. Se hizo una edición limitada de 50 ejemplares y todos estaban numerados. "La Noche del Deminio" es una primera aventura que continua con "El Ámbar Rojo", otro módulo autoeditado de los mismo diseñadores. (he puesto que el año de publicación fue el 1988, pero realmente no sabria concretarlo con exactitud, el Club de Rol existió entre el 1986 y el 1989).

title: Warhammer Townscape: Card Buildings for Warhammer games
description: user summary Warhammer Townscape is a collection of 39 card houses, inns, towers, ruins, outhouses and much more. Printed in full colour, the buildings are quick and easy to assemble - the ideal backdrop for wargames and roleplayng games. All the Townscape buildings are compatible with Warhammer Fantasy Battle, Warhammer 40,000 and Warhammer Fantasy Roleplay.

title: Seven No-Trump
description: In this Crossroads Adventure, players take on the role of Random, the current King of Amber, as he tries to determine the whereabouts of his brothers, the Princes of Amber. The only clue to their disappearance is a set of twisted pictures, which Random must travel through to find his brothers in the shadows of Shadows.

title: Jack Williamson's The Legion of Space: The Legion at War
description: From the back cover: Command the Legion of Space You are Vice Admiral David Ulnar, commanding the Ninth Defense Squadron on the Orion Frontier. You are all that stands between the League of Worlds and the world-wrecking planetoid of Ka'slaq invaders. Now your command will be tested in the most destructive battles ever fought in Jack Williamson's classic tale. Will you outguess the Ka'slaq and block their light-speed attacks - or will their deadly antimatter weapons hold hostage a crippled League of Worlds? Combat Command book #5

title: The Honour of the Yorkshire Light Artillery
description: It is 1890, and the London dailies are filled with shocking accounts of jewel thefts in the west of England. Sherlock Holmes investigates, and you and Dr. Watson accompany him. As Holmes looks into the thefts, you are asked to protect a golden eagle called "the Honour." While guarding it you witness a re-staging of an historic duel, and a tragic mishap occurs. (From back cover.)

title: Camo Specs: A Guide to Regimental Paint Schemes
description: From the back of the book: The most powerful regiments are comprised of Battlemechs and MechWarriors. All are steeped in tradition, honor, and duty. A single BattleMech can level a city, and can only opposed by another of its kind. Piloted by the elite class known as MechWarriors, these machines, when organized into regiments, are a force to make planets tremble. Regiments are built on pride. A regiment's color embody that pride. The Guide to BattleTech Regimental Paint Schemes provides the distinctive pattern and colors, as well as a description of selected regiments of the Inner Sphere. Includes 44 full-color illustrations, including several 'Mechs, tanks, and other vehicles in Arctic, Woodland and Desert camouflage schemes.

title: Return of the White Witch
description: User note: This book is quite rare, as most of the line was destroyed after printing, before distribution. From the back cover: A game where you adventure in Narnia! All seems well in Narnia. Fauns and nymphs whirl in midnight dances under the forest's eaves. The wild Red Dwarfs feast and treasure-seek in mines and caverns far beneath the forest floor. In the vaulted Great Hall, the noble folk of Cair Paravel listen to minstrel's tales. Yet there are signs of discord. Black Dwarfs and Wolves comb the ruins of the White Witch's castle, while a Werewolf haunts Lantern Waste. And an evil Hag hobbles along the peaceful country lanes. In Return of the White Witch(TM), YOU must confront these enemies and thwart their foul plans! In NARNIA SOLO GAMES(TM) you can: - Play a ready-to-go hero/heroine. - Create your own hero/heroine using the NARNIA SOLO GAMES(TM) system. - Complete a noble mission for Aslan and all of Narnia!

title: Desperados
description: From the back of the book"Ya Must Be Cheatin'!" The young Cowpoke had just lost a week's wages, and anger had gotten the better of his good sense. "I said yer a cheat; and I guess if ya won't step outside ta prove me wrong; yer yeller too." The Gambler slid his chair from the table and stood slowly. Wearing his best poker-face, he walked toward the door. "I'll be waitin' for ya in the street." The Cowpoke, beginning to doubt himself, looked around the bar room for support. Finding none, he screwed up his courage and stepped outside. The Gambler was waiting, ready. The Cowpoke went for his gun first; but before it cleared the holster, he had the Gambler's bullet in his heart. The Gambler stepped over the body, back into the saloon, and back to his place at the card table. Still poker-faced, he began shuffling. "Now that the kid's gone, we can do some serious gambling..." With DESPERADOS, you could have played that fight in about the time it took to read it. Easy percentile based rules, a hit location system that works, and a character sheet that's the only reference players need for combat, give DESPERADOS ihe spirit and pacing of a classic Western movie. Cinch up yer gun belt, saddle up yer horse, grab a character sheet and some dice, and come with us now to a thrilling game of yesteryear; to a world where justice comes from the end of a six-shooter; to the game of DESPERADOS!

title: Prince of Thieves
description: Super Endless Quest Adventure Gamebook #18 From Back Cover: Now you can experience all the thrills of playing the ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Game by yourself-- in book form! ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Adventure Gamebooks use a simple, unique role-playing game system based on the popular ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Game, incorporating two standard six-sided dice, hit points, skill points, experience points, and much more. In PRINCE OF THIEVES, you are Jadd Hachen, master thief, and your latest "acquisitions"- three gems of undreamed-of powers- not only change your life forever but also threaten the very existence of the kingdom! ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS Adventure Gamebooks read like books, play like games, and offer a thrill a minute-- with YOU as the hero! 34 possible endings.

title: Ghost of a Chance
description: In this solitaire RPG/choose your own adventure book, the player takes on the role of Max, an inhabitant of Xanth with the magical Talent of the lantern jaw. He seeks the lost mate of Brun, the Bluebird of Happiness, in order to procure Brun's blessing on his own impending marriage to Onda.

title: The Bats of Mercille (1st Edition)
description: From the front page: "The Jump-Start Kit includes: - a complete, easy to run adventure. One that explores the role-playing & adventure possibilities inherent Ars Magica - eight characters, including two magi, With personality, histories and relationships all detailed. - Four different one page game briefings that quickly and simply introduce players to Ars Magica."

title: Animonde
description: Designed by the noted French designer Croc, and drawing on the themes in "La Planete Oubliee" by M. Leinster, Animonde is a fantasy RPG set in a world without metal, and where animals and humans have a harmonious, symbiotic relationship. The game is intended to be non-violent, with the players using persuasion and intimidation instead of direct violence (although it is possible). Written in French, with a very poetic, idyllic, fantastic world, and derived from Croc's previous RPG Bitume, Animonde was published by Siroz in 1988, and it's non-violent approach was in stark contrast to the hack & slash genres dominating the market, leading to an inevitable cancellation of the game. - user summary -

title: The Crown vs. Dr. Watson
description: It's 1894, and Sherlock Holmes has been presumed dead for the previous two and a half years. Watson's story, "The Final Problem," seems to confirm the loss of the great detective. Seeking to fill the void left by Holmes, you are asked by Mycroft Holmes, Sherlock's brother, and a reluctant Scotland Yard to investigate the murder of Sir Terrence Milton, a London businessman. The evidence points squarely at Dr. Watson, who had an altercation with the victim shortly before his death. Can you uncover new evidence and expose the murderer? (From back cover.)

title: Der schlafende Drache von Iliahad
description: The first of multiple Universal-fantasy-adventures from the "Drachenlandverlag". With stats for DSA, Midgard and AD&D. This is a classic Crawl, but it's not a dungeon, it's an Island with a Tower. Traps and secret chambers and of course at the end, the sleeping Dragon of Iliahad.

title: La Vallée des Rois
description: From the back of the box Entrez dans l'univers des légendes et des exploits héroïques. Combattez les ennemis de l'Égypte aux côtés des grands Pharaons. Regardez s'élever les pyramides sur le plateau de Gizeh, et à la cour raffinée d'Amarna écoutez l'enseignement mystique d'Akhénaton. Voyagez vers de lointains rivages pour la gloire de la grande Hatshepsout, et à la « fenêtre des apparitions » contemplez la belle et mystérieuse Néfertiti. Selon votre inspiration devenez Prêtre d'Amon (aux pouvoirs magiques redoutables), soldat farouche luttant pour la gloire de son Roi, sculpteur immortalisant dans le granit d'Assouan les formes gracieuses des princesses royales, et bien d'autres personnages encore. Alors, au moment de votre comparution devant Osiris, vous pourrez prétendre aux plaisirs tranquilles du « bel Occident ». Vous pouvez devenir maintenant ce héros dont les artistes immortaliseront les exploits. Translation Enter the universe of legends and heroic exploits. Fight Egypt's enemies beside the great Pharaohs. See the pyramids arise on the Gizeh plateau, and in the refined court of Amarna listen to the mystical teachings of Akhenaten. Travel to distant shores for the glory of the great Hatshepsut, and at the "window of apparitions" admire the beautiful and mysterious Nefertiti. You can be a Priest of Amun (wielding formidable magical power), a fearsome soldier fighting for the glory of his King, a sculptor immortalising in the Aswan granite the graceful figures of the royal princesses, or any one of many more characters. Then, when you stand in judgement before Osiris, you will rightfully claim access to the tranquil pleasures of the "beautiful Occident". You can now become this hero, whose exploits artists will immortalise. Further player assessment The boxed set includes two booklets (civilization and rules), 72 and 184 pages long, dice (d4, d10, d20), and a copy of Les Héritiers du Soleil tome 1 : Le Masque de mort, by Didier Convard (the series was turned over to Thomas Mosdi and Frédéric Bihel with volume 10, and concluded by Frédéric Bihel with volume 13).

title: The Royal Flush
description: A guard at Buckingham Palace is found murdered under circumstances which implicate a member of the Royal Family. Queen Victoria is worried that an official inquiry would result in lurid newspaper articles. To avoid damaging the prestige of the monarchy, she directs the Chamberlain to conduct a preliminary investigation. He has 48 hours before Scotland Yard intercedes. Perplexed, he turns to YOU! (From back cover.)

title: ULTRA Deadly
description: From published material YOU are John Canaan, an American secret agent asked to serve the British government during World War II. Your assignment: keep ULTRA (the best-kept secret of WWII) from falling into the wrong hands. It is known only to a few higher-ups in the Allied command, and when a briefing on ULTRA is stolen by Germany's top agent, you are sent to retrieve it. The Germans will have a hard time cracking the coded brief, but if they do, the Allied invasion of Normandy could be mass suicide! To succeed in your mission, you'll have to make snap decisions under pressure and use the abilities you've acquired after years in the service--firing a gun with deadly accuracy, overpowering an opponent in hand-to-hand combat, and sneaking into and out of areas with uncanny agility. The challenge is yours because the situation is ULTRA deadly. Additional User Information ULTRA Deadly is Sniper! Adventure Gamebook #5. This title is part of a series of solo adventures published to generate interest in TSR's release of Sniper! 2nd edition" after their acquisition of SPI. The player follows a series of paragraphs, each of which end with a series of choices (or die roll checks) that continue the adventure to the next paragraph. The simple combat resolution system requires only the game book, 2 dice, pencil, & paper. The abilities of the player are preprinted in the gamebook and include Stealth, Hand-to-Hand, Firing, & Bomb-Throwing. The player's starting weapons and equipment are also included.

title: Silrin
description: The Silrin core rules are composed of 3 softcover books slipped into the gamemaster screen. The first book (8 pages) contains the Universom rules. The second book (28 pages) describes the world of Silrin, with a map of the continent, chapters about each race, fauna, and additional rules for character creation (allowing to create a character of any of the four sentient races), gear, vehicles, special powers like the Celnack nobles' ability to fly, social statuses... The third book (also 28 pages) is a scenario ("l'héritage d'Arthron") with pre-generated characters, a Celnack noble and his retainers who go on a quest to retrieve three artifacts to fulfill a prophecy. The game also contains a character sheet and the gamemaster's screen.

title: Berlin XVIII
description: The Berlin XVIII core rules are split in 3 booklets, held together by the 2-panel gamemaster screen. The first rulebook contains the Universom standard rules explained in 9 pages (stats, skills, the combat system). The second booklet details the Berlin XVIII universe: historical summary, maps of Europe in 2070, descriptions of Berlin, the different gangs, the different police forces, how justice works... There are also the character creation rules and a list of available gear (weapons, vehicles...) The last booklet, a scenario ("Auslander") is a serial killer hunt with a twist. It serves as a player introduction to the life of a Falkampft (police procedure, scientific police work...).

title: Jerry E. Pournelle's Janissaries: Lord of Lances
description: From the back of the book: A Soldier of Fortune trapped in an alien world! You are Paul Nelson. Once a CIA mercenary, you took your orders from Rick Galloway. Then an operation went bad. Outnumbered. Outgunned. You faced certain death, until the alien spaceship appeared. But it wasn't a rescue. Dropped on the distant planet Tran, you and the men of your outfit were told to enslave a medieval culture. Using advanced weaponry and military skills, your unit accomplished its mission...then you decided to strike out on your own. Now, with limited firepower and not much ammo, survival is a game you play every day - but can only lose once. Combat Command book #7

title: Adventurers' Guild Bounty Hunters' Handbook
description: From the introduction: Greetings. My name is Def Rillan - master bounty hunter. The tome that you now hold in your hands is compiled from my collection of wanted posters from all over the World of Narak. I have picked some of the worst of the worst for those of you who think you are tough enough to go after them. I myself am even listed in here, in case you go hunting with me, or... go hunting for me. I am wanted by a few unhappy relatives of some of my victims.

title: The Adventurer's Guild Swordtag Rulebook
description: Publisher Blurb from Catalog: Become the character you always wanted to be! Find treasure! Battle monsters! Swordtag is this and more! What profession will you choose to pursue? The spellcasting Mage? An adept Woodsman? Or the poetic Bard? Nine basic professions in all to choose from! What kind of armor and weapon will you use? Can you wield the big awkward battle axe or will you slay monsters with your trusted magical sword? Weapon making directions included. Can you learn the intricacies of spellcasting? If so, will you be able to find the much needed material components? Nearly 100 fully detailed spells included! Will you find and use Holy Water, Potions, and other Magic Items? Will you fight the dreaded Dragon? Nearly 100 Magic Items and Monsters with full stats included! SWORDTAG... Sorry, No Dice!

title: Through the Wire: The Great Escape
description: From the introduction: Your name is Alistair Thompson and you are a Flight Officer in the Royal Air Force. The date is September 1940. You have been literally living on Biggin Hill air field for the past two months, continually on standby. Every time the siren wails, at least three times a day, you leap into the cockpit of your aircraft.
