The mechanics have been further streamlined, and for the first time To Challenge Tomorrow is completely compatible with Ysgarth, our groundbreaking fantasy system. Open the door to a thousand brilliant adventures... play To Challenge Tomorrow."

title: Hardware
description: "Hardware" is the companion book to the Italian version of "Cyberpunk 2020". It contains the final chapters (11 to 15) of the original English language Cyberpunk 2020 rulebook, translated in Italian and with some new artwork.

title: The Adventures of Luther Arkwright
description: From the Foreward of the book: Have you ever thought that there might be a conspiracy out there? Someone, or Something, really behind IT ALL. Manipulating everything. Wars, politics, the weather, advertising (especially advertising), the clothes you wear, the average length of hair, TV and Rock and Roll - the WHOLE PICTURE. There is. They are called the Disruptors. But it's Okay. Luther Arkwright defeated them. We're safe now. And the tale was told in three graphic novels by Bryan Talbot - Luther's chronicler on this Parallel. That's Parallel as in Parallel Earth. The Disruptors were not after just our little corner of all Creation, not by a long shot. We had is easy, really. No dangerously subtle manipulation of the religions on our Parallel, no supposedly accidental Eco-disasters, no Bio-engineered diseases with insidious social side effects, no tendency to concentrate wealth and power in the hands of a minority who are in turn controlled... Which makes it easy to relax. To play a game such as this. To take part in a strange and terrible conflict. To smash the conspiracy, tear away the mask, reveal and destroy the hidden forces manipulating those other Earths. Welcome to the Multiverse.

title: The Most Dangerous Game
description: From the back cover: "The Most Dangerous Game contains nine scenarios of adventure and adversity for the TRAUMA roleplaying game, written by some of the best new authors from Australia and the USA. All nine scenarios are set in the modern world - a familiar world turned hostile. Thugs, cocaine dealers, vigilantes, fugitives, heroin pushers, rebels, syndicates, corrupt officials, psychopaths, hunters - all of them can be found here. TRAUMA is a roleplaying game of contemporary adventure and suspense. Players are encouraged to play characters much like themselves, caught in extraordinary situations of heroism and adversity. TRAUMA scenarios are build out of real world situations we all hope to avoid: conspiracies, hijackings, drug wars, psychotic reactions - terrors we usually experience second hand. The TRAUMA roleplaying game, and supplements, are available in English from Darcsyde Productions."

title: Veil of Flesh
description: From the back of this 64-page adventure: "Strange goings-on are typical in Washington, D.C., but when dehydrated, ancient-looking corpses are found in an alley near Capitol Hill, not even the F.B.I. is willing to get involved. Enter your characters. Four to six of you must investigate these "murders" and venture from the Library of Congress through Washington Park, all the the way to The White House. This horrifying scenario features a new Creature of the Unknown, based on actual creatures of legend. Six pregenerated characters and maps of the D.C. area and of the White House are included."

title: 1992 TSR Product Catalogue
description: Annual catalog sent to game retailers. Promotes TSR's product line that year, profusely illustrated with descriptions of the various games and game aids.

title: Dämmerung im Purpurkreis
description: The Velvet Circle: a scenario in seven chapters, which takes many sessions of play to conclude. The adventure includes a complete red-light district, with business notes and a plan of the Circle. The adventure is out of the Demon Magic: The Second Stormbringer Companion. The German version is a stand alone product. Because Demon Magic: The Second Stormbringer Companion is included in the German stormbringer boxed set. From the Back of the Book: "Dämmerung im Purpurkreis": Mit diesen geheimnisvollen Worten enden jene mysteriösen Träume¸ deren Lockruf die Charaktere unwiderstehlich in die Stadt Ilmar geführt hat. Hier harrt ein aufregendes Abenteuer für das Fantasy Rollenspiel Michael Moorcock´s Sturmbringer auf sie¸ das die Gefährten auf eine gefahrvolle Reise entsendet. Beginn in Ilmar * Worin die Abenteuer ihren Träumen folgen und so in den Genuß einer mysteriösen Einladung gelangen... Ein Treffen und seine Folgen * Worin die Abenteurer in den Wald von Troos reisen¸ dort einen seltsamen Besucher aus einer anderen Dimension begegnen und sich den Schrecken der Gruft stellen. Ein ereignisreicher Weg * Worin die Abenteuer nach Fakash reisen¸ eine junge Frau retten und eine fremdartige Prophezeiung zu hören bekommen. Geheimnisse in Fakash * Worin die Abenteurer in der Stadt Fakash anlangen und dort auf ein Geheimnis stoßen Eine Oase in der Wüste * Worin die Abenteurer auf den Messinghund und dessen Gäste treffen Eine Höhle¸ ein Wächter und ein Schatz * Worin die Abenteurer den Gegenstand ihrer Suche finden¸ ebenso wie den Wächter¸ der zu seiner Bewachung zurückgelassen wurde. Anhang: Freuden und Gefahren des Purpurkreises * Worin das Vergnügungsviertel Ilmars ausführlich beschrieben wird. Tavernen¸ Bordelle¸ Gasthäuser¸ Kasinos und andere Etablissements dieses Stadtteils werden detailliert vorgestellt und ihre Preise und Dienstleistungen aufgeführt.

title: Sun and Storm: The Enchiridion
description: From the back cover: "The Dark Fantasy Role-Playing Game "Take the role of a gritty, stoic hero driven to survive in the face of overwhelming odds and a ruthless, relentless foe. "In A World Of The Living And The Dead... "The Enchiridion is an indispensable survivor's guide. It supplies the history of a war-torn world and step-by-step instructions for creating heroic characters. "It contains in-depth descriptions of skills, special abilities, armour, weapons and other equipment. "It provides rules for playing the game, using magic, engaging in combat and more."

title: Monster Maker RPG Holy Axe
description: The core rule book for the first Monster Maker role playing game.

title: Caves of Fury
description: From the back of the book You, o fearless warrior, must employ your mighty strength and supreme battle skill to fight the evil monsters that lurk in the Caves of Fury, and claim the wondrous treasures that they guard.

title: Festival des Totes (1992)
description: Author's blurb: "Citizens of Niederhofen! I hereby proclaim Baron Johan von Ritterm..." The ostentatious herald's announcement is drowned out as a wagon rolls through the village center with a heavy rumble. "...von Rittermann's view that at the autumn eve of Sigmar's year, 2515, a special ceremony will be held due to a pleasing visit by the honorable Panterridder..." Another interruption as a blacksmith begins rhythmically pounding on the anvil - a soldier glares menacingly and he stops. "...Panterridderen, Sir Leonius Altheim von Arzig-Münicher, with consequence!" It was intended as an impressive crescendo of an announcement. A cold autumn wind blows across the black fields. User summary: The scenario is distributed as three PDF files: * 28-page scenario * 7-page maps and art (print-and-play) * 14-page characters (print-and-play)

title: Path of the Crusaders
description: User summary. The reader is a journalist. The latest assignment takes you to follow the path of the crusades. While on your assignment you discover one of the members of your group may be a terrorist.

title: A Death in the Family
description: Author's blurb: Utah, Salt Lake Desert, June 1992: Six young people are severely injured while searching among the wrecked remnants of a Desert Mountain. In all likelihood, magma in the interior of the mountain caused a giant explosion. They are hospitalized with memory loss, at the prestigious Whitedale Recreational Facility. Six months later they're still there - still with memory loss, but otherwise healthy and healthy. Kolding, Denmark, Summer 1993: Six tired young people - Cathy, Bobby, Alex, "Bananas", Lee and Niels Bo - are taking a break in peaceful, sunny Denmark at Niels Bo's family house, the Christensen family. Mrs. Christensen is delighted with her American guests and dishes up mountains of homemade meatballs. The pretty little townhouse is full of Danish spirit and the rich smell of brown sauce. It's the time of music festivals, and rock and roll fills Denmark. It's the time of long summer holidays and people stroll the beaches. The sun shines mildly from an unusual blue sky; even school teachers are longing for the beaches, and the beer has never been colder or cheaper. Life couldn't be better for our six ragged friends...

title: Sweet Wall of Death
description: This module is for 4-6 player characters of levels 2-4. From the back cover: "The figure mounting the top of the hill wears the unmistakable colors of the town watch, but they are in some way distorted, covered with a thick amber-hued substance that reflects the late afternoon sun. The robbery of the temple this morning had sent him and two other guards in pursuit of the perpetrators. Now, the lone guard staggers once and tumbles down the hill, the amber substance adhering leaves and twigs to his exhausted form. When he finally ends his violent descent at your feet, you see on his skin, partially obscured by debris and completely coated with the sticky substance, several large ripe welts, bright red in color. At this close distance, the substance caries a familiar scent - that sugary smell of honey. His last words are a whispered response to the unspoken question on your astonished faces; 'The Sweet Wall of Death.'"

title: McCarron's Armored Cavalry
description: From the back of the book: "Colonel Marcus Barton commands the Second Regiment of McCarron's Armored Cavalry, also known as Barton's Regiment, and he is a man obsessed. He has waited forty years to avenge his father's dishonor at the hands of Lester J. Otto. He blew his first chance, but he swears he will not make another mistake. He knows he has a second chance on Marlette, and Marcus Barton will have his revenge this time or die trying. And either way he plans to take his regiment with him... McCarron's Armored Cavalry is a BattleTech scenario pack which includes the unit's history, prominent members, and special equipment. The scenarios chronicle the campaign known as "Otto's Last Stand," and can be used individually or as a campaign culminating in one of three different endings. "Otto's Last Stand" features deadly installations and a point-based system for customizing the defending forces, so that scenarios can be fought more than once and never be the same game twice."

title: Earthquake!
description: From publisher blurb: You're visiting San Francisco when suddenly a strange rumbling fills the air and buildings begin to shake-it's a major earthquake! You look around and see an old man pinned to the sidewalk by rubble. You want to help him, but a friend of yours may be in even bigger trouble nearby. Every second counts. What are you going to do?

title: Monsters, Magic & Sorcery II
description: From the back cover: Quest of the Ancients expands with MMS II. Monsters of all sorts await the adventerous, from the Evil lantern shade, who seeks its lost treasure, to the three-headed bronze dragonoid which stands guard over an Arwinian fortune. Magic items aplenty are also found within, from the Ebon Shawl of Alison Gross, to the Horn of the Huntsman. So, whether you're looking for new, challenging dungeon denizens, or unique, enchanted treasures, you'll find what you need in Monsters, Magic & Sorcery II.

title: Identification Friend or Foe
description: From Introduction: A long-distance caller hires a BlackEagle cell to protect an unknowing teenager from a possible death threat. The teen’s father’s work for the Colombian military makes him a target for retaliation by FARC, the Colombian insurgency. The threat is real, but FARC’s not the only one gunning for the kid. The identity of the mysterious client may hold the key to unraveling this complex, fast-paced assignment. This adventure originally appeared in White Wolf magazine in 1992.

title: Insh Allah
description: Insh Allah explains the role of Islam in the INS/MV alternate earth. The book contains details about the history behind Islam, its place in the Great Game between god and lucifer, how to create islamic angels and demons, three islamic Archangels and three islamic Demon Princes, and three scenarios, "Les carottes sont que huit" for angels or demons, "F.I.S. de pute" also for angels or demons and "L'envol", only for angels.

title: The Quest for Laughter
description: This is a collection of scenarios for the Dragon Half RPG from Fujimi shobo. Included scenarios are: Chapter 1: The captive princess (wife and ghost at once) in te old castle Chapter 2: The infamous delivery service Chapter 3: The war record of the Dragon Cupboard

title: Marathom I: Gestrandet im Sog der Zeit
description: user summary This is a german fantasy universal adventure for all "known Rolelayingsystems" (tested with D&D, AD&D, DSA, MERS and Palladium). The adventure was designed for 4-6 characters. The adventures takes the player through the time of one dessert city to search for an old artifact.

title: Spor der Skræmmer!, eller, Træls Bod
description: Author's blurb: Keep hold of the sword. Stay calm, there is nothing to be afraid of. Keep your cool. Feel forward with your feet, step by step. Stay calm. Nothing to fear. It doesn't know you're here. Recite the Oath of Tyr in your mind. That's all that matters. Keep on sneaking. There... there's the sound of the dragon... The story begins on one fall day a little over a thousand years ago. User summary: The scenario is published as three files: 17-page PDF scenario 7-page PDF pre-generated characters 7-page PDF GM screen and summary

title: Master of Martial Arts
description: From publisher blurb: You and your best friend, Billy, have traveled the world together, sharpening your martial-arts skills. So when Billy mysteriously disappears one night, you don your black belt and set out to find him. Your search takes you to the exotic city of Bangkok, where you suddenly find yourself surrounded by a band of Thai fighters. Across the street, a strange woman beckons to you frantically. Is she inviting you to safety or a swift, horrible death? The fighters are closing in. What will you do?

title: Bloodlust Écran
description: The gamemaster's screen contains a 4 panel screen and a stapled 8 pages book with a scenario (a murder investigation in the city of Inaccessability).

title: Im Reich der Phantasie
description: Im Reich der Phantasie was a sized down version of the Midgard core rules designed for beginners and newcomers to the Midgard world. It contained two books, Buch der Regeln (Book of Rules) and Buch der Abenteuer (Book of Adventures). The Book of Adventures contained three adventures: Unter den Nebelbergen (Under the Misty Mountains) Über den Perellion-Paß (Over the Perellion Pass) Die Insel des Widdergottes (The Island of the God Ram) The rules of this boxed set included only four basic character classes, the warrior, the adventurer, the priest and the mage up to level 5.

title: OPIGS: One Page GamIng System
description: The main rules for OPIGS, the One Page GamIng System, written by David Morgan-Mar in 1987, first published on the newsgroup rec.games.frp.misc in 1992, and later made available via the author's web site. Contrary to what the name suggests, the rules span one double-sided sheet, rather than a single page, when printed at a normal size.

title: Alienoïds
description: The world of Alienoïds is the same we live in, with a small difference: aliens have been on earth since 1938, plotting, infiltrating governments and preparing for their final goal, a planetary armageddon which will allow them to harvest the entire population of the planet and fill the freezers of their living mothership with tasty carbon-based lifeforms. The players' characters are alienoïds, extraterrestrial infiltrators tasked with protecting the alien master plan against the human resistance and other alien species. They'll have to adapt to and understand human culture and technology (which don't make any sense to a galactic predator using biomechanical technology), and manage the complexities of human emotions (the longer they are exposed to humans, the harder it is for their empathic brains to avoid being tainted by feelings and heretical thoughts like compassion or fear). The game system uses several d6: actions are resolved by rolling a number of dice equal to the sum of a stat and a skill, each result of 5 or 6 is a success. The number of successes allows the gamemaster to quantify the result. There are also rules for playing diceless. The book includes the rules system, a detailed chronology of the world since 1938 (the year of the alienoïds' first contact with earth authorities), details about the alien master plan and those who oppose it, a huge list of biomechanic gear (from weapons like blasters and flamethrowers - which need to be fed regularly and fart a lot, to spaceships and backpacks), and a seven scenarios campaign, starting with the characters' first assignment on earth and ending with the characters, now overwhelmed by human feelings and emotions joining the Resistance and taking the fight to their own specie.

title: Gladestrom
description: From the back cover: "You were absolutely certain that the priceless painting you found on the cart was an original one-of-a-kind piece -- and so was the one just like it. Investigating this matter led you to the giant vineyard and a meeting with an art dealer, whose assistant looks astonishingly like one of the brigands you killed earlier on the way here. Now you've discovered the dead body under the floorboards of the meeting room, and it's the same man you just spoke to five minutes ago in the lobby. What's going on? And why is your companion, the man who paid you to investigate all this, drawing his sword on you and grinning wickedly?"

title: L'Empire Ténébreux
description: From the back of the book: Ce supplément au jeu Hawkmoon décrit à l’intention du maître de jeu l’empire granbreton. Il découvrira dans ces pages son histoire et celle de ses conquêtes, une analyse complète de la science granbretonne et ses applications les plus importantes, une vue détaillée de Londra et bien sûr comment créer un personnage issu de ces terres perverses. De plus, la totalité de la Granbretanne a été détaillée, les terres, ses habitants, leurs cultures, les mutants et bien sûr plusieurs scénarios vous permettront de confronter vos joueurs à l’empire de l’épouvante.

title: The Planet Eater
description: From the Back of the Book As a Space Hawk, you've gone up against some of the most deadly enemies in the solar system. But you've never faced an opponent as awesome as Nemesis. This all-powerful alien, with its 400,000-mile-long spaceship, devours any planet in its path--and Earth is next. It's a challenge only the Space Hawks can meet--or can they? Unless the Space Hawks can overcome Nemesis, Earth is doomed!

title: The Abandoned Gold Mine
description: Publisher blurb The Ringers make a frightening discovery while exploring an old mine shaft. Will they run away, or risk their lives? It's up to you!

title: L'Esodo su Terminus
description: L'Esodo su Terminus The Exodus to Terminus First volume of the Galactic Foundation Games, a collection of Italian gamebooks set in the Foundation trilogy by Isaac Asimov. The famous Sci-fi writer is credited as the author, but actually they have been written by Leonardo Felician who is credited as "testi a cura di" ("text editor"). From the back Cover Would you kill Hari Seldon with your own hands? Would you break Salvor Hadin career? How would you organize the transport of 100,000 people on a planet on the edge of the galaxy?

title: GAME: Greatly Abbreviated Mechanics, Etc.
description: User Summary The idea of GAME is to provide an ultra-compact, flexible system that can be fit onto a single 8.5" x 11" piece of paper, front and back. GAME serves only as the barest guideline for roleplaying and leaves all details to the GM's judgement, and is meant to ease on-the-fly additions.

title: Périple en Amodan
description: The third Anashiva Reahna describes the Amodan continent, under attack by the Ney-Dora empire. The book contains a description of Amodanii history and geopgraphy, economy and politics, chapters about the capital city of Rupellaem, Amodanii religions, Amodanii legends and mysterious places, an Amodanii lexicon and 2 scenarios. The book comes with a 40x30cm map of Amodan.

title: Berliner Nacht
description: "Berliner Nacht" is a scenario book for Berlin XVIII second edition. There are seven scenarios: - "La Nuit des gladiateurs" is about clandestine fighting competitions; - "Le Falkampft sifflera trois fois" is set in England (with heavy Mad Max overtones); - "Le Vengeur fou" has the players try to protect a mafia boss from a vigilante; - "Le Jour où Berlin sauta (ou pas ?)" is about preventing a terrorist attack; - "Meurtres dans les marches" send the players on an investigation on the eastern front; - "Le Congrès" is about an international police convention in America, where things don't go quite as planned; - "le Caissier voleur, sa femme et son amant" is half standard investigation, half vaudeville.

title: Il Toro sul Nilo
description: Second in the Storiagame collection of gamebooks, summer gifts of the "Storia e dossier" monthly magazines. This one is set in ancient Egypt.

title: Stalking the Steel City
description: A supplement for the Bureau 13: Stalking the Night Fantastic roleplaying game. 96 pages with over 200 illustrations, 5 complete adventures, 2 supernatural places of interest, 6 Bureau 13 Agency Information Files, and 16 new special equipment and weapons. The rest of the text on the back of the book reads as follows: Pittsburgh: Beautiful, modern, nestled in the foothills of the Allegheny Mountains, it has been called the Most Livable City in America. But no one takes this big city that acts like a small town seriously. That is about to change! Evil has come to the Steel City It's secret places are filled to bursting There is no place to hide

title: Mediterraneo (1st Edition)
description: "Mediterraneo" - First Edition Mediterraneo is a Role Playing Game set in Greek/Roman Myth. Players assume the roles of heroes of the classic age and have to face monsters, perils, legends, and capricious deities. The game system is based on the difference between two 6-sided dice: characteristics range from -5 to +5 and are at the same time the bonus/malus used in checks. The system is taken from the first iItalian gamebook "In Cerca di Fortuna" and used here for the fitrst time for a RPG. It will be later klnown as "Qui comincia l'avventura". Mediterraneo has been used not only by regular players looking for something different, but also as an introduction to RPG’s in schools and libraries for kids as young as 7 or 8 years old. But first of all, Mediterraneo has been conceived as a game for players, not for educational use. Anyway, "Mediterraneo" and its spin-off "Orlando Furioso" have been a successful tool for teachers and librarians, and they helped to silence the very few that tried to attract the attention of media upon themselves importing foreign criticisms about supposed dangers of RPGs. The rulebook appeared as a bonus booklet attached to issue 5 of the bi-monthly Italian game magazine E Giochi, in January 1992. It includes a general introduction to RPG’s, rules, a Character Sheet, a complete adventure and a monster selection to help the Game Master to write further adventures. The introductory one, "L'isola delle amazzoni" ("The Amazon's Island"), has a lightly humorous tone: It is a simple rescue of a magic item on a small Greek island with all the ingredients of the classic myth, from syrenes to cyclopes, from a forbidden city to an oracle and so on.

title: Der Ketzer
description: User summaryDer Ketzer (The Heretic) is a German generic fantasy adventure and sourcebook. The book describes the city of Gâdiz di Héo in detail, which is lightly based on real-world Venice. The sourcebook part is 84 pages long. The 28-page adventure throws the characters right into a coup of the Sun Warriors that seize the power in Gâdiz di Héo. There are several possible ways to solve the adventure: in a diplomatic way, with open warfare or with an undercover operation.

title: Krim
description: From back of the book: Krim is a module for you who want to play in the black sector - on the other side of the law in the hard future of Mutant. New occupations makes it possible to play Yakuza or hitman, Tatsu or courier. The criminal syndicates are described, the black corporations and the terror organizations.

title: Harbinger of Darkness
description: An adventure module for 4-6 characters of levels 7-9. From the back cover: "A cold, foul wind blows through the city of Dorona. The rats lift their noses from the dirty streets in anticipation, for they know the smell of Death when it comes to town. But this wind is human and it bears a name - the name of Shadar Al-Meryk. Shadar Al-Meryk is a name few know, and fewer still speak aloud. Shadar Al-Meryk, the necromantic wizard. Shadar Al-Meryk, the midnight robber of graves. Shadar Al-Meryk, the worshiper of dark and ancient Gods. He has come to the city of Dorona this storm-tossed evvening for a reason. He is searching for something. Something that is not his, but will soon be."

title: Magic Master
description: From the Back of the Book: Will you discover the amazing secrets of the Magic Master? You've been a longtime fan of the Magic Master, whose spellbinding feats of magic have astounded audiences the world over. When the famous magician not only comes to your hometown to perform but actually moves there, you decide to visit his house, hoping to discover the secret of his powers. When you arrive there's no one home, but the door is unlocked. You are tempted to go inside and take a look around. What will you do?

title: Skilletan
description: A city sourcebook for Fifth Cycle. Skilletan is described as a "third cycle city" that "survives on a constant supply of excavated artifacts." Includes a players' section with general overview and GM's section with secrets. There are 23 individual NPCs and 26 generic ones, 107 locations, a foldout map. Two new professions and nine new skills, plus adventure seeds.

title: Highlander: The Gathering
description: author's blurb: Highlander: The Gathering, often referred to as H:tG, is a net.supplement which incorporates Highlander-style Immortals into White Wolf's World of Darkness. It was first released by Hank Driskill in September 1993, and a Second Edition, developed by John Gavigan in conjunction with Hank, was published in June 1994. It is generally acknowledged as the best and most successful net.supplement ever produced, having been downloaded by thousands of users, and even translated into French! The internet roleplaying community now generally accepts Immortals as a part of the World of Darkness, even if White Wolf does not. At the moment, we are working on a Third Edition of the rules, which will update the rules to reflect new developments in both Highlander and the World of Darkness, and will add a wealth of new material which, we hope, will add greatly to the playability of Immortals.

title: Nathan Never Cyberpunk
description: Initially designed as "Il gioco di ruolo di Nathan Never" ("Nathan Never's RPG") by an author who had bought in advance the license rights for this famous Italian comic character - he will also design a videogame with the same setting. By decision of the publisher, this book was finally edited without an original system and became a supplement for the Cyberpunk 2020 system. Nathan Never is an agent of the Agenzia Alfa, a character created by Medda, Serra e Vigna, and published by the same Bonelli that brought to success Dylan Dog - whose RPG proved very successful in 1991. The supplement gives 4 classes: Alfa Agent, Alfa Technician, Exoskeleton Pilot and Interplanetary Pilot. The sourcebook ov background and vehicles is completed by two adventures: "L'esame" ("The exam") and "Guerra alla Akuza" ("War to Akuza").

title: Angel/Demon: The Rapture
description: From the Game's Introduction: Welcome to Angel/Demon: The Rapture. A public domain supplement to the Storyteller system. After being inspired by the professional quality of the public domain supplement Highlander for the same system, I decided to modify a game I had developed for my own Reflex system Angel/Demon to the Storyteller format. Thus we have Angel/Demon: The Rapture. I hope in doing this I have make a useful addition to the World of Darkness, that can be enjoyed by all roleplayers. For I believe this game will add a new dimension to any World of Darkness game that you might be playing. Be it Vampire, Werewolf, Mage, etc. What exactly is Angel/Demon: The Rapture. and how does it interface with the rest of the World of Darkness? Well, Angel/Demon chronicles the battle that exists between the first born of god, Angels, and the minions of Lucifer, Demons. Or as they know themselves: The Cherubim and the Belial. The mythology surrounding these beings is probably known to most of you, being of Judo-Christian backgrounds, and no matter what your feelings about their true existence we must all understand that in the World of Darkness they are very, very real. These are the final days. The end of the world is near, and soon all of virtue will be taken up in the Rapture. But Satan's power has grown strong, and his minions, the Belial, move upon the surface of Eden. Corrupting, twisting, defiling. But taking physical forms of their own, these beasts must take the bodies of humans if they are to continue their evil works. Possessing the body, suppressing the mind, corrupting all those around it. Leaping from body to body they continue their destruction, bring more and more mortals one step closer to eternal damnation. But this evil does not go unchecked, for God also has his agents working amongst his children. These creatures, The Cherubim, move through humanity by the same method as their enemy. From body to body, person to person. There mission, though, is not the anti-thesis of the Belial. They are not sent to earth to save humanity, that is not within their power. They are upon the surface of Eden in a desperate holding action against the Belial. They are buying time until the Judgment day. Facing and defeating the Belial where every they encounter them. It is to this desperate battle for time that the two sides raise their forces. Will the Cherubim drive back the Belial and allow the final days to came, or will the Belial swallow up the souls of all the mortals and leave no one to be taken up in the Rapture? Only the players of Angel/Demon: The Rapture. will know for sure.

title: Tokyo Nova The 2nd Edition
description: This is the core rulebook for the second edition of the Japanese RPG Tokyo NOVA. Rules have been revised, and specifically the character creation system was overhauled and made faster. The RPG uses tarot cards as a randomizer and for character creation.

title: Riddle of the Runes: The Worlds of TSR
description: From the back of the box: More than a Screen Saver, an Interactive Puzzle! Worlds of TSR introduces you to the incredible realms created by the masters of fantasy. These stunning art panels include a built-in puzzle in the form of a treasure hunt. It seems a crafty fiend made off with the treasure of a god and left clues to its whereabouts scattered across the Advanced Dungeons & Dragons worlds. Test your intellect as you scan for hints to the final resting place of the richest treasure of the known realms. Features: - Built-in puzzle makes this more than a screen saver, but an interactive adventure! - Three modes to choose from: The Seer - Randomly displays the fantasy artwork, The Adventurer - Plan your own quest, and The Riddlemaster - Gather clues to the treasure hunt! - Over 200 masterpieces from the archives of TSR digitally recreated to display the wonders of the fantasy realms! - Original characters and worlds taken from the classic AD&D campaigns included: Forgotten Realms, Dark Sun, Ravenloft, and Planescape. - Eight haunting musical scores that match the style of each campaign setting.

title: Nausicaä: Il gioco di ruolo
description: Attached to issue 32 of the Kaos magazine (December 1995) as a separate booklet, this supplement for the SimulacreS RPG has actually all is needed to play, including the core rules of the system. The setting is the Nausicaä Anime/manga, by Miyazaki. Nexus Editrice, who published Kaos, was specialized in Japanese official licenses: had already published the successful Il gioco di ruolo di Ken il Guerriero, also based on SimulacreS, and will later publish Il gioco di ruolo di Dragonball/Dragonball Z, together with two Dragonball boardgames.

title: Cosmic Enforcers
description: From the back of the book: "A superpowered science fiction role playing game Myrmidon Press puts you in control of techno-powered super heroes and villains in a hard core science fiction future of the year 2025. Earth Central sits at the head of the table of the Galactic Alliance, a coalition of the Milky Way's seven known races. After years of warfare, peace finally reigns, but not for long... Dark forces have gathered their power waiting for the perfect time to strike down the alliance and restore chaos to the universe. Now, only one barrier stands in the way of their obsession with total anarchy. They are known as the Cosmic Enforcers, the Galactic Alliance's last bastion of defense, and the only hope to save all that is known from the brink of madness. Game highlights include: Easy high powered character generation Fast and playable combat system Many types of super-powered Cosmic Enforcers to choose from Progressive power advancement Total control of magic and psionics Over 291 super powers Plenty of robots, cybergadgets, power armour, and weapons Insane villains, noble heroes, and insidious aliens guarantee many hours of exciting adventure

title: Storyboard
description: From the Back Cover: Storyboard offers simple yet versatile role-playing. Describe your character in words not numbers. It is compatible with any story or game genre you can imagine. It requires pencil, paper, dice (optional) and a very active imagination.

title: Giant Psychic Insects from Outer Space
description: The Player Characters are victims of an alien abduction. Their abductors crash and the PCs find themselves in a strange rural area populated by twisted, bizarre creatures (we call them locals). Odd things start happening after the crash and a logging operation appears overnight. A bunch of doctors decide that an epidemic is breaking out and quarantine the area. The local diner starts serving sushi and a lot of people are wearing sunglasses, two sure signs of the Apocalypse. Victims of the dreaded disease are taken away for treatment... special treatment. Trucks, and hearses, roll into Erie, Pennsylvania taking unusual cargo from the crash site to the NERDCo facility. Strange things start happening in the area around the labs and none other than Rudolfo (infamous smut television talk-show host, fashion plate, and brawl-magnet) decides to do a series of shows from Erie because the town is apparently experiencing poltergeistic phenomena. At that point things start getting really weird...

title: The Complete Starter Set
description: From the Back of the Box: Picture a fantastic world filled with monsters, treaures, and daring deeds waiting to be done. Imagine crumbling stone towers and mysterious underground dungeons waiting to be explored and plundered. Imagine yourself as a mighty hero, a cunning wizard, or a stealthy thief, facing every danger with your sword or your magic. The ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGON game is the best-selling fantasy role-playing game ever published. In it, two to seven players control the actions of imaginary warriors, wizards, and other heroes in battles against fierce monsters for gold and glory. This box contains everything you need to play ADVANCED DUNGEONS & DRAGONS: - The Player's Handbook outlines the basic rules, expanded rules, and magic spells. - The DUNGEON MASTER Guide provides special information for the game master, and three thrilling adventures; - The MONSTROUS MANUAL is filled with ferocious monsters and rich treasures; - A full-color dungeon map gets your first adventures started right away; - Six hero cards give complete details on the game characters; - A stand-up screen contains the information the Dungeon Master needs to run the game; - Six plastic figures represent the players' characters; - Seven different types of dice control the action; - The Book of Lairs provides enough adventures for hundreds of hours of play!

title: The Complete Guide to Unlawful Carnal Knowledge
description: From the introduction: This guide should not be used by anyone that the GM feels is either to young, to immature, or not a good enough role-player to handle the topics presented here. User summary: Often considered a satire, the contents of this netbook widely were ridiculed, railed against, or mocked on various pre-World Wide Web computer systems. Nevertheless, it remained the "number one" download on almost every system that hosted it. The "final" version, dated Jan 1, 1996 (which came in two versions with varying content and format) included a summary detailing the controversy. Entirely devoid of graphics, the document today seems rather silly and benign.

title: Kalèvala
description: Kalèvala A RPG set in the Finnish myth, inspired by the epic poem by Elias Lönnrot with the same name. While the first Italian edition has the small format of all the "I Giochi del Duemila" collection, the On-A-Stik English edition has been enlarged and deepened. First agile edition, 14 x 10 cm, 32 two-colors pages, #15 in the "I Giochi del Duemila" collection. The small booklet includes "Le trame di Lemmi Vaakelainen" ("Lemmi Vaakelainen's plots"), a complete introductory adventure.

title: Blood Dawn
description: " "Blood Dawn is a role-playing game designed by Lawrence R. Sims and first published in 1996. The game's tagline is Roleplaying in Earth's Second Dark Age. The Blood Dawn license was later sold by Optimus Design Systems (ODS) to SSDC, Inc. in September 1999. Blood Dawn is set in an alternate 21st century timeline, on Earth. Characters can play "normal" humans or mutants."" Source: Wikipedia, "Blood_Dawn," available under the CC-BY-SA License.

title: The Guide to Herbs for RPGs
description: From the introduction: Herbs have been a staple of fantasy literature for many years. Striders use of Athelas in “The Lord of the Rings” to cure Frodo, and Polgara’s herb lore in “The Belgariad” and “The Mallorean”, and Raistlin’s use of herbal teas to calm his cough in “Dragonlance” are [three] examples that come to mind. ... This guide was written in order to allow a more detailed method of using the Herbalism Non Weapon Proficiency than that provided in the Player's Handbook (AD&D 2e). It may be used only by characters with the Herbalism proficiency. A revised Herbalism proficiency which can be used in any FRPG [also is provided]. User summary: The document went through three major editions (3rd/4th/5th); the presumed 1st and 2nd Editions were not publicly circulated; the 3rd Edition was not widely circulated. While some game stats are provided, the sourcebook is easily used for any fantasy game. The document occasionally is encountered with the title "The AD&D Guide to Herbs".

title: The 23rd Letter
description: The time is now. Psychics exist, not many, but enough - enough to worry those in power, and interest those who seek power. Governments and Corporations fight over them. The Network provides an imperfect refuge for those that escape the clutches of those who would abuse them or their powers.Thousands are maimed and killed each year by the Corporations and Governments. Some do this for power, some for defence, but they all do it, - for the next war will be fought with the mind - and the victor will control the destiny of the human race.

title: Neon Genesis Evangelion RPG
description: Neon Genesis Evangelion RPG - final battle in New Tokyo 3 was one of the anime-based japanese light RPGs using the MAGIUS system. This is the core rulebook for that game.

title: Villains & Foes
description: From the back of the book: "The newest supplement for Cosmic Enforcers focuses on the criminals and miscreants of the Galaxy - from sinister villains to misguided heroes, from super-powered terrorists to insane murderers. Enemies Galore: Dozens of villains, organizations, and teams to throw against your heroic Enforcers. City of Sin: Chicago in the 21st century is owned by the criminals. Inside you will find important characters, the rulers of the Windy City, and their minions. Cybenetics [sic]: Limbs, weapons, and deadly Combat Chassis. New Weapons, Powers, Magic and Psionics: Useful information for any Enforcers Campaign, from lethal blasters to such powers as Cold Weather Manipulations and Pleasure and Pain Projection. Plus a new archetype: the Jacker-technologist, master hacker and electronic courier of the Information Age. Designed for use for Cosmic Enforcers, but usable by any Super-heroic or Science Fiction RPG."

title: The Taint: A Game of Unwilling Heroes
description: From the Introduction If you had an origin as a superhero, how would you know that you had gained a power? How would you know what that power was, or how far it could go? If superheroes really existed, what would the government do - and what would they do to each other? How would everyday people react? The World of the Taint The Taint seems to have always existed. It may be a natural part of the evolution of humanity. The Taint may be the result of an experiment, but the authors of the experiment are described variously as: 1) aliens, 2) devine beings meddling in human affairs, 3) mutated humans who have moved to the next step of natural with trans-human abilities, 4) the result of increasing contaminants in the environment, and/or 5) a genetic technology developed by a secret society hundreds of years ago and applied as a control device on the general population. Some of these may be true. The world of the Taint is very similar to ours, with a few chilling exceptions. In this world there are traditions, rumors, myths, legends, lies, cover stories and warnings about people who aren’t really human — they are more than they seem. Some say those with the Taint are children of Cain, marked and cursed by the Almighty. Some think they are mutations who have no control over their trans-human abilities. Some people believe the Taint is a great Gift, bestowed on selected individuals to save humanity. Some people think they are aliens and should be destroyed as soon as they are discovered.

title: WitchCraft (1st Edition)
description: From the back of the book: A Game of Magic and Dark Secrets They are the Gifted. Feared for their unique powers, they have been hounded for centuries, and forced to practice their Arts in secret. The time for hiding is over. A time of Reckoning draws near. It marks the end of an era and the beginning of a new one - or the destruction of all things. the choices the Gifted make will determine what our future will be like. WitchCraft is a complete roleplaying game, with rules to created Gifted and Mundane characters, an overview of a modern world beset by supernatural danger, and the Unisystem, a set of game rules that can use dice, cards or be completely story-driven to fit the needs of your gaming group.

title: Unknown Providence: SAVE in New England
description: From the back of the book: "This 128-page sourcebook/scenario pack takes players to New England for four new adventures, and provides players and Chillmasters with background on the New England area, including the current operations of SAVE. Each scenario is designed to test the players' abilities in a different way, and at least one of the adventures is a part of the Chill story arc, a storyline spanning several years and having far reaching effects on the Chill universe. This is the first in a line of new products featuring this continuing plot line."

title: Coming of the Storm
description: Excerpt from the back of the book: Coming of the Storm is a collection of scenarios, backgrounds, hints for players and gamemasters and an original story based on the world of Dragon Storm. This adventure book includes a tri-fold gamemaster's screen, maps and a complete card list for the Dragon Storm collectible role-playing card game.

title: Shades of Earth
description: Shades of Earth is a world of where magic is the secret weapon and the grit of simple men and women make all the difference. Secret cabals and organization battle one another and the monsters they now know exist in a battle for who will control the world. It is not so clear who are the good or if someone is evil, only whether you trust them to fight by your side. In 1938, the world stands poised on the brink of war. Some rush towards it while others fight the rising tide. Which side do you want to be on? Department 12, the newest of the secret organizations, has its roots in the League of Nations. It is a joint operation between the British and the United States. They hope to curb the rising forces of the Gestapo while learnign as much as they can as fast as they can. The Gestapo work to increase the power of the Reich, gather arcane objects and collecting vast pools of spiritual energy...by any means necessary. The Guard of Babur maintain their holy vigil against the Thuggees. The Thuggees, worshipers of Kali though destroyed in the 1800s, have bided their time, waiting for their numbers to grow. They are now poised to move. The Neter, an Egyptian cult, are pledged to guard the Gate of Ptah, a portal to other worlds. There numbers have waned but their power still protects the Gate. The Mafia and the Catholic Church battle themselves as much as any other force. The Church maintains two organizations, one dedicated to the irradiation of magic while another dedicated to its research. Through all of it, the most ancient of them all, the Clan of the Dragon watch as kingdoms rise and fall, petty rulers implement their plans. The fight the Japanese in China but maintain a much larger view of history and the future. All of these groups pursue their own special agendas using agents and minions to do their will. Look into your heart and ask yourself, which side am I on?

title: Death from the Mist
description: A Mid-Level (4th-6th) Living Jungle Adventure In this adventure, the PC's discover that the Rudra tribe, a proud and somewhat insular tribe on the River Of Laughing Idols, has been raided by a never before seen species of humanoids, the Lacerials. These evil Lacerials, led by the evil lacerial mage Blackflame, attacked the village, injured many of its inhabitants, poisoned the chieftain, and kidnapped the chieftains daughter. The PC's must track the Lacerials into the dread Valley Of Spirits, rescue the daughter, recover an antidote to the poison, and return before their three day deadline elaspes.

title: Die Stadt, das Blut und der Tod: Frankfurt bei Nacht
description: From the Back Cover: Die Stadt... regiert von einem Prinzen, um den sich heftige Gerüchte ranken. Verwaltet von einem gespaltenen Rat der Erstgeborenen. ...das Blut... wird knapp in Frankfurt, denn Überbevölkerung bedroht das Unleben aller Kainskinder der Mainmetropole. Jede Nacht wird zur Existenzfrage. Überall herrschen Doppelzüngigkeit und Falschheit. ...und der Tod. Allgegenwärtig, drohend, letztlich unabwendbar. Er lauert hinter der Fassade glitzernder Bankhochhäuser. Er harrt der Kainiten in den Bürgerhäusern und Slums des Molochs Frankfurt. Und er weiß: Seine Zeit wird kommen. Frankfurt bei Nacht enthält: eine detaillierte Beschreibung der Rhein-Main-Region, ihrer Geschichte und Kultur mächtige Kainskinder, die hier, am Puls der deutschen Wirtschaft, ihre finsteren Pläne verfolgen, sowie Evas letzte Tochter, eine komplette, spielbereite Vampire-Geschichte, die mitten hineinführt ins dunkle Herz der Stadt. User Translation from German: The City... is ruled by a prince who wraps himself in violent rumours. Maintained by a divided Council of the firstborn. ...Blood... is becoming scarce in Frankfurt, because overpopulation threatens the unlife of all Kindred of the metropolis. Each night, the question of existence. Everywhere duplicity and falsehood prevail. ... and Death. Ubiquitous, menacing, ultimately inevitable. He lurks behind the facade of glittering bank skyscrapers. He waits for the Cainites in the town houses and slums of the Frankfurt Molochs. And he knows: his time will come. Frankfurt by Night includes: A detailed description of the Rhine-Main region, its history and culture Powerful Kindred who are pursuing here, on the pulse of the German economy, their sinister plans, and Eve's last daughter, a complete, ready to play vampire story that leads right into the dark heart of the city.

title: Realms of the Sun Book One
description: Includes Bright Fires, a quest by Nicole Lindroos and Realm Guides to Sun Mound and Highpole by Geoffrey C. Grabowski. From the back cover: Ripped from the gatepath during a spherewalk, the heroes must embark on a hazardous journey across the Great Desert and back, or a whole realm will die. Of course, no one has ever returned from across the Great Desert.... Includes a complete Everway quest and Realm Guides to two realms, Sun Mound and Highpole. Features new art by Janine Johnston and Matt harpold, plus a previously unreleased Spherewalker promo card by artist Doug Keith.

title: Providence: Main Rule Book
description: A well regarded RPG that mixes Superpowers and Fantasy elements in a player friendly game design with a rich campaign world. The publisher says "This book provides the complete rules system for the Providence role-playing game, providing players with everything they will need to start a game of Providence. Learn how to be a hero again. ".

title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park Role-Playing Game Book
description: From the Back of the Book: Welcome to The Lost World: Jurassic Park, where once-extinct dinosaurs now run wild! In this revolutionary evolutionary new game, you'll be catapulted into a dangerous reality based on the hit film! Go head to head with Jurassic and Cretaceous Era beasts in a bloody battle for survival! Roll the dice, choose your character cards, assume identities, and enter The Lost World. Will you hunt down the Velociraptor? Risk your life to save a baby T-rex? Or will your plans be sabotaged by the greedy InGen Corporation? Be careful! What you decide will either lead to your character being a hero... or lunch for a hungry T-rex!

title: Realms of the Sun Book Two
description: Realms of the Sun Book Two includes the quest Heart of Stone by Greg Stolze and the Realm Guide to Swift Plains by Geoffrey C. Grabowski. From the back cover: They say you can't cheat fate. Maybe that depends on who's on your side... In Heart of Stone, Spherewalker author Greg Stolze takes us on an epic quest in the realm of Swift Plains.

title: Pulp Dungeons: Uninvited Guests
description: A collection of three adventures written to be easily adapted to any fantasy RPG system. From the publisher: Jolly Jongleurs by Gary Gygax The Jolly Jongleurs are a fun-loving band of bards and entertainers who make their home in a clearing off to the side of Grimfens Low Road...or are they? What darker purpose could they and their brightly painted caravans conceal? The Brimming Stein by Lester Smith An old friend greets you as you enter the gates to the city and invites you to stay at his new inn - free of charge, of course. But what have you gotten yourself into? What could that menacing eye that stares at you in your dreams possibly mean? Finding out means the difference between life and death not only for you and your friends, but possibly the world! Baron Mikrut's Tomb by Bryan Winter Invading hordes gather near the ege of town and you and your companions are called upon to raid the resting place of Baron Mikrut in order to recover his legendary amulet. A simple enough request until you find out what is protecting the Baron and his family tomb!

title: Charlie Don't Surf
description: website blurb: 1969 Cthulhu scenario, 4-6 Investigators required: characters supplied. May 10th 1969, the A Shau Valley near Hue, it's been quiet... too quiet... the air is thick with the expectation of an attack from Charlie in response to operation Apache Snow. You and the remains of your squad from the 101st Airborne are out on deep recon, trying to get back to your base at Hill 937: 'Hamburger Hill'. You come across two abandoned vills. Scouting the area you find something that changes your world. Makes you think that Charlie may be the least of your worries...

title: The Book of Souls
description: The Book of Souls was the first Ravenloft Netbook published by the Kargatane, a group of Ravenloft fans who went on to run the official fan site for many years. The Book of Souls is a compendium of shorter Ravenloft and Masque of the Red Death articles covering the people, places and creatures of the setting, as well as a handful of new rules, and advice on running games in the Domains of Dread. From the front of the book: Fear In a Handful of Dust Welcome to the Book of Souls. Brought to you by fellow devotees of the dark lands of Ravenloft, this netbook is a gift to all who find themselves drawn into the Demiplane of Dread. In his poem The Wastelands, T.S. Eliot said, “I will show you fear in a handful of dust.” He was right, of course. Something as simple as a handful of dust can hold fear... and horror. Dust is the poison slipped into the wine of a politician’s rival. Dust is the ashes of the woman who immolates herself when her lover turns to an other. Dust is the remains of the vampire scattering on the winds. Dust is that which drifts from the cerements of the dead. Dust is soil without life promising famine. The worlds of Ravenloft are our handfuls of dust. Ever since the first handful the Kargat brought, we came and added our own dust. An island of dust, a continent, a world, two worlds built of the dusts that bear fear and horror. As the Demiplane of Dread and the Gothic Earth grow, we add more and more to our wasteland of dust. This is our handful of dust. Let us show you fear within it.

title: The Trouble With Derro
description: From the introduction: A Greyhawk Adventure with tie-ins to The Doomgrinder for the Adventurer's Guild Retail Play Program. User summary: This scenario uses resources from The Adventure Begins.

title: The Blood of Heroes
description: From the publisher "The Blood of Heroes is a superhero and supervillain roleplaying game that uses the exponential gaming system created by Mayfair Games (sometimes called MEGS). Designed for Mayfair's DC Heroes Roleplaying Game, the system is ideal for playing supers at any power level. Character creation is fast and easy. The point-based system will also help a team balance itself, so that no member overshadows the rest of the group. And, as a side-effect of the simplicity, the system is flexible enough to design nearly any character imaginable. Our first product is no longer available for sale and is out of print. It was well-supplemented by the Sidekick Sourcebook and both books were effectively replaced by The Blood of Heroes: Special Edition, still available everywhere."

title: The Player's Guide to Shadowrun House Rules
description: From the back of the book: The Player’s Guide To Shadowrun House Rules There are times when the basic rules of a game just don’t seem to cover a situation that comes up during a game. There are also times when you’re not satisfied with the way the existing rules handle particular circumstances. Or when you just want to experiment with something different. This book provides a large number of optional rules for Shadowrun Second Edition, devised by gamemasters and players for their own campaigns, but equally usable in others. GENERAL RULES BIOWARE CHARACTERS CYBERWARE KARMA MAGIC MATRIX MELEE COMBAT RANGED COMBAT VEHICLES This compilation copyright © 1998 by Gurth; individual house rules copyright © by their authors. All rights reserved Shadowrun® is a registered trademark of FASA Corporation Plastic Warriors 0007

title: GURPS Character Sheet
description: User Summary A java-based application for creating characters for GURPS 4th edition. Runs under on any system that supports Java 6. (slightly earlier incarnations only required Java 5) Earlier editions supported GURPS 3rd edition and were Macintosh based only.

title: Narcissist
description: This is the core book for the Narcissist: Crash Free roleplaying game. It recapitulates a subset of the rules from the C°ntinuum: Roleplaying in the Yet roleplaying game, but presents them from a different perspective in order to play the enemies of the Continuum. Table of contents: I. Introduction II. Glossary III. Game Mechanics Character Creation Skills Combat IV. Travelling Time & Crashing Free Span Frag Crash Time Combat Span & Yet Cards Time & Dreaming V. A Glimpse of the Multiverse 16,000 Years from Safety Yrne & the Royal Road The Castes of Yrne Mapping Infinity The Long Cold Drive up to Hell The Philosophies Crash Cards and Character Sheets

title: Swords of the Middle Kingdom
description: From the back of the book: IN AN AGE OF LEGEND AND A TIME OF TYRANNY THE LAND CRIED OUT FOR A HERO. WILL YOU ANSWER THE CALL? Explore the World of Wulin, defeat the minions of the evil Manchu, and restore the benevolent Jen Dynasty to power--all while satisfying your craving for kung fu carnage! Event Horizon Productions proudly presents the newest game in the slam-bang Cinemaction line. All of the outrageous, magic-wielding, wire fu action you love is here complete with a fully realized Chinese fantasy world built right in. So answer the call . . . Wulin needs you!

title: Abenteuer in Cormyr I
description: User summaryAbenteuer in Cormyr I is the translation of the three adventures from Four From Cormyr. The adventures is suitable for characters of levels 9 to 12. Included are: Der Grenzübertritt (The Border Crossing) Der Büchernarr (The Bibliophile) Unangenehme Nachbarn (Bad Neighbor Policy)

title: DragonQuest Ultimate Character Sheet (XLS)
description: Known by several names, including DQUCS, and DQ Ultimate Character Sheet, this MS Excel worksheet has multiple tabs ("Character Record", "Magic Record", "Combat Skills", "SKills", "Armor", and "More Tables") and formula that partially automate the character creation process for both DragonQuest and DQ Open Source.

title: Abenteuer in Cormyr II
description: User summaryAbenteuer in Cormyr II is the translation of two adventures from Four From Cormyr. The adventures is suitable for characters of levels 9 to 12. Included are: Ein höchst magischer Mord (Murder Most Magical) Erlösung (Redemption)

title: Demon City Shinjuku Role Playing Game and Resource Book
description: From the Back Cover: Tokyo. The turn of the millennium. The glittering skyline, which once rivaled any in the world, lies cracked and crumbling under the weight of an oppressive evil. One man, the tyrannical Levih Rah, has created a Demon City in Shinjuku, the heart of Tokyo. That's just the beginning. Rah's mission is to destroy the world by opening a gate to the Demon Void, which will turn Earth into a living Hell. In three days, the world as we know it will be no longer. Hope. For years, no one had dared to enter the city infested by Rah's evil minions - until now. Desperate to save the life of her captured father, the beautiful Sayaka Rama ventures into Shinjuku with the aid of Kyoya Izayoi, a Tokyo teenager skilled in the mysterious martial art of Nenpo. With assistance from a cynical and aged mystic, a streetwise youth, and an enigmatic spiritual healer, Kyoya and Sayaka might actually have a chance to defeat the dark sorcerer, Rah... This role-playing game is based on the popular feature-length 1988 Japanese animated action-adventure horror film, from the original work of Hideyuki Kikuchi. You can now join the fight against the dark powers of Levih Rah, or be a part of the demon hordes that terrorize Shinjuku. The game features the intuitive and easy-to-learn Tri-Stat System game mechanics, which is fully compatible with Guardians of Order's other anime role-playing games. Additionally, the book offers extensive resources and references and for Demon City Shinjuku fans (including an extensive movie summary and detailed character bios), and a full-color gallery of spectacular animation art. The ideal role-playing game for fans of Japanese anime, or occult-gothic horror.

title: Dragonball + Dragonball Z - Il gioco di ruolo
description: Dragonball + Dragonball Z: Il gioco di ruolo Summary courtesy of Andrea Angiolino, used with permission. The RPG of Dragonball is an Italian project of an easy RPG and two boardgames, that can be used independently or to enrich the RPG. The setting is the famous comics and cartoon world of Dragonball, by the Japanese artist Akira Toriyama. Martial arts play an important role in the game, that keeps the humorous style especially typical of the first part of the story. The boardgames have been distributes in Italian newspaper kiosks. At less than 10 euros each, both the full colour boardgames and the RPG have been a successful way to promote the hobby among non-players enthusiasts of Japanese cartoons, that are very popular in Italy since the state television started to broadcast them in 1978. A quite deep research work has been needed to make those games to depict the world of Dragonball and all its characters. This has been even more difficult since the Italian translations of comic books, TV serials and movies have been different, giving different names to places and people. Besides, the private channel Italia Uno that broadcasted the TV series at national level censored several scenes. The RPG has a very simple basic system that is explained in just two pages of rules and allows to play the first introductory adventure, "Le Sette Sfere". To help beginner players, 16 characters are pre-rolled. They stage the main characters of the cartoon and they can also be used as famous Non Player Charcters (NPCs). Each character has 4 main characteristics: Strength, Agility, Smartness and Charm. Then there is Defence, that measures the chance of avoiding dangerous things: a mix of dexterity, luck and bulk. And then there is Endurance, that is the capability to stand fatigue and pain: a mix of constitution and willpower. Every character has also a Flaw: Goku has a Out-of-control Hunger, Mr. Satan has Vanity, Olong has Fetishism, Lunch has Double Personality... So, as in the RPG "I cavalieri del tempio", sometimes the character does what he want, and not what the player wants! There is also a set of advanced rules, with the indications about how to generate new characters and where the martial arts are especially detailed. Anyway, players wanting more detail can solve combats using the boardgame "Dragonball Z - Il Torneo". A long section about the world of Dragonball, its creatures and the famous NPCs from the comics and movies complete the book. The boardgame "Dragonball - Alla ricerca delle sette sfere" provides more encounters and some magic / technological items that can be used by the Game Master to further enrich the adventures. The colourful board of the game is also useful as a reference map of the world.

title: Obsidian: Age of Judgment Quick Start Guide and Adventure
description: From Prelude: Welcome to the quick start guide for Obsidian: Age of Judgment created by the Apophis Consortium. We at Apophis Consortium would like to thank you for picking up this quick start and taking the time to read through it. This is an extremely streamlined version of rules to introduce you to the World of Obsidian. In section VI we have included a short adventure for you and your players to enjoy. Everything you need to run this game is included in this packet.

title: Alternity Bughunters Conversion ShoreCon Adventure
description: Adventure Outline This adventure is written for the Alternity game set in the Amazing Engine world of BUGHUNTERS by TSR, Inc. In this setting, players take on the role of space marines and play out sequences not unlike the films Aliens and Starship Troopers. They work for UTRPF, the United Terran Reconnaissance and Protection Force (see Handout 1). SETUP: UTRPF HQ receives word that the small colony outpost Konois 5 in the Beta Hydri system (5th planet) recently became victim of an electrical storm, which blew out their agrodome, and repairs are not going well. A backup engineering crew and lots of food supplies are being prepared to be shipped to the colony. A small marine crew is sent as escort and to lead the mission and assure compliance from the frantic colonists. The players take the parts of the pregenerated Marine force.

title: Into the Desert
description: A Three-Round Tournament From the introduction: Yesterday was the most exciting, the most frightening, and the most harrowing day in your short lives. In the morning, you were street waifs scrounging for food on the streets of the city of Jeffiah. Then you discovered that there was something strange about the boy Ala’i who had recently joined your band of abandoned and outcast children. He was sought by assassins – a price had been put on his head by someone in the palace.

title: The Shadow Falls (Sin City, Vol. 1)
description: Welcome to New Centennial City, or "Sin City" as the people who live here call it. New Centennial City is a new Hong Kong - a place of commerce, opportunity and dark secrets. It is a foreboding place, crouched somewhere in the shadows between Tim Burton's Gotham and the Los Angeles of Ridley Scott's Blade Runner. New Centennial City is a place where powerful men prey on the weak-minded with disastrous results. A series of violent crimes and a mysterious explosion, draw the players into the clutches of the Calendites, a modern-day Mayan cult led by the enigmatic Ethan Rayne. In trying to learn its secrets, the players are caught up in the frenzy of impending doom. The stage is set! The end of the world is nigh!

title: The Tired, Hungry, and Poor
description: A Three-Round Tournament From the introduction: In the Land of Fate there is a city named Jeffiah, which lies at the foot of the plateau that forms the High Desert. This beautiful city was built many hundreds of years ago around a sacred oasis which harbored a pool of golden water.

title: Battle Scarred Veterans Go Hiking
description: The PCs are normal people - well, slightly subnormal, though not as low as LARPers. The GM's job is to put sub-normal characters into stressful situations until the GM feels better about what the players did to him/her in their last D&D game. That should teach them to be rules lawyers! Simple rules, d100 system, much flexibility on the part of the GM is required.

title: Masks of Darkness (Sin City, Vol. 3)
description: 'Just what the hell is going on here?' Something is happening behind the scenes in New Centennial City. A gang war has erupted in the Projects and an influenza epidemic sweeps the city. Meanwhile, a man has gone missing and no one seems willing to help find him. The players get asked to help and inadvertently get pulled into a web of lies, deceit and confusion. Masks of Darkness introduces several new underground ernpathic cells that are active in New Centennial City. They, together with the players, will work together to uncover the truth that lurks behind the facade of everyday city life. If the players aren't careful, they could get more than they bargained for.

title: The Book of Secrets
description: The Book of Secrets was the seventh Ravenloft Netbook published by the Kargatane, a group of Ravenloft fans who went on to run the official fan site for many years. The Book of Secrets is a collection of articles about the people, places and monsters of the Ravenloft and Masque of the Red Death settings. It also contains some new rules, and articles on running games in those two settings. From the preface of the book: The first step towards vice is to shroud innocent actions in mystery, and whoever likes to conceal something sooner or later has reason to conceal it. - Jean-Jacques Rousseau Secrets by their very nature are an effectual contrivance of horror. Ever since Horace Walpole first penned The Castle of Otranto, the secret has been a cornerstone of the Gothic genre that has grown into the horror literature we know and love today. Secrets can be extremely dangerous, whether they remain hidden or are inopportunely revealed. It should be no surprise that the Demiplane of Dread is a land of many secrets. The Kargatane are pleased to present the third annual Ravenloft Netbook, The Book of Secrets. Herein lie the secrets of many talented Ravenloft® and Masque of the Red Death® fans. We have dutifully collected their secrets for several months, and now choose to reveal them to you on this darkest of nights. In the pages that follow, you will find many secrets of the Demiplane of Dread and Gothic Earth revealed. Now you must ask yourself, "are these secrets I keep, or secrets I reveal?" If you are truly fortunate, the Dark Powers and the Red Death will not take notice of your choice.

title: Ravaged Planet
description: From the back of the book: Ravaged Planet is the player's guide for Brave New World. It comes packed with all sorts of information to immerse players in this sharply realized setting. Besides all the vital details, there are 10 brand-new power packages for players to build new kinds of heroes with. Plus the book introduces one of the most power factions in Brave New World: The Covenant.

title: The Death Hand of Saint No-One
description: An official scenario for the Continuum roleplaying game published on their web site at http://www.aetherco.com/continuum/downloads.html. Designed for 4-8 players, plus a GM. From the web page: 1990s Brooklyn spanners encounter a little-known figure of the 10th Century who's misplaced a reliquary containing bits of himself. Antiques Roadshow ain't got nothing on this.

title: The Mind's Eye Theatre Introductory Kit
description: [Back cover] Beyond dice Beyond table-top Beyond traditional roleplaying Join the next generation of roleplaying entertainment -- Live-Action Roleplaying This FREE quick-start introductory kit gives you everthing you need to step into character and become one of the lords of the night. New York Times, Dateline, Newsweek, Spin -- everyone's talking about the phenomenon of live-action Vampire: The Masquerade. Try it yourself and see what roleplaying entertainment can be. To learn even more about live-action Vampire, check out the new revised edition of Laws of the Night for the complete rules of live-action roleplay. Laws of the Night contains all of the clans, all of the rules and a full introduction to the setting of Vampire: The Masquerade. Available in regular and deluxe editions, as for Laws of the Night wherever you picked up this quick-start.

title: The Brand X Hero's Guide
description: The Brand X Hero’s Guide The rest of the rules tell you how to create a superhero, what your numbers mean, and the rest of that shit. This book gives you some pointers on how to play the damn game. Consider this a primer on the physics, sociology, and politics of a world with super heroes. This is a world where anything can happen, and, sooner or later, everything does. Men & Supermen website

title: The Body Farm
description: From the front page: The Body Farm A Single Session Virtual Seattle Adventure By Mark Somers From the introduction: With a rather bemused experssion on her face she will explain that the nature of this job is somewhat distasteful, but she expects that the often-elastic morals of teh runners should be able to cope. The mission is to gain posession of the whole body of Dr Robert Carling, without anyone knowing that they have done so. The good doctor - a forensic examiner with the Seattle Coroner's Office - died two months ago. He was buried in Bellevue, in quite a nice marble edifice.

title: Guia de Itens Mágicos
description: From the back of the book: "Este Guia foi criado especialmente para Mestres Jogadores de qualquer sistema de Fantasia, Horror e Aventura, e contém uma das maiores coleções de Objetos Mágicos já vistos no mundo dos RPGs. Ao todo, os Guias trazem para você mais de 1.000 Itens Mágicos, entre Anéis, Poções, Óleos, Elixires, Varinhas, Cajados, Varas, Gemas, Pedras, Amuletos, Correntes, Braceletes, Botas, Roupas, Bolsas, Capas, Armaduras, Espadas, Escudos, Machados, Lanças, Varinhas, Cetros e uma infinidade de outros objetos místicos. Contém também dezenas de Artefatos, todos provenientes da mitologia grega, egípcia, babilônica, judaica, cristã islâmica, russa e outras culturas, incluindo a Cabeça da Medusa, a Arca da Aliança, o Martelo de Thor, Machado de Xangô, Pomo da Disórdia, o Santo Graal e Excalibur. Todos os Itens e Artefatos possuem detalhes, lendas, histórias engraçadas e variações a respeito deles, que podem ser utilizados por Mestres e Jogadores como pano de fundo para aventuras e campanhas. Tudo isso recheado com centenas de desenhos para ilustras os itens mais conhecidos, desta forma você pode saber exatamente que objeto seu personagem está carregando. O GUIA possui descrições de jogo de cada objeto, e regras simples que permitem adaptá-los para QUALQUER livro de RPG existente. google translation From the back of the book: "This guide was created especially for Masters players of any system of Fantasy, Horror and Adventure, and contains one of the largest collections of magical objects ever seen in the world of RPGs. In all, the Guides to bring you over 1,000 Magic Items, including Rings, Potions, Oils, Elixirs, Wands, Staves, Rods, Gems, Stones, Charms, Chains, Bracelets, Boots, Clothing, Bags, Covers, Armor, Swords , Shields, Axes, Lances, Wands, Scepters and a host of other mystical objects. It also contains dozens of artifacts, all from Greek mythology, Egyptian, Babylonian, Jewish, Christian, Islamic, Russian and other cultures, including the Head of Medusa, the Ark of the Covenant, the Hammer of Thor, Shango Axe, Pomo's Disorder, Excalibur and the Holy Grail. All Items and Artifacts have details, legends, anecdotes and variations of them, which can be used by Masters and Players as a backdrop for adventures and campaigns. All this filled with hundreds of drawings to illustrate the most popular items, so you can know exactly what object your character is carrying. The GUIDE has descriptions of each game object, and simple rules that allow them to adapt to ANY existing RPG

title: Wildblades: The "Easy-Play" Fantasy Fuzion Rules
description: From Introduction: This is your introduction to the exciting world of fantasy roleplaying in the Fuzion system. Using these rules, you can run or play in a fast-paced, versatile game in just a few minutes. Better still, characters created with these rules are fully compatible with full Fuzion games.

title: Chromebook 1 to 4 Conversions
description: From the authors website: This book has Shadowrun game stats for 99.9% of all gear, weapons, chips, programs, vehicles, cyberware, and nearly everything else from all four Chromebooks (for Cyberpunk 2020), plus a few new sample characters thrown in as a bonus. You'll need to buy the Chromebooks to really use the conversions. Edition: Second & Third Even if you own the earlier Chromebook Conversions, this book is worth the download, as it has game statistics for use with Virtual Realities 2.0, Cybertechnology, Rigger 2, and Shadowrun, Third Edition as well as stats for use with earlier rulebooks! Also included are updates for the Plastic Warriors books Running Gear, Tech Specs, Project: 3, and Paranoid Animals of North America!

title: Mind's Eye Theatre Prop Deck
description: A deck of 100 cards (two of which are the front and back cover) for use with the various Mind's Eye Theatre live action role-playing games. These represent a variety of props, from physical items such as modern and medieval weapons, to cards denoting the various supernatural traits of game characters (Blood, Rage, Gnosis, Pathos, Glamour, Willpower).

title: Apocrypha
description: From the archived website: "The Apocrypha system is designed so that the bulk of the players’ effort will go toward character generation. Once the points are spent and the character’s design finalized, the three six-sided die system runs quickly and efficiently during play, so that the director can direct and the players can play without mathematics distracting them from their roleplaying. Every character has access to magic, and technology quite comparable to that used by the players themselves can be had among the scientific races and in the larger cities. Games have an enormous variety of physical setting, from the remote heights of the Palashome Mountains where the Children of Ouliria, sentient cousins of the fiercest birds of prey, hunt freely for booty and trophies of glorious kills, to the equally treacherous streets of Miroc, the urban nightmare that has come of mixing the eleven races under no law but survival of the boldest." Core book has two parts, "Book One" and "Book Two", the latter of which is the directors (game masters) resource.

title: The Book of Wird
description: (from the back of the book) Open your mind to the infinite possibilities of Wird. The Book of Wird, written by Steven S. Long is the ultimate guide to magic in Providence. This massive tome includes fantastic spells for a wide range of Wird casters, including the familiar distinctions of Wird Weaver, Wird Dancer, Wylder and the vile Blight Crow, as well as the new Bright Orioles and Faithsingers. In addition, the Book of Wird includes spells for many of Providence's professions, such as the Recognizers, the Slaywinds, and the D'Shau monks. This encyclopedia of Wird also provides numerous secret spells for the Serpenkine. the Green People, the Swarm Dancers and the White Crow. Finally, the Book of Wird provides magic items and detailed rules on their creation.

title: Zenobia
description: From the Foreword: ZENOBIA is the fantasy roleplaying game of adventure and magic in the ancient world. It is a world where the deserts are home to fierce Saracen tribes, lost cities, sphinx and scorpion-men. And where the cities are home to scheming princes, bandit kings and sinister cults. Opportunity and adventure are everywhere. There are centuries-old tombs to be robbed (if their fiendish residents can be defeated), ancient underground complexes full of obscure traps and breath-taking treasures that were left behind by the gods, palaces owned by fabulously wealthy kings, and temples dedicated to the gods - temples whose physical defences cannot compare with their magical enchantments. From hundred-gated Thebes to the oasis city of Palmyra, from the wealthy caravan city of Carrhae to the strict religious city of dusty Hierosolyma, adventure awaits the brave, the foolhardy and the greedy.
