Dive into Deify for an immersive experience unlike any other! A magnificently illustrated hardcover with gold foil details.

title: Moonsoon: Game Master's Guide
description: Publisher blurb: Dive into the expansive setting of Moonsoon with the Game Master’s Guide, covering the archipelago’s geography, cultures, and multifaceted religions. This guide is a treasure trove of tools for crafting compelling campaigns, including detailed explorations of nine regions and the mystical 12 moons, each packed with adventure hooks and encounters. Enhance your game with the Moon Deck system, bringing the celestial dance of the moons directly into play with dynamic effects.

title: Mystified! A Flabbergasted Expansion
description: Unravel a myriad of mysteries. Mystified! is an expansion to the ENNIE-nominated game Flabbergasted! Learn how to craft a mystery-driven campaign to introduce into your Flabbergasted games. Befuddle, bewitch and bewilder your players as they unravel a myriad of mysteries, with 3 NEW ARCHETYPES, 3 NEW LOCATIONS and plenty of campaigns, story hooks and pre-made club sheets. All of this will be made easy to incorporate into your Flabbergasted games through our new Mystery Sheet.

title: Crooked Moon: A Folk Horror for 5e
description: The Crooked Moon is a massive 632-page tome that brings Folk Horror to 5E! Inside, you'll find player options & GM tools to terrify your friends in a haunting game you’ll never forget, drawing inspiration from grim folklore, legendary horror films, and classic Halloween aesthetics. Immerse yourself in the creepy, thrilling, and mysterious campaign setting of Druskenvald - a demiplane of endless night where the living and dead coexist. Brand new mechanics and features such as the Fateweaving System - easy to use tools to intertwine player character backstories and motivations into the main narrative! Explore all new character creation options including 15 folk horror subclasses and 13 Halloween-inspired reborn species - such as: the Sinner Rogue, a roguish archetype empowered with inflicting hexes & curses with the art of Jinxcrafting; or the Harvestborn, a species of scarecrow-like constructs of wood, metal, or straw. A robust bestiary featuring 85 monsters and beasts inspired by Halloween and folklore, and 11 Legendary Boss Monsters such as the Wikcker Man, The Ghost Train, or The Horned King, to create vivid, iconic, and challenging combat encounters! 26 epic upgrading magic items to use as loot in your games! A terrifying 350-page original adventure for levels 1-13 where a party of wayward souls must journey through the shroud aboard the Ghostlight Express to uncover the dark machinations of the Crooked Man! (from Drive True RPG's page)

title: Marvel Multiverse Roleplaying Game Starter Set: Escape From Planet Hulk
description: The Marvel Multiverse RPG Starter Set includes everything needed to dive into the Mulitverse and become a Super Hero! It features a condensed ruleset, a starting roster of six heroes, maps, tokens and custom dice for an easy start. The starter adventure, "Escape from Planet Hulk", thrusts you and your team into the arena by the Grandmaster to battle a cabal of super villains led by the mysterious Technomancer! Using your unique powers and abilities, you must defeat these villains, evade the rampaging Hulk, and work together to overcome obstacles that no single hero could handle alone. So gather your friends, marshal your strength and prepare for the deadliest challenge of your hero's career!

title: Knight of the Grave
description: From publisher blurb: Written for the 5th edition of the world's most popular roleplaying game. The Grave Knight Leonidas Uviir has returned from a hundred-year absence and now seeks to recreate the Androsan Empire through conquest, and subject all of the former lands to his despicable rule. To that end, his undead army has laid siege to Lorcastle, where he intends to create a bastion, and retake the empire that fell a hundred years ago. It is up to the PCs to defeat him in battle and turn the tide before it's too late. However simply killing him is only the beginning, for a graveknight is only truly destroyed when its armor is unmade. The characters must then journey to the temple of the snake-like Nkoll, and then defeat numerous subterranean challenges so they can challenge him a second and final time, and ending the tyranny of Uviir once and for all. Knight of the Grave was designed by Darrin Drader, who designed the official Death Knight for the third edition of the world's most popular roleplaying game, as well as the original graveknight for Paizo Publishing, for the first edition of the Pathfinder roleplaying game. The graveknight has been fully converted to 5th edition and is designated as open gaming content. This adventure is designed for characters of 17th level.

title: The Caverns of Thracia (5E)
description: From the back cover: A MEGADUNGEON FOR CHARACTER LEVELS 1-10 A Thousand-Year-Old Dungeon Mystery Waiting To Be Solved! The end of one empire sees the birth of a new one deep within the earth. Born in the forgotten chambers and caverns of age-lost Thracia rises a formidable foe: the Minotaur King, along with his host of dogfolk, gnolls, lizardfolk, and others. Some say even Death itself lingers in the Caverns of Thracia, ready to claim any who stumble upon its unholy chapel. The secrets of Thracia are manifold, its treasures thrice that. Do you have the mettle to survive… the Caverns of Thracia? The Caverns of Thracia is a complete mega-dungeon setting that supports a full-year campaign! Takes characters from levels 1 through 10 6 vast dungeon levels 150+ detailed encounter areas 45+ fully-developed new monsters and NPCs 35+ new magic items and spells, plus one new divine domain Plenty of opportunities for combat, exploration, and social interaction with interesting NPCs Tribute essays from professional RPG designers A full reprint of the original The Caverns of Thracia by Jennell Jaquays

title: Keepers of the Elven-rings
description: “In Eregion long ago many Elven-rings were made, magic rings as you call them, and they were, of course, of various kinds: some more potent and some less. The lesser rings were only essays in the craft before it was full-grown, and to the Elven-smiths they were but trifles – yet still to my mind dangerous for mortals. But the Great Rings, the Rings of Power, they were perilous.” In the twilight of the Third Age, three realms of the West-elves still endure in Middle-earth. By the power of the Three Rings, they exist as a testament to a time that was. But not only the Elves remember. As Sauron gathers his strength to bring war to Middle-earth once again, he plans a devious vengeance against those who humbled him long ago. Keepers of the Elven-rings is a supplement for The Lord of the Rings Roleplaying, the official tabletop roleplaying game based on the works of J.R.R. Tolkien. Chapter 1: Where The West-elves Still Linger describes the realms of Lórien, Rivendell and Lindon and those who dwell there, including the Elf-lords who are their guardians. Chapter 2: The Days Darken provides the Loremaster with details about Sauron’s schemes against the Immortal Folk, including possible events, Adversaries, and Loremaster characters. Chapter 3: Landmarks outlines twelve sites of interest, including locations mentioned in The Lord of the Rings™ and others created specifically for the game. The Appendix contains rules to play High Elves and Wood-elves, and introduces guidelines to create Elf-lords, powerful characters who can reach level 20 and are therefore suited for epic level play.

title: Cycle of the Snake-Wolf 3: The Scales of Hyper-Visor God or The Final Voyage of the Crab King
description: From publisher blurb: A paranoid, conspiracy laden, 296-page adventure for Levels 1-6+ for Dungeon Crawl Classics. A Town with Problems, a trek through the Woods, a crawl across The Capitol, and an endless dive into a Mega-Dungeon—chasing a man who dared to touch a God. All tied together with a [Conspiracy Die] that shrinks and grows as the world becomes more and less rational; unable to explain itself. Honestly I was reading Muster, became obsessed with Old School Essential’s dungeon creation procedures, and dove in… then Sean McCoy (Mothership) kicked off Dungeon23 and I knew I needed to release this product. It all started in the Shattered Woods—an agent, a family man, the Crab-King, was sent to visit a god. He touched He, setting off a chain reaction of events, the foretold death of a god, and his own mutagenesis. Enter Mercy of Angels, the haven in the Shattered Woods that housed the dying god and the government man gone mad. Here is the base of operations, where this adventure could start—but doesn’t need to. Everyone wants to find the Crab-King and just bring him home. To his family? To Washington? Rumours say he needs to be brought back to Washington, so maybe there? Maybe you find him? Maybe you just wandered over the Covered Bridge, but you make it to Washington, a place much more paranoid than you think it would be. You can’t trust anyone, but the food is good, and the drinks are even better. Supposedly the Salons (House Parties) are good too. Also, the Witch-Phase have emerged from the earth… I should have mentioned that earlier on the page. They’re elves with shark lineage from the pits of the earth. Dodge the People-in-Black, or don’t—they’ll just send Black Helicopters to bring you back. Don’t say alien. And, most importantly, DO NOT UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCES EVER ENTER THE FACILITY. THERE IS NOTHING THERE AND IT IS DEFINITELY NOT JUST AN OFFICE BUILDING ON TOP OF A BLACK SITE ON TOP OF AN ALIEN CRASH SITE ON TOP OF A SET OF INTERDIMENSIONAL PORTALS. It is in fact a mega-dungeon. Mercy of Angels, the starting town that once housed a god. The Shattered Woods, a point crawl investigating what’s really going on in the woods. Washington, a city crawl searching for “the facility”: A mega-dungeon with 360 rooms, spanning 12 levels, with 13 factions vying for power, 6 layers of history, and at least 2 dragons. 270+ encounters — a lot of things are wandering about, eating people, firing lasers, the usual. 210+ NPCS — from healers, card game players, town mayors, bounty hunters, to a walking brain. 100+ Bestiary Entries — people-in-black, black helicopters, black cars, mutant shock troopers, holographic alien ghosts... the usual 90+ new pieces of gear — from Trinidadian food to shock rifles to all matter of reverse engineered alien technology. 40+ Vehicles — tanks, catamarans, hang gliders. 24 Mercenaries — including a strange man in a mask and a were-jackalope. 30 adventure hooks — there’s UFOs in Washington you know. 30 occupations and new character Desires to go with them. — you can be a noise musician! 1 new playable species. not including the Metal-Faced Giantess, the Giant Robot, or the Spell OS Drives scattered all about; as well as procedures for Oracle Cards, Trading Cards, and Collectible Card Games.

title: Hellpits of NightFang
description: From the back cover: HELLPITS OF NIGHTFANG A SPINE-CHILLING ADVENTURE FOR 9TH-LEVEL CHARACTERS Nocturnal attacks have locals terrified, fearful of leaving the safety of home and hearth once the sun goes down. Something is haunting the night and the locals have dubbed it “NightFang.” The locals are desperate for assistance. Hushed whispers and pointed gestures suggest that the Hellpits, a trio of sinkholes said to house the tomb of a great hero of old that may be the lair of NightFang. Do the heroes possess the cunning and prowess to confront the ferocious vampire which haunts the Hellpits? And is this undead menace the sole evil that lurks within those sunken chambers and passages? The heroes will prevail, or end up the helpless thralls of a vampiric master! Hellpits of NightFang is a 5E adventure intended for use with four to six 9th level characters. Originally written by renowned game designer Jennell Jaquays, Hellpits of NightFang has finally been fully converted for use with 5E rules and can be easily inserted into any home campaign.Hellpits of NightFang is a 5E adventure intended for use with four to six 9th level characters. Originally written by renowned game designer Jennell Jaquays, Hellpits of NightFang has finally been fully converted for use with 5E rules and can be easily inserted into any home campaign.

title: Acquisitive Minds: An OS Mixtape RPG
description: You are entering Lastlands at the height of the Diluvian Age... Welcome to a world of tiny empires, intrigue and opportunity. Welcome to the Dungeonarium. (Publisher Blurb) Acquisitive Minds is a tabletop fantasy roleplaying game about unscrupulous ruffians seeking glory and wealth in a world well past its prime – in the distant future, or perhaps some long forgotten past. What little dry land remains is divided into squabbling city-states, resources are scarce, and deep, dark places teem with monsters eager to overthrow the reign of the surface dwellers. Instead of heroes destined for guaranteed greatness, player characters are ordinary people trying to improve their lot in life with any means necessary. (If that makes them ”murder hoboes”, then so be it.) 12 character trades. 11+ character species. 4+ schools of magic. Any combination is possible – or come up with something entirely new. The rules are short and simple, designed to form an intuitive framework for improvisation and negotiation. Characters grow with experience, but not along any preset class career path; once a player has picked a trade – spy, artisan, trader, scholar, explorer, assassin or something else – they are free to develop their character in any way they wish. Likewise, traditional fantasy species and monsters have been left out. In their place, avians, Lemurian titans, gravedodgers and screwheads fight against twisted gits, bloated slobs, well devils, gigantic ice worms and evil sentient houses. Combat is quick and dangerous, and magic is brutal. Any semblance of play balance is purely accidental. 6 attributes. d20 save and hit rolls. Hit, experience and spell points. Roots firmly in the late seventies, but with all new rules and unprecedented weirdness. Acquisitive Minds is a new take on oldschool roleplaying published by No Just Books. It was dreamed up and written down by Antti Hoo. and illustrated by Hilda Herkman, Anton Erwe & Antti Hoo. Maximum imaginative freedom. Player-driven character development and worldbuilding. No game balance or verisimilitude. Beautiful madness.

title: Glory Road Roleplay IIE - Core Rules
description: Publisher's blurb: What started out as a slight cleanup and change in presentation led to one major change in the system itself after another[.] This edition can be used with all of our previously published settings, GM and player aides, and modules[.] ... Material to run games in the Black Mountain District, urban games in the great city of Old Meyoss, and games on the Silk Road.

title: Age of Adventure RPG (Deluxe Edition)
description: Age of Adventure RPG is a rules-light old-school heroic fantasy tabletop roleplaying game inspired by John Harper's Lasers and Feelings d6 system The "Deluxe Edition" expands the original one-page rule-set to 12 pages with additional rules and improved layout and design for accessibility (easier to read, learn and play). Includes bookmarks, chapters, and a booklet printing option. Welcome adventurer – to an exciting world of monsters, mayhem and magic. All you need are some 6-sided dice and your imagination. All the rules needed to play are included: CHARACTER CREATION: Choose a Community (Dwarf; Elf; Goblin; Halfling; Human; or Orc) and a Role (Archer; Barbarian; Cleric; Rogue; Warrior; or Wizard) PROFICIENCY: choose a number from 2 to 5. A high number means you’re better at Might Actions. A low number means you’re better at Mind Actions SKILLS & STAMINA: your Community & Role determines your Skills; rules for death and healing HERO POINTS: meta-currency to complete heroic Actions PROFICIENCY CHECKS: simple d6 dice pool resolution MAGIC & SPELLS: rules on using Magic and casting Spells EQUIPMENT: all the gear needed for the adventure ahead using a simple "slot-based" encumbrance system RUNNING THE GAME: Random Generators; Turn Order system; Combat DAMAGE & SPECIAL ACTIONS: rules for different types of Damage; Surprise and Special Actions ENCOUNTER TABLE: adversaries, hazards, traps and obstacles CAMPAIGN PLAY: complete adventures to gain Fortune & Glory! Now Onward To Adventure!

title: Adventures in Gilead
description: From publisher blurb: Amidst the crumbling Imperial ruins and war-ravaged wastes of the Gilead System, dangerous heretics, sinister xenos and malevolent plots lurk in every shadow… Adventures in Gilead is a thrilling collection of exciting adventure scenarios and character frameworks for Warhammer 40,000 Roleplay: Wrath & Glory. Players are plunged straight into the intrigue, peril, and danger that define life in the Gilead System from the introductory Rain of Mercy through to the dark machinations of the Chaos Lord Maloquence. Game Masters will find Adventures in Gilead to be an indispensable tool, bursting with ready-to-play adventure scenarios, detailed character frameworks, and countless plot hooks to help build further campaigns and bring the Gilead System to life! From rampaging Orks to Chaos warbands, Adventures in Gilead is packed with gripping adventures and detailed character frameworks, including: Pilgrims of the Wastes — Face down savage Ork Kommandos, duplicitous Aeldari and their insidious machinations, and a sect of the Adeptus Ministorum so fanatical their beliefs border on heresy, all while attempting to navigate the vicious ash-storms and rampaging megafauna of Nethreus! Wretches of the Writ — A grim collection of traitorous Chaos-aligned characters, locations and artefacts. Learn of the Dark Apostle Maloquence and his vile presence within the Gilead System and his plans to seize it in the name of the Dark Gods. Shards of Ul-Khari — Negotiate the cutthroat politics of the Corsair courts, rival warbands, Drukhari hunters, Asuryani envoys, and nefarious Imperial Rogue Traders in this Aeldari-focused adventure. Gutshiva’s Commandos — This explosive adventure framework lets you take control of a group of Ork Kommandos as they sabotage defences, sow chaos and confusion, and prepare to unleash destruction upon an Imperial hive city in preparation for the arrival of a full-blown Waaagh!. Whether you’re weaving a single desperate mission into an existing campaign or creating an epic chronicle of survival in the Emperor’s name, Adventures in Gilead brings the thunderous war zones and awe-inspiring action of the Gilead System to life!

title: The Norway Projekt
description: From publisher blurb: Skis, snow and the hunt for a Nazi superweapon! It is late 1942 and the tide of war begins to turn against Germany. Desperate for a breakthrough, the Nazi high command turns to the emerging field of atomic weapons—placing their hopes in the Norwegian heavy water plant at Vemork. Norway becomes a key battleground in the race to prevent the Nazis developing an atomic bomb as the Allies launch Operations Grouse, Freshman and Gunnerside. Yet Mina Wolff, head of Nachtwölfe, has little patience for conventional science. Instead, she turns to a darker source: a little-known Norwegian cult called the Flokk of the Allfather, whose arcane knowledge may unlock a new generation of atomic wunderwaffe. What begins as a simple mission to rescue captured resistance fighters quickly escalates into a desperate race against time. Section M’s newest team of agents must uncover a vast conspiracy that threatens to rain atomic fire upon Allied cities. With the coldest of fronts rapidly heating up, can your agents foil Nachtwölfe’s twisted ambitions, neutralise the Flokk’s eldritch magics, and prevent the summoning of the Daemon Sultan himself—Azathoth? The 156-page, full-colour The Norway Projekt PDF features: Complete Guide to Norway with new weapons, vehicles and equipment Epic three-part campaign featuring nine linked missions across the Norwegian front Art, maps and handouts bring this huge campaign to life with free digital map pack! Introduces a sinister new cult, the Flokk of the Allfather, plus the terrifying Spellbook of Azathoth The stakes couldn’t be higher: can your agents stop a plot to end the war in atomic horror?

title: The Red Pill
description: Where the Lies End, the Test Begins The shadows of Seattle stretch high into the sky—and deep into the past. When a pair of Johnsons tap the team to trace the source of a dangerous new narcotic, the trail leads straight into the ACHE: the towering husk of the old Renraku arcology, now a vertical warren of desperation, gang turf, and forgotten tech. The runners must find a way inside—whether by stealth, subterfuge, or sheer force—and navigate a maze of hostile factions and crumbling infrastructure. Somewhere within, a Renraku pharmaforge is churning out the drug. But the deeper they go, the more the mission starts to feel like a test. Because it is. The job is a setup. The Johnsons are watching. And if the runners can survive the traps, the gangs, and the truth, they might just earn more than a paycheck. It’s a trial by fire in the heart of the ACHE. And in the Sixth World, the only way out is through. SRM 2081-12 The Red Pill is for use with Shadowrun, Sixth World. - publisher blurp

title: Best Served Cold
description: From publisher blurb: “In the Black City, even the dead are for sale. Until they weren't.” In the heart of the Black City, the Silent Fang assassin guild hid a lucrative operation behind a wagon wash and laundry, preserving their victims in cryomantic cold storage and selling the bodies to a gnome necromancer in exchange for silver, jewels, and rare relics. Their greed proved fatal when a Lantern of Undeath was accidentally lit among the stored corpses, unleashing a ravenous undead horde that overwhelmed the guild; now the block lies eerily silent, rumored to be filled with lurking dead and abandoned riches. Best Served Cold is the second official module for FLAIL roleplaying game. A hidden assassin guild’s corpse-laundering scheme collapses into horror when a necromantic lantern awakens their frozen victims, leaving an entire city block sealed, silent, and crawling with a vengeful undead army and ill-gotten treasure.

title: Too Hot to Handle
description: From publisher blurb: A shipment of enchanted cheese never reached its destination. In an abandoned cluster of storage houses on the edge of town, crates stamped for delivery to Rordon Gamsay’s arcane kitchen tower have been left to fester. Heat lingers where it should not, stone sweats with melted residue, and something has begun to move among the forgotten cargo. What was meant to be carefully monitored culinary magic has spoiled into something volatile and dangerous. As the party investigates, living cheese oozes out of containment, spreading heat and chaos through the ruined plaza. The longer the situation goes unchecked, the worse the environment becomes. Too Hot to Handle is a short, high-pressure side quest focused on containment, environmental hazards, and reckless misuse of Rordon Gamsay’s experimental techniques.

title: Artifact Zero
description: From the back cover: TIME IS AN IMPATIENT KILLER Disappearances in rural Montana are nothing new. Thousands go missing every year. Most are teenagers. An outsize proportion are indigenous. Only a few hundred vanish altogether, never located. Those are rarely scientists. Spring, 1998. A mining company explores a Montana site with seismographs, hoping to find precious metals. Instead it finds what look to be anomalous artifacts at depths that make the readings suspicious. Summer, 1999. University researchers scanning the site find “erratics,” objects that should not be there. They dig up bolts and pieces of machinery; aviator sunglasses and a Rolex wristwatch; a skeleton with a steel surgical pin in its leg and fillings in its teeth—all buried for five million years. Fall, 1999. Researchers start vanishing. Delta Green sends the Agents to investigate. They may find deadly terrors beyond time itself. One of the most infamous scenarios in the history of Delta Green, Artifact Zero can be played with Delta Green: The Role-Playing Game or the free quickstart rules in Delta Green: Need to Know. It makes a perfect companion to the 1990s sourcebook Delta Green: The Conspiracy. Further incursions are available from Arc Dream Publishing. If your Agents are still there to face them.

title: The Drowned Relic
description: From publisher blurb: The small Village of Trutsmman kept a secret passed from mother to daughter until a rebellious young woman revealed it. Meanwhile, two Sahuagin women cast a powerful spell. A tsunami struck, destroying the dock, killing many workers, and sinking the village, which was later abandoned by the survivors. Now considered cursed by greed, the ruins are claimed by the Sahuagin, who seek to recover their people’s lost Relic at any cost. Welcome back, adventurer! Our adventure series continues with its seventh release! After exploring The Desolated Forsaken Reserva, we now dive into a region of salt, rock, and strange creatures! We invite you to brave the The Salty and Rocky, uncover the mysteries of the The Coral Golem, explore the The Sahuagin Cathedral for treasures and secrets. Inside this volume you will find: 4 different locations with hidden treasures, creatures and hooks; A distinct dynamic of exploration (hex 4); A brand-new creature: The Coral Golem - complete with statistics and an original illustration.

title: Replicant Rebellion
description: From publisher blurb: Welcome to the REPLICANT REBELLION expansion for the official BLADE RUNNER roleplaying game. An evocative cat & mouse race of provocateurs and powderkegs lit to explode, expose, and oppose the establishment, one way or another. In a neon-noir city of dazzling lights and moral contradictions, it’s you on the streets, wrestling with the hypocrisies of the justice system, the razor’s edge between reform and revenge, and the burdens of having too much to lose for a cause sacrificing it all. Yet regardless the price, the Rebellion must live on.

title: DC20 One Shot Collection
description: UNRELEASED GAME This game has not been released yet, therefore some of the information above may change in the future. Until this game is released, please don't submit images for it unless given direct written permission from the publisher which must be noted in the image caption. From the Kickstarter: This will have an assortment of One-Shots of all kinds that you can run to showcase DC20 to your current group or a new group of friends.

title: The First Step: A Level Zero Adventure For The Stormlight RPG
description: From publisher blurb: Step into the world of Roshar! Ordinary people must rise and become heroes in this unique adventure for the Cosmere® Roleplaying Game. The First Step™ is a “session zero” adventure in which players begin with blank character sheets. As the story progresses, each player’s choices determine the character they create—and the fate of a convoy ambushed by a vengeful Shardbearer! This adventure contains basic rules required for play, but a copy of the Stormlight™ Handbook completes the experience. The First Step can also serve as a prologue for the Stonewalkers™ adventure. This “level zero” adventure is a great starting point for the Cosmere® RPG! Create your first character in the Stormlight Campaign Setting while making important decisions during a life-threatening scenario. Includes a free download link for a solo version of the adventure!

title: Star Trek Adventures Second Edition Starter Set
description: From publisher blurb: TO BOLDLY GO WHERE NO ONE HAS GONE BEFORE! The Star Trek™ Adventures roleplaying game takes you to the final frontier! You may travel to the edges of known space, to colonies in need, to the borders of neighboring galactic powers, or into the eye of interstellar phenomena. Explore strange new worlds, encounter fantastical alien life-forms, and engage in dramatic adventures in a detailed and immersive Galaxy. PLAY STRAIGHT OUT OF YOUR INBOX! This Starter Set PDF includes almost everything you need to begin your journey into the final frontier, including an epic campaign of three linked missions designed to challenge a brave crew of 3–7 player characters. The Star Trek Adventures Second Edition Starter Set PDF features: RULES BOOKLET: A 48-page PDF booklet providing an overview of the 2d20 game system adapted for Star Trek Adventures, for use with the included campaign. CAMPAIGN BOOKLET: A 60-page PDF booklet containing the epic, three-part campaign “Infinite Combinations,” which guides you through the rules as you play so you can begin exploring the Galaxy right away. SHIP SHEET and CHARACTER SHEETS: A PDF statistics sheet for the Constitution-class U.S.S. Challenger and seven player character sheets, with a rules reference sheet for each character. REFERENCE SHEETS: Eight rules reference sheet PDFs. PRINT AND PLAY TOKENS: Six Momentum tokens for each of six different civilizations (i.e., 36 total Momentum tokens), 36 Threat tokens, and 9 Determination tokens.

title: Space 1889: After (5E)
description: From publisher blurb: Everything Jules Verne could have written.... Everything H.G. Wells should have written.... Everything A. Conan Doyle thought but never published because it was too fantastic! Space 1889: After is the next iteration of Space 1889—the popular steampunk tabletop game—with new rules, new possibilities, and new stories to tell. The Victorian Age in Space 1889 was marked by astounding discoveries that opened the strange, inhabited worlds of Mars and Venus to exploration and colonization. Space 1889: After takes place ten years later, after war and disaster separated the worlds for a time. Now, adventurers must risk the perils of space to explore other worlds and fight new enemies that lurk in the ether. If you find the steampunk setting compelling, but are uncertain about learning the Empyrean ruleset, we've got you covered! Space 1889: After is available for the fifth edition of the most popular game system in the world. The converted rules for ether-fliers, sky galleons, and aerial gunboats are compatible with any space-faring 5th edition game, and the Space 1889: After setting itself is a potential destination for any spell mishap or astral plane excursion. Cannons, weird science, and boarding actions beckon!

title: Lost Lore of Thracia
description: From the back cover: Much lore was lost when Thracia collapsed and the bestial inhabitants invaded the Caverns below. A few scraps of ancient knowledge survived on crumbling scrolls and broken pottery overlooked in the subterranean gloom. Now, for the first time in centuries, those lost secrets are revealed. Lost Lore of Thracia collects the stretch goal-achieved content from The Caverns of Thracia crowdfunding campaign, all assembled in a single volume for our backers' convenience. You will find within these pages information on the ruined city of Thracia, the Island of Thracia, and the starry archipelago it occupies. This book also includes selective ecologies on the monstrous denizens of the caverns, as well as 17 all-new Thracia-inspired monsters to further populate your campaign. While this book is suitable for any fantasy RPG campaign and doesn't require a copy of The Caverns of Thracia to make use of it, owning a copy of that book, also published by Goodman Games, will greatly enhance your enjoyment of it.

title: Transgender Deathmatch Legend II
description: Step into the shoes of a hard done by trans wrestler in the city of Slamchester and confront old friends, bitter rivals, violent bigots, awful doctors and more. Put the HRT in hurt, and put the hurt on anyone who tries you. You’re someone who’s been pushed around, screwed over, and badmouthed. They say you’re too much, they say you’re out of control. The world says you’re too loud and too violent. You should show them what it really means to be loud, to be violent, if they wanna knock you for being the wrong way, let them know you hit back. Transgender Deathmatch Legend II is a TTRPG beat-em-up designed for two players, using a deck of playing cards. Play out brutal street fights resolved via a trick-taking game with individuals tricks zooming in on the beat-by-beat of violence. Navigate the hyper-stylised city of Slamchester through seven dangerous hexcrawls. How does it work?One player will act as the Face, responsible for creating and playing the titular transgender deathmatch legend, while another player will act as the Promotor, who'll frame scenes, fill in the world, and take on the role of opponents for any fight. The Face moves across the hexmap and the Promotor introduces challenges, different weapons to be grabbed, allies of the Face, and a series of dangerous enemies. When a fight hex is entered the fight commences. In a fight players run through a trick taking game, with an individual trick zooming in on a singular beat of a fight. Played cards correspond to the attempted maneuvers. The four fighting styles of the game include different flavouring of moves as well as unique trump effects reflecting their individual style of attack. Will you be relying on strength and fighting spirit, technical knowhow, a desire to break all limits, or a bag of dirty tricks? Featuring: Easy to grasp and intuitively tactical rules Four Unique Fighting Styles Seven Hyperviolent Hexcrawl Scenarios Vibrant and raw photography throughout Templates and instructions for online play Alternative Rules Accommodating More Players Arcade Mode! Tag Team Turmoil! Own Transgender Deathmatch Legend in physical form!The book with be released physically soon in A4 landscape wiro-bound format. Pre-order via the BackerKit pledge management site here. Source: The Developer's Website.

title: The Horn of the Dawn Part 1: Traces of Darkness
description: From publisher blurb: Ambin Silfverspear stepped off the railing with a big sigh of relief. Almost a full month had she spent on the ship that had taken her from the capital city of Bastion to the obscure colony called Windheim. It felt strange to walk on solid ground once more. So this was Foamsvale? The town from which the Bastionites first settled and then conquered this godsforsaken island. There were tales in the capital almost as old as the city itself of great artifacts and magical treasure to be found here. Ancient dwarven weapons and forgotten relics. There was no better place to go to find these things, or to experience untamed wilderness, it was told. She would sleep the night here and then she would head off out into the wild. Her father would regret not naming her his heir. She would show him! The world shall know her name! Traces of Darkness is part one of the epic campaign Horn of the Dawn, set on the island of Windheim. This scenario takes the adventurers through a feared mountain pass and beyond, where they will uncover ancient secrets, treachery, and cold blooded murder. Included are also a few adventure seeds, written by guest authors Robert J Schwalb, Maria Ruthgård and Robert Jonsson. Ambin Silfverspjuuth steg av landgången och drog en suck av lättnad. Hon hade spenderat nästan en månad på skeppet som tagit henne från huvudstaden Bastion till den avlägsna och obskyra kolonin Windheim. Det kändes underligt att än en gång känna fast mark under fötterna. Så, Brusdala… Staden som bastioniterna grundlade för att ha som bas under koloniseringen av denna avkrok till ö. Det berättades sägner i huvudstaden, lika gamla som staden själv, om artefakter och magiska skatter att finna här. Uråldriga dvärgiska vapen och reliker. Det fanns ingen bättre plats att finna dylika saker, eller för att uppleva otämjd vildmark, sades det. Hon skulle övernatta här, och sedan ge sig iväg ut i ödemarken. Hennes far skulle få ångra att han inte utsett henne till sin arvinge. Hon skulle visa honom! Världen skall få veta hennes namn! Spår av mörker är del ett i den episka kampanjen Gryningshornet som utspelar sig på ön Windheim. Scenariot tar äventyrarna genom en farofylld dal, bortom vilken de kommer att finna uråldriga hemligheter, förräderi, och ond bråd död. Boken inkluderar även några äventyrsfrön, skrivna av gästförfattare Robert J Schwalb, Maria Ruthgård och Robert Jonsson.

title: DC20 Monster Collection
description: UNRELEASED GAME This game has not been released yet, therefore some of the information above may change in the future. Until this game is released, please don't submit images for it unless given direct written permission from the publisher which must be noted in the image caption. From the Kickstarter: This will have a wide range of Monsters of all types and levels that you’ll be able to build campaigns and encounters around.

title: DC20 Advanced Guide Collection
description: UNRELEASED GAME This game has not been released yet, therefore some of the information above may change in the future. Until this game is released, please don't submit images for it unless given direct written permission from the publisher which must be noted in the image caption. From the Kickstarter: This will have tons of tips, tricks and tools to help you create, run, and build out DC20 Sessions and Campaigns!

title: Reanimated: An RPG for the Zombie Apocalypse
description: Publisher's blurb: Reanimated is a zombie survival sandbox game using the d8 based Key Powered System that offers a great deal of options for customizing the zombies, from the mindless shambler to some creatures that are absolute nightmare fuel.

title: Welcome to Night Vale
description: [Back of the Box] Enter the world of the macabre and the mundane that one of the most popular fictional podcasts calls home! The Welcome to Night Vale Roleplaying Game inviste you to explore the fictional desert town from the hit podcast that's captured listeners' imagination since 2012. The absurd and surreal join your game with Night Vale's expansive cast of characters, creatures, and phenomena. Enjoy the included five-part mystery or craft a story that leads you anywhere in Night Vale - just NOT the dog park! The Welcome to Night Vale Roleplaying Game includes: Complete rules to roleplay in the bizarre town of the hit Welcome to Night Vale podcast. The Citizens' Guide teaches basic rules and helps you create your denizen of Night Vale. The Host Guide contains everything you need to share strange and surreal adventures. The Visitor's Guide helps you find your way around the desert town of Night Vale. A five-part mystery adventure that leads characters to the Numbers Station, the Library, and more of NIght Vale's environs. 8 Dice and a Game Master's Screen provide all the needed supplies at your fingertips. 11 pre-generated characters ready to immediately start your adventures in Night Vale. Additional Threats for your citizens to encounter in the peculiar locale. Visitor's Guide: 44 pages Citizens' Guide: 76 pages Host Guide: 76 pages Adventure "The Skeleton Gorge Incident": 64 pages

title: :OTHERSCAPE - The Mythic Cyberpunk RPG - Demo Game
description: Publisher's blurb: [C]ontains the basic rules, three pregens, and a demo adventure! User summary: The booklet was distributed with a sheet of tracking cards (17" x 11") and three double-sided character sheet posters (17" x 11") - Genji, Unagi, and Wilson

title: God's Light
description: From the back cover: WHERE LIES THE LINE BETWEEN MADNESS AND EVIL? It is madness to cross the threshold. It is evil not to seek the threshold. Which do you choose? A seemingly ordinary American mass shooting draws the attention of Delta Green. The shooter left evidence that seemed to come not from some unknown place but some unknown version of reality. The investigation finds a social media app called Picky Eater. But is it more than an app? A modified OS? A firmware update? An inexplicable hardware update? Picky Eater curates your social media feed, picking and choosing experiences to suit your desires. But it may reach far deeper than that. It may reach strange places that stand on either side of the border between worlds, between realities, between madness and evil, and force your Agents to choose. Delta Green: God’s Light … makes a perfect companion to the acclaimed campaign Delta Green: God’s Teeth. User comment: One of four scenarios that expand God's Teeth. To be collected in God's Hunt omnibus hardback in 2025.

title: The Garden of Flesh and Blood
description: From the publisher blurb: The Garden of Flesh and Blood is a faerie themed wilderness adventure site with 6 sub-locations. Featuring a tricky villain, new monsters and magic items, and original dark-fantasy art. This deadly but rewarding location is the perfect addition to your wilderness encounter tables, or a great one-shot adventure. The three new custom monsters are provided with complete stats in the style of the groundbreaking ACKS II Monster Manual, so they can be used throughout a detailed sandbox campaign.

title: Cohors Cthulhu Germania
description: From back cover: This supplement includes a wealth of information for Cohors Cthulhu gamemasters running campaigns venturing into Germania from the Roman-occupied town of Laurium. This includes information on the many tribes of Germania, details on Herjan's Horde, an expanded Adversary roster, and 8 self-contained adventures taking place in Germania itself: Castrum Primum - detailing a lost fortress that has become a place of horror. Stars of Ill Omen - featuring an incursion of Mi-Go in the Germanic hills. The Harvest - where a tribal settlement is beset by a mysterious ailment. The Shield of Aeneas - featuring the impossible return of an ancient hero making wild claims. Anno Diaboli - where an attempt to impose a ridig calendar upon a tribal people has unforeseen consequences. Between Scylla and Charybdis - where a holy festival provides the spark to set ablaze a religious conflict. Against the Chaos Gate - where PCs enter the gladiatorial arena and eventually discover an ancient buried peril. Aurum Habet Tolosanum - featuring a risky foray sneaking into the heart of Herjan's Horde to recover gold stolen from Rome.

title: World Tour - London
description: London is calling—and the shadows are answering. Fresh off global acclaim and a catastrophe in Paris, the runners find themselves invited to an exclusive, high-society gala at a decadent London mansion where money, power, and secrecy flow as freely as the champagne. Beneath the glittering lights, AR-enhanced gardens, and designer suits lies something far more dangerous: a clandestine underground auction catering to the world’s richest and most corrupt elites. On the block is cutting-edge binaural signal technology—highly illegal, devastatingly powerful, and capable of reshaping minds, markets, and entire cities. As bids soar and masks slip, the runners must navigate a web of corporate intrigue, criminal middlemen, and velvet-gloved security, deciding who walks away with the tech… and who doesn’t walk away at all. Will the team play the room, crash the auction, or burn the whole operation to the ground? In London’s upper crust, every smile hides a knife—and every deal echoes through the shadows. Dress sharp. Watch your back. And never trust a polite host. - publisher blurp

title: Shadowrun Anarchy 2.0 Core Rulebook
description: From the publisher's website: Shadowrun: Anarchy 2.0 is the second edition of the rules-light alternative core book for the legendary Shadowrun roleplaying game. Staying true to Shadowrun’s iconic blend of near-future cyberpunk grit and arcane mysticism, Anarchy 2.0 offers a fast, cinematic approach to the Sixth World—ideal for newcomers or those who prefer narrative-first gameplay. It retains the franchise’s beloved setting rich character creation options and six-sided dice rolls while paring down detailed mechanics to spotlight storytelling and action. Shadowrun; Anarchy 2.0 is published in both English and French by Black Book Éditions, under license from Catalyst Game Labs.

title: Hot War Second Edition
description: From publisher blurb: London. Winter. 1963. It is a year since the Cold War went hot. This was not just a nuclear war, as darker weapons with their roots in World War II’s suffering and fringe science were deployed. Survival and re-building are all that matter now. But human nature and the breakdown of society mean that everyone has their own ambitions. Into this maelstrom steps a motley band of women and men tasked with the jobs too dirty or dangerous for anyone else. They have to deal with relationships, loves, hatred, and hidden agendas in a collapsing, ravaged, horror-strewn Britain. Revised and reimagined from the first edition that appeared in 2008, Hot War keeps everything great about this critically acclaimed RPG while bringing fresh ideas to the texts and the mechanics. When conflicts arise in the game, players create pools of ten-sided dice from attributes, traits, tools, hidden agendas, and relationships. Control (the GM) assembles a pool of dice from various thematic groups (external enemies, internal enemies, monsters, London, and Breakdown) to provide opposition. Control and the players roll their dice, and for each die that's higher than an opposing die, that side gains 1 "success". The side with the most successes wins, and can spend those successes to generate consequences that alter the characters, the story, and Control’s dice groups. Players and Control will consistently be making taut, meaningful, and challenging decisions with their dice as the game progresses and tension rises. The game mechanics reinforce the key themes of the setting: in Hot War the nuclear destruction of society is ever present in the form of 'Breakdown' and Control can use this to make the characters' lives increasingly difficult.

title: Forjador de reyes: Tomo 2
description: From publisher blurb: ¿Quién gobierna las Tierras Robadas? Las Tierras Robadas han sido durante mucho tiempo el dominio de bandidos y monstruos, ¡pero ya no! A tu grupo se le ha concedido una patente para explorar estas tierras vírgenes, derrotar sus peligros y construir una nueva nación. Sin embargo, no todo el mundo os acogerá como nuevos vecinos y existen poderosas fuerzas sobrenaturales que tienen sus propios planes para la región. ¿Podrás derrotar a los enemigos de tu reino y convertirlo en una de las nuevas naciones más grandes del mundo? Tomo 2: Los capítulos 9 a 11 de la Senda de aventuras Forjador de reyes original, ampliados y actualizados para su uso con la 2ª Edición de Pathfinder. Cientos de páginas de contenido nuevo que amplían la campaña para cubrir toda la gama de niveles de 1 a 20. para cubrir toda la gama de niveles de 1 a 20. Extensas reglas para construir y dirigir reinos, asentamientos y ejércitos, junto con reglas basadas en la narrativa para resolver rápidamente combates de masas.

title: Ashes to Ashes (The Necromancer's Game)
description: From publisher blurb: From Dust You Come and to Dust You Shall Return Far from the trade roads and walled cities of the interior lies the town of Thistlewatch, built on the bones of something older and buried. Perched between the hills and the forests of the outer frontier, Thistlewatch survives on stubbornness alone. Its fields are poor. The town is surrounded by a land once known for fertile fields, now half-swallowed by scrubland and moss-slick boulders. The forests here are twisted thickets gnarled and grown over ruins where birds do not sing and paths loop back upon themselves. Animals have gone still. Rangers whisper of sleeping deer that do not rise, of foxes curled under trees unmoving, of bears snoring beside their kills. And now, in Thistlewatch, the children are beginning to fall asleep and never wake again. It began with one. Then two. Now there are twelve, perhaps more. Healthy children, laughing and playing, who one day did not rise from their blankets. Their breathing is steady. Their hearts are strong. But nothing, neither medicine nor prayer can wake them. They sleep as if buried in a grave. Desperate, the townsfolk turned to the Temple of St. Cuthbert, the moral anchor of Thistlewatch. Sister Lenna, the priestess there, found something older buried beneath its foundations, a sealed stair, hidden under a cracked stone near the altar. And now, amid fear and famine, she begs for aid. Something lies below. Something ancient. Something that breathes the same dust that now chokes her sleeping flock. The player characters have been asked to look into what is happening. Ashes to Ashes is an adventure for The Necromancer's Game that will challenge 2–6 player characters of levels 4–8+.

title: Marvel Rivals: Timestream Adventure
description: From publisher blurb: The latest Marvel RPG adventure expands to the world of Marvel Rivals, from the hit video game. Get caught up within the Timestream Entanglement as the Marvel Multiverse Role-Playing Game joins forces with the hottest video game around, Marvel Rivals! Join Psylocke, Mantis, Jeff the Land Shark and many other characters as they escape from the Collector’s Museum. This adventure features a new 10-page comic and an RPG adventure that serves as a prologue to Marvel Rivals.

title: Clockwork & Magick: Chronicles of the Steam Age Vol. 1
description: From publisher blurb: Clockwork & Magick - Chronicles of the Steam Age is a zine dedicated to New Europe and the World of Castle Falkenstein. Within its pages you will find ideas for new adventures, details for the Victorian life and festivities, Host Characters, Magick, SteamTech and more! Vol. 1 adds the following to your Castle Falkenstein game: A Terror in the Night: a new Headline, the equivalent of Cyberpunk's Screamsheets for Castle Falkenstein Advice for the Host - suggestions and know-ho regarding combat, all-in and when to use your own cards Masks, Manners, and Music: The World of Fancy-Dress Balls in New Europe Austro-Hungarian Sky Hussars: The Peregrine ornithopters of the Austro-Hungarian Army SteamTech Wonders: Special Formulations for your game History of Late Winter Carnival Season in Europe: a short introduction to the Carnival Season Artifacts of New Europe: The Mask of Casanova, a lesser known, but potent artifact for your Sorcerer! The Restless Explorer: a ready-to-play Dramatic Character for Players The Dwarven Engineer’s Townhouse: A new location and Host Character in Paris Carnival and Easter Delights: Seasonal Sweets of the Victorian Table Anton Kolchak – The Man with the Hypnotic Gaze: a new Villain for the Host Mr. Punch – The Masked Enigma of New Europe: a new Ally (or Villain?) for the Hiost

title: Demon-Seed of the Plant God (The Necromancer's Game)
description: From publisher blurb: The Wilderness Is Watching. The Seed Is Stirring. Farmhome is a place where stories linger. The crows are too quiet. The goats are restless at night. And the trail beyond the village leads into fog and vine, where travelers vanish, and only bones come back. The journey will not be kind. Every hill, every cave, every fallen log may hide danger. But the road leads forward, through wet woods and broken idols, to where the earth drinks blood and something far older than gods begins to wake. The Demon-Seed of the Plant God is is an adventure for The Necromancer's Game that will challenge 2–6 player characters of levels 4–6.

title: The Dark Heart Tree
description: From publisher blurb: After crossing a series of green hills, the characters will find the Land of the Black Trees, a place abandoned by any form of human civilization. It is said that the region is plagued by hauntings, inexplicable voices, and sudden disappearances. However, much is left unsaid, for no one has returned to tell the tale. Welcome back, adventurer! Our series continues now in the depths of dense tropical forests. After venturing through #0 — The Hollow Tower in the desert sands, #1 — The Pact Flame in volcanic lands, and #2 — On the Frozen Wastelands, #3 — The Suns Betrayal, we now bring you the fifth region. We invite you to brave The Dark Forest, uncover the mysteries of the Well and the Monolith, explore the Inverted Stairs, and face the Dark Heart Tree, a terrorizing creature born by nature. The Sun’s Betrayal is an adventure for characters of levels 2–4, designed for use with Old-School Essentials. Written by Nicole Mattos • Edited by Davide Tramma and Ícaro Agostino • Original art by Geraldo Marinho Inside this volume you will find: A Hexmap of the Region, featuring four distinct locations to explore, including a mini dungeon; A new game mechanic: The Moving Trees; A brand-new creature: The Dark Heart Tree – complete with statistics and an original illustration.

title: Glory Road Roleplay IIE - Encounters
description: Publisher's blurb: The first beings in this book are people. They have speech, societies and most of them are bipeds. I never intended that a campaign use every kind of people listed but they are available for use, modification, or neglect. The next group of beings are the dragons, who have speech, rarely have societies, and are not bipedal. My take on dragons, that they are distinguished from one another by many things but not by their breath weapon doesn’t prevent my presenting a large variety of dragon kinds. The third group is called beasts and is a grab-bag of beings, mostly without speech or societies, differing greatly from one another. Some are real-world animals and others are from fiction and folklore. The undead, ranging from almost-harmless Voices, repeating what a person said in life, to Liches and Presences, powerful inimical beings. Then demons, both minor and major And finally, vampires.

title: Deniable Assets
description: Sometimes you need a ready-to-play character for a pick-up game; other times, just a spark of inspiration or a starting point to tweak and make your own. Either way, having a few pregens handy is always appreciated. So, here are 15 new pregen characters for Shadowrun Anarchy v2.0, including 2 technomancers! - publisher blurp

title: AnamnesiA
description: AnamnesiA is a GM-less narrative roleplaying game of psychological horror for 2–4 players. Your characters wake up with no memory of a traumatic event. Over 5 cycles of play (90–150 minutes), you piece together what happened — and what you did. THE RIGHT-HAND RULE The core mechanic: when you roll dice to recover a memory, the player sitting to your right interprets the result. Not you. You never control your own past. WHAT'S INCLUDED — 4 Archetypes: The Survivor, The Witness, The Protector, The Catalyst — 2 complete scenarios: The Incident, The Betrayal — Built-in safety tools: X-Card, Lines & Veils, Session Zero guide — Expanded Keeper Guide with phase-specific complication generators — Universal Fragment Tables for improvised play — Game variants: Custodian-less Mode, Campaign Mode, Light Thriller Mode — Free appendix: The Lethe Infection (compatible with OSE, Mörk Borg, Call of Cthulhu) Free Quickstart available on itch.io and DriveThruRPG. Designed by Riccardo Scaringi (Il Gioco in Tavolo). ZineQuest 2026.

title: Courts & Crowns: A 5E Supplement for Court Intrigue, Romance & Social Mayhem
description: From publisher blurb: Your sword will not help you here. You've been invited to a ball — and it's the most dangerous room you've ever walked into. No dungeon. No dragon. Just candlelight, champagne, four people already plotting something, two people desperately in love, and one person smiling at you in a way that means absolutely nothing good. Courts and Crowns is a complete 300-page social mechanics supplement for 5th Edition D&D. Inside: A full Reputation Point economy The Gossip Engine — a living rumor-and-scandal system Six new Courtly Classes: Courtier, Gossip, Herald, Paramour, Performer, Schemer Dancing, flirtation, wit duels, and kissing mechanics (yes, with a critical failure table) A Wardrobe System that will make your players think very carefully about what they wear to a coronation Court Profiles for every existing 5E class Full Season frameworks, NPCs, story hooks, and patron relationships For new players who love Bridgerton and Pride & Prejudice. For veteran players ready to watch their dragon-slaying Warrior get destroyed by a seating chart. Social encounters deserve the same mechanical weight as combat. This is the supplement that gives it to them. The Season has begun. Do try to wear the right thing.

title: Witch: Fated Souls 2E
description: From publisher blurb: Witch: Fated Souls Second Edition is a modern, dark fantasy game where you play a witch who sold their soul to a demon for power. Your soul might be lost, but there’s still hope for redemption if you can outwit your demon! The game draws its inspiration from TV shows like: The Chilling Adventures of Sabrina, Supernatural, and American Horror Story: Coven. Behind the familiar façade of the world you know hides a world of supernatural wonder and horror. This game deals with witches—known as Fated—people who sold their souls in a demonic bargain. The reasons witches strike such a hellish bargain are varied. One witch might fate themselves for power, another for money, and a third to save a loved one. Magic is never the point of the bargain, but it always comes as a side-effect whether the witch likes it or not. Most witches come to regret this deal sooner or later.

title: The Forsaken Reserva
description: From publisher blurb: The Reserva lies underground, beneath a one-room building where Carlos’s assistants once worked. There they acted as intermediaries between the public and the prisoners, often dealing with families who wanted to commit someone, and rarely with those who wished to visit. Welcome back, adventurer! Our series continues now in the depths of a dungeon. After exploring dense forests in #3 — The Sun’s Betrayal and the very heart of darkness in #4 — The Dark Heart Tree, we now bring you The Desolated Zone. We invite you to brave the The Desolated Zone, uncover the mysteries of the The Chainwailer, explore the The Forsaken Reserva for treasures and secrets The Forsaken Reserva is an adventure for characters of levels 1–3, designed for use with Old-School Essentials. Written by Nicole Mattos • Edited by Davide Tramma and Ícaro Agostino • Original art by Geraldo Marinho Inside this volume you will find: A Dungeon with 17 rooms to explore, filled with creatures, puzzles and treasures; Two distinct locations around the Reserva with its own backgrounds and events possible to happen; A brand-new creature: The Chainwailer – complete with statistics and an original illustration

title: Against the Scarlet Hunger
description: From publisher blurb: A Year on Gosfel Survive a Year of Peril, Intrigue, and Horror on the Cursed Island of Gosfel. Against the Scarlet Hunger is a full length 5th Edition campaign, designed to take a party of adventurers from 1st to 5th level. What begins as a simple year long contract as an Isle Warden on the remote island of Gosfel spirals into a desperate struggle for survival. This campaign features 12 interconnected monthly adventures that blend survival horror with heroic fantasy

title: FLAIL
description: From publisher blurb: FLAIL is a fast, scrappy OSR brawler where spells act up, weapons fall apart, and characters survive mostly through nerve, luck, or whatever they grabbed on the way in. Hit points are low, danger stays high, and plans rarely make it past the second roll. It’s classic fantasy chaos with a slightly unhinged grin. Created by Andre Novoa, FLAIL is developed and published by Games Omnivorous. Uses a tactile item-and-gear system that makes inventory management actually interesting, and a push-your-luck combat system that feels like a game of dice poker. Quick to learn, quicker to implode, ideal for hexcrawls, dungeons, and players who enjoy seeing what goes wrong first.

title: Terminus
description: Publisher blurb: It is Tuesday 31st May 2022. London’s new Underground line has finally opened… and people are going missing at Tottenham Court Road. Strange disappearances, esoteric architecture, and buried secrets combine in this system-neutral horror scenario for tabletop roleplaying games, set in modern London. Terminus draws on the genuine art and architecture of Tottenham Court Road, the archaeology of the surrounding area, and strange quirks of history to spin a tale of architectural horror with just a touch of Lovecraftian mythos. Terminus is expected to run in 1–2 sessions and should be compatible with a variety of systems. Terminus comes with a two map handouts, and is accompanied by a variety of supporting resources including VTT backgrounds and real-world documents that can be used as further handouts.

title: The Distant Way
description: From publisher blurb: The Distant Way is supplement booklet for Witch: Fated Souls 2e. It includes three different scenarios and various props to download and print for your game.

title: A Guide To Fauna Expansion
description: From publisher blurb: This is an urgent appeal. Despite the best efforts of the Galactic Council, the planet Darwin is facing an extinction event that will wipe out 99% of all life within the next 100 years. To preserve the millions of unique species present on Darwin, the GC has begun work on a new terraforming project but it will not be completed before Darwin is destroyed. We need your help to catalogue, study, and relocate the animals of Darwin to wildlife reserves around the galaxy until a new home can be made for them. In this 102 page expansion you can: Explore 3 New Worlds - Darwin (a doomed wild planet), Verglas (an iceball with a surprising ecosystem), and Menagerie (the galaxy's largest zoo space station). Undertake new Research, Relocation, and Poaching Missions. Take part in new detailed Expeditions on any planet! Explore a new Galactic quadrant with new Travel Events and its own Map. Choose from new Captain Backgrounds, new Crewmate Traits and Skills, and Ship Systems. Generate pets with their own Traits and Personalities! and create Species Files detailing all the weird and wonderful animals you come across! This expansion is perfect for players who want to delve deeper into xenozoology or for those who just want to include more wildlife in their stories.

title: As Above, So Below
description: From publisher blurb: "STARS CAVORT IN THE BLACK FIRMAMENT ABOVE, DEMONS DANCE BELOW. WHAT BURDEN, WHAT SUFFERING IS LEFT TO ENDURE BEFORE THE SUN RISES ONCE MORE?" As Above, So Below is an unofficial supplement for Heart: the City Beneath. It contains: — Lunir, or moon elves, an ancestry for the original inhabitants of the moon, now displaced by her deific becoming. — The Lantern Order, a new faction of zealous haven-defenders that will scourge the City Beneath of occultism at all costs. — Two new classes: the LAMPLIGHTER, a sacrificial protector with an accursed lantern, and the EIDOLON, a victim-made-vessel for a newborn demon.

title: Maximum Mystic Punks Volume 1: The Trial at Wenderd's
description: From publisher blurb: Who's got the munchies??? Mystic Punks’ first official adventure for group play! In The Trial At Wenderd’s your Punks step into a fast-food hell house overrun by a Xofl invasion––foul creatures from another dimension determined to conquer the earth! Get ready for 24 pages of horror as they trek through Wenderd’s - Home Of Mega Melty Munchies™. Get ready to face off against: deranged imps a ball pit of doom a frozen lich wizard and more!

title: Outpost 33
description: From publisher blurb: A supply ship descends upon the ice-covered planet of Kaldoth during a routine delivery. Shrill winds whip against the facility. The air crackles with electrostatic. As the crew pull into the landing pad, there is no greeting party, no smiling faces awaiting their only lifeline. Something is terribly wrong. Outpost 33 is a standalone investigative adventure for the Mothership Roleplaying System, designed to be played over 1-3 sessions.

title: Blade Runner Asset Pack & Solo Mode
description: From the back cover: “I’m Deckard. Blade Runner. Two sixty-three fifty-four. Filed and monitored.” This Asset Pack for the multiple award-winning Blade Runner: The Roleplaying Game includes a weath of resources and carefully crafted handouts to enhance your game. Using these tools, you can easily create your own Case Files for your players — or play the game solo! Contents: 3 Independent Sentinel front pages, stuffed with news and leads from 2037 Los Angeles. 20 detailed action maps, covering a wide variety of Blade Runner locations, ranging from generic to iconic. 18 cardboard character standees for use in the action maps. 28 mugshot cards of various denizens of future noir LA, including famous characters pulled from the silver screen like Mariette and Gaff. 10 detailed data sheets of key individuals. 8 crime scene photos, all with hidden clues for players to discover. 2 Esper surveillance photos. A handy Heat Meter showing just how hot the streets are right now. 24-page solo mode booklet — using the tools and guidelines in it, you can walk the mean streets of 2037 alone, without the need of a Game Runner.

title: Skyfall War
description: From publisher blurb: The sky has no mercy here—only memory. Above the raw firmament, where gravity still argues with intention and the upper atmosphere peels like wounded metal, engines scream their ownership into the void. Vapor trails cross and recross like duelist’s scars, each one left by a pilot who believes the sky itself can be broken and claimed. Below, the land waits in silence, untouched by banners yet already stained by ambition. This is not a war of nations—not yet. It is a proving ground. A convergence of mercenary wings, corporate-backed aces, exiles with nothing left but a cockpit and a call-sign whispered in fear. They come not for territory alone, but for dominion of air and orbit, for the right to decide who may descend and who will burn before touching soil. Satellites die young here. Sensor ghosts drift through the upper dark, remnants of skirmishes no one admits to starting. Automated defense grids flicker awake and sleep again, confused by the speed and savagery of human instinct. Every landing is a gamble. Every ascent an invitation to be hunted. Pilots learn quickly that the sky is layered with lies. What looks empty may be watched. What looks safe may be bait. Wings fold. Wings snap. Sometimes a burning craft falls like a dying star, and sometimes it vanishes cleanly, as if swallowed by the heavens themselves. Among the pilots, legends are already forming. Not of heroes—but of survivors. Those who fly wounded craft through firestorms. Those who cut engines to drift unseen. Those who kill without locking on, guided only by instinct and rage. They trade favors, betray contracts, and sell kills as currency. The sky remembers all of it. Control of the air means control of everything that follows. Supply. Reinforcement. Escape. Whoever owns the skies decides the future written on the ground below. And so the engines ignite again. The war for the sky has begun.

title: Crypt of Death
description: From publisher blurb: Kidnapped by a black-hearted band of evil adventurers, and with little more than a torch, a ten-foot pole and their wits, a gaggle of peasants must detect and defeat (or avoid) the tricks, traps and guardians protecting the gloom-drenched tomb-vault of the loathsome priest Vilho Antero. With the screams of the first group herded into the crypt yet ringing in their ears, and their captors’ swords at their backs, the characters have no choice but to dare the Crypt of Death! Crypt of Death is a Shadowdark Gauntlet adventure for four to six 0-level characters set in the Duchy of Ashlar but easily adapted to virtually any GM’s campaign.

title: Titan of the Verdant Maw
description: From the back cover: Somewhere beyond the edge of maps sprawls the Verdant Maw. This warped demi-plane of carnivorous flora, mad science, and ancient ruins is now stalked by the Mutagenic Behemoth: a biomechanical nightmare that warps everything it touches. Adventurers prepare, cults awaken, and the jungle itself hungers. Track the monster, outwit rivals, and survive traps and mutant horrors.

title: Cohors Cthulhu Laurium Adventures
description: From publisher blurb: Laurium, Gateway to the Hidden Wars One remote town is uniquely situated on the precarious divide between the controlled holdings of the Roman Empire and the wild lands of Germania: the frontier town of Laurium. Far from the Eternal City, though governed by a magistrate who speaks with the authority of Roman law and protected by the soldiers of the I Minerva Legion, Laurium is more exposed and vulnerable than most of its residents believe. The town’s sizable population of Germanic natives includes many who resent Rome’s occupation, some of whom meet in secret to foment rebellion. But this simmering turmoil is only one of the more obvious problems here. Other hidden forces—far more sinister—threaten all who live in Laurium, be they Roman, German, or from other cultures that have come to this place from afar. The lands near Laurium hide many secrets, including a rising swell of Mythos forces eager to corrupt this town. Dark cults lurk both within and outside its palisade walls, serving forgotten and malevolent gods whose goals imperil all of humanity. The few mortal groups knowledgeable enough to oppose such enemies also hide in Laurium, pretending to live ordinary lives while secretly locked in the Hidden Wars. This 152-page supplement includes a wealth of adventure opportunities and information for Cohors Cthulhu gamemasters running campaigns centering on the Roman-occupied town of Laurium. This PDF includes detailed information and profiles on many of the most significant NPCs living in and near Laurium, an Otium rules system for handling downtime activity between major adventures, and seven self-contained adventures either set within or near the town of Laurium: “Vigilia,” written by Harrison J. Chadd and Dave Semark, features an investigation into a child-abduction ring that reveals a greater plot to empower the Outer God, Mormo. “The Silent Mine,” written by Tyler Omichinski, is a story where a newly built silver and gold mine conceals the machinations of Mi-Go north of the town. “Fabled Herbs,” written by Jeff Provine, sees a deceptive sect of cultists attempt to corrupt Laurium by enthralling its leaders through the use of an insidious herb. “The Amber God,” written by Cat Evans, is set in a prosperous amber mine which brings considerable wealth to Laurium, but where a powerful Mythos entity has long been entombed. “Artifact From the East,” written by Mari Tokuda, features the visitation to Laurium by mystics from lands far to the east of Rome, who intend to use an Atlantean artifact to advance the goals of the Outer Gods. “Under a Menacing Moon,” written by Simon Kaye and Selena Gray, follows the disappearance of a Roman centurion that reveals a sinister Mythos creature that menaces nearby villages. “Find the Witch,” written by Paul L. Mathews, where a clash between mystics in Laurium reveals the threat of a Deep Ones invasion of the town—one that imperils all who live there.

title: Ships of the Shattered Empire
description: From publisher blurb: The Fleets of the Great Civilizations Muster for Battle! Three thousand years ago, the Twilight Wars tore the galaxy apart. The fleets of the great powers ravaged the Lazax Empire, shattering civilization even as they destroyed themselves. Now, as those powers climb out of the ashes and prepare to reclaim their title as “Great Civilizations,” armadas of warships once again gather in the sky above their homeworlds. Ships of the Shattered Empire is a comprehensive sourcebook for starships and vehicles in the Twilight Imperium universe. From new rules for space combat and massed battles to over a hundred new profiles for starships and vehicles, this book gives you everything you need to experience space warfare like never before. This supplement for the Genesys Roleplaying Game includes: - A simple but inclusive system for commanding major military forces and leading them in major combats. - New talents for player characters focusing on starship and vehicle operations and fighting and winning massed combats. - GM guidance on how to organize starship encounters, operate crews of NPCs, and allowing your players to command massive battleships without being bogged down in minutia. - Extensive information on the militaries of eleven of the most powerful civilizations of the Twilight Imperium setting. - Over a hundred new starship and vehicle profiles, many of which are perfect for player characters to operate, along with adventure seeds for working those vehicles into your campaign.

title: Flowing Tide
description: From publisher blurb: Warriors of the Flowing Tide study the resilience of water, believing a river does not conquer stone through force alone. It surrounds, reshapes, and sustains. These monks learn techniques to act as a living current, restoring allies and guiding the rhythm of battle with steady motion. Where others rely on relentless strikes, disciples of the Flowing Tide move with purpose, weaving through conflict to keep their companions standing. In battle, they are never still. Warriors of the Flowing Tide surge forward like crashing waves, carrying allies out of danger, and scattering enemies who stand in their path. Through discipline and balance, they become the tide itself, steady, relentless, and renewed.

title: Strange Tales of New England
description: From publisher blurb: Welcome to New England.... Man has long been subject to demonic possession and ghostly hauntings. New England history is plagued with such strangeness, from the Salem Witch Trials to the ghosts of Wood Island Lighthouse. Here something wicked permeates the soil and rolls like fog through the twisted landscape. You are invited to New England to face demons, vampires, satanic witches and other supernatural minions of the darkness. Each of you will answer a different calling. Some cast out demons using an exorcist’s prayer, others fight evil spirits with psychic powers. And of course, some take up arms or use science and deception to win the day. All will discover, things are not as they seem in these strange lands. Strange Tales of New England is a horror RPG with a focus on monster-of-the-week investigations into local anomalies. It uses the core system found in Strange Tales of Songling and is a blend of classic and surreal horror. The game includes: Five character paths: Spirit Medium, Exorcist, Expert, Combatant and Charlatan A simple core rules system designed with rulings in mind Prayers and magic A Gazetteer of Strange New England that includes 33 potential adventure sites, 10 Cults and 10 Boogie Men Two sample adventures and eight adventure seeds

title: Mistborn World Guide
description: UNRELEASED GAME This game has not been released yet, therefore some of the information above may change in the future. Until this game is released, please don't submit images for it unless given direct written permission from the publisher which must be noted in the image caption. From the kickstarter: Step into the World of Scadrial with this beautifully illustrated book. This guide to the history, cultures and key figures of Mistborn is a must have whether you're new to the setting or a longtime reader eager for new canonical details.

title: It's World Snake Day, Everyone!
description: From publisher blurb: Save Our Snakes! When the heroes are invited guests of honor for World Snake Day, promoting awareness of our ophidian friends, they're in for a surprise from the Scalemaster, who doesn't think anyone's love for snakes can be as sincere as his! But Scalemaster's takeover of the vivarium is just the first step in a sinister plan to exploit fear of snakes and to turn World Snake Day into a terror such as the city has never known! As a supervillain aims to use people’s ingrained ophidiophobia to their advantage, can the heroes prove themselves friends to the serpentine and save World Snake Day from disaster?

title: Cold Oaths
description: From publisher blurb: Far north of the settled bronze roads lies Eldholt, a land of iron frost and starless winters. Here, the wind scours memory from stone, and the dead do not always stay buried. Black pines claw at a white sky. Frozen rivers whisper in forgotten tongues. In Eldholt, survival is a currency, and loyalty is bought in blood and warmth. A mercenary brotherhood—hardened by war, betrayal, and hunger—has taken contract in these frozen reaches. Their task is simple in word, impossible in truth: discover what stirs beyond the northern ice. Caravans vanish. Watchfires go dark. Villages are found split open, as if by titanic claws or ritual knives. Something ancient has begun to move again. Steel is cold. Magic is colder. And the north remembers what the south has forgotten. You stand at the edge of Eldholt, breath frosting your beard and lashes. The snow crunches beneath your boots like broken bone. Behind you, the mercenary banners snap in the wind—ragged, oil-stained, stitched with old victories. Ahead lies a land that does not care if you live. A horn sounds somewhere distant—too deep for any beast you know. The men look to you, waiting. Command is a weight heavier than armor. You grip your weapon, feel its honesty in your hand, and step forward into the cold. The north has issued its challenge.
